## Phase 6.45 — Security audit follow-up (2026-05-03)

External security review identified ~80 findings (CRIT/HIGH/MED/LOW)
across all crates.  Pre-verification против current code revealed
several were stale or already fixed in prior phases; the table below
tracks **verified** findings and their resolution status.

### Verified findings + status

| Audit ID | Severity | File:line | Reality check | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **C1** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-bootstrap/src/signed_invite.rs:347-351`](crates/veil-bootstrap/src/signed_invite.rs) | `try_into().unwrap()` chain — provably-infallible (read enforces N bytes) but ugly hygiene | ✅ Phase 6.45 — replaced с `read_u16_be`/`read_u64_be` helpers + defensive `checked_add` overflow |
| **C2** | CRIT | [`veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs:4489`](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs), [`veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs:1808`](veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs) | Verified: HashMap'ы без cap.  `live_sessions` partially bounded via `max_per_ip`/`max_per_subnet`/`max_concurrent` (Epic 322), но `peer_sovereign_identities` + `per_session_mlkem_dk` unbounded | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — bounded-insert helper at оба insert site, capped by `MAX_PEER_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITIES = 4_096` + `MAX_PER_SESSION_MLKEM_DK = 4_096` |
| **C3** | CRIT | `veilcore/src/node/admin.rs:23-30` | **ALREADY FIXED** — `ADMIN_TOKEN_FILENAME` (admin.token sidecar) для TCP, file mode 0600 для Unix-socket. Audit stale | ⊘ done (pre-existing) |
| **C4** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs:173-181`](crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs) | **PARTIALLY FIXED** — `total_size > MAX_REASSEMBLY_BYTES` check IS before `insert`.  Without per-entry cap `transfers` HashMap could be filled with many small manifests below the byte cap | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — `MAX_TRANSFERS_CONCURRENT = 256` cap, idempotent retransmits bypass it |
| **C5** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-proxy/src/veil_connector.rs:79-127`](crates/veil-proxy/src/veil_connector.rs) | Verified: 512 SOCKS5 × 256 KiB duplex = 128 MB baseline, slow-readers hold buffers | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — `MAX_PROXY_BRIDGES = 256` (`Arc<AtomicUsize>` counter + RAII `BridgeSlot::try_acquire`); `PROXY_DUPLEX_BUF_SIZE` reduced 256 KiB → 64 KiB.  Worst-case proxy memory: 256 × 64 KiB = 16 MiB (vs legacy 128 MiB).  Cap exceeded → `Socks5Error::ConnectFailed`; +2 unit tests |
| **C6** | CRIT | `crates/veil-mesh/src/forwarder.rs:144-147` | **ALREADY FIXED** — `BroadcastSeenSet::check_and_insert` (4096 entries, 10s TTL) at line 160; spoof-check + dedup both present.  Audit stale | ⊘ done (pre-existing) |
| **C7** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs:269-291`](crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs) | Verified: `cipher.decrypt(...)` returns `Vec<u8>` with master-seed bytes — sat в plain heap until manual zeroing loop.  Window of microseconds но real | ✅ Phase 6.45 — wrapped decrypt result в `Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>` immediately; auto-wipe on every exit path |
| **C8** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs:1761`](crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs) | `AppIpcSendPayload::decode` already bounded by frame body (≤ MAX_FRAME_BODY = 16 MiB), но е2е encrypt branch не имел explicit early-reject — bypass via direct IPC over the FFI cap was possible | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — explicit `MAX_APP_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 16 MiB` cap before E2E encrypt; new `ipc_send_err::PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE = 6` error code returned to client |
| **H1** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/resolver.rs:684` | `panic!("claim pow mining failed")` on attacker-influenced PoW | ⊘ test-only — `mine_claim_pow` сидит в `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`.  Reclassified as audit false-positive (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H2** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/lib.rs:85` | `unreachable!()` в test_support, но adjacent к prod | ⊘ test-only — `pub(crate) mod test_support` под `#[cfg(test)]`.  Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H3** | HIGH | `crates/veil-anonymity/src/circuit.rs:216-257`, `packet.rs:197-236`, `sender.rs:226-274` | Multiple `panic!()` on assertions при walk circuit (visible через wire) | ⊘ test-only — все panic!()'ы в `#[test]` блоках. Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-2) |
| **H4** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/verify.rs:154-155` | `device_id` compared via `PartialEq` (non-constant-time) | ⊘ false positive — `device_id = BLAKE3(pubkey)`, оба значения public; нет секрета для timing-leak.  Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H5** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/sovereign.rs:157-162`, `publish.rs:30-33` | "Ed25519-only guard for now" — verify accepts Falcon-512, produce — нет | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — это **по дизайну**: runtime-hot signing требует Ed25519 ~50µs vs Falcon ~5ms на бюджетных Android.  Falcon-512 в cert-chain (signs ~yearly).  Доком зафиксировано как invariant, не TODO |
| **H6** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs:226`](crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs) | `Vec<[u8;64]>` per relay без cap on `n_relays` (DHT-sourced); each cert triggers ML-KEM-768 encapsulate ≈ 1.1 KiB ciphertext + AEAD work | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — `MAX_FANOUT_CERTS = 16` (mirrors `MAX_INSTANCES`); new `TooManyCerts` error |
| **H7** | HIGH | [`crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs:357-363`](crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs) — `veil_send` | `ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(..., 32)` без bounds-check; `slice::from_raw_parts(data, len)` без cap | ✅ Phase 6.45 — added `VEIL_MAX_DATA_LEN = 16 MiB` cap; documented 32-byte SAFETY contract for `dst_node_id`/`dst_app_id` |
| **H8** | HIGH | [`crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs:444-451, 1056`](crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs) | Callback called from tokio worker без `catch_unwind` → panic crosses FFI = UB | ✅ Phase 6.45 — wrapped recv + event handlers с `catch_unwind`; logged + drop frame, channel kept open |
| **H9** | HIGH | `crates/veil-update/src/fetch.rs:45` | `MAX_BINARY_BYTES = 100 MiB` checked AFTER download | ⬜ todo — stream-fetcher с early reject |
| **H10** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-update/src/manifest.rs`](crates/veil-update/src/manifest.rs) | Нет clock-skew tolerance на `release_unix`; нет `signature_issued_unix` | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — `MAX_MANIFEST_FUTURE_SKEW_SECS = 86400` + `MAX_MANIFEST_AGE_SECS = 90 days`; новые `FutureSkew`/`Stale` errors; `verify_manifest()` принимает `now_unix_secs: Option<u64>`; checker.rs автоматически передаёт wall-clock из `SystemTime::now()`; +4 теста |
| **H11** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs:76,103`](crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs) | Только глобальный cap, нет per-peer — single misbehaving peer мог занять все слоты в `MAX_PENDING_ACK_ENTRIES` budget и заглушить retransmit для остальных | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — `MAX_PENDING_ACK_PER_PEER = 16` + per-peer counter (HashMap incremented on register, decremented on ack/failure); 2 unit tests added |
| **H12** | HIGH | `crates/veil-dispatcher-state/src/lib.rs:70` — `CaptureEvent::body: Vec<u8>` | При active debug-capture весь frame клонируется + broadcast → 10 MB/s @ 10K pkt/s | ⬜ todo — truncate body к 256 B + per-peer rate 100/s |
| **H13** | HIGH | `veilcore/src/node/runtime/lifecycle.rs:106-157` | 13× `tokio::task::spawn_blocking(...).await.unwrap_or_else(log)` swallows panics | ⊘ already-handled — `unwrap_or_else(\|e\| log::error!(...))` НЕ swallows: `e` это `JoinError`, panic surfaced через `log::error!`.  Shutdown best-effort; propagate Result не нужен. Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H14** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs:308-330`](crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs) | Eclipse: 1/s per-bucket rate насыщается за 4-5 min с 256 IP | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — token-bucket (`DHT_BUCKET_TOKENS_MAX = 5`, refill `1.0 t/s`) + exp backoff (`1s → 2s → 4s → … → 60s`); добавил `MAX_NODES_PER_AS16_PER_BUCKET = 10` (/16 IPv4 / /32 IPv6 cap дополняет `/24` cap) |
| **H15** | HIGH | [`veilcore/src/node/runtime/{maintenance,lifecycle}.rs`](veilcore/src/node/runtime/maintenance.rs) | `observed_peer_count` from connected peers (Sybil-influenceable) | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — `min_responder_prefix_bits` теперь питается ТОЛЬКО `sessions` (post-handshake), не `rt_size.max(sessions)`.  DHT-table Sybil-влияемая, sessions — нет |
| **H16** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs:428`](crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs) — `all_contacts()` | Clones whole routing-table под `lock!()` | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — добавлены `node_ids() -> Vec<[u8;32]>` (32 B/entry vs ~200 B) и `for_each_contact<F>` без аллокаций.  Diversity-XOR call-site в kademlia.rs:1102 переключён на `node_ids()` — ~6× меньше памяти и lock-hold |
| **H17** | HIGH | `crates/veil-bootstrap/src/signed_invite.rs:347` | `pk_len = u16::from_be_bytes(...) as usize` без cap | ✅ Phase 6.45 — combined fix с C1; helpers + overflow check |
| **H18** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-routing/src/discovery_forwarder.rs:94`](crates/veil-routing/src/discovery_forwarder.rs) | `.unwrap_or([1u8;8])` (slice→array) в constructor — hidden panic-fallback | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — заменил на прямую индексацию массива `local_id[0..8]`, типобезопасно (`local_id: [u8; 32]` всегда ≥8 байт) |
| **H19** | HIGH | [`veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/control.rs:398-414`](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/control.rs) | Нет проверки session-identity отправителя `NAT_RELAY_REQUEST` — `peer_id ∈ {a, b}` слишком loose, `node_b` мог регистрировать туннель к произвольному `node_a` | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — строгий `peer_id == request.node_a`; добавил regression-test `relay_request_rejects_node_b_as_sender` |
| **H20** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-nat/src/puncher.rs:183`](crates/veil-nat/src/puncher.rs) | `peer_candidates.to_vec() + clone + sort` per попытка hole-punch — O(N²) | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — `MAX_HOLE_PUNCH_CANDIDATES = 10`; `truncate(10)` после sort-by-priority, защита от forced O(N²) на attacker-controlled lists |
| **H21** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs:1449`](crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs) — `handle_bind` | Нет per-peer rate-limit на decode-failures | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — `MAX_BIND_DECODE_FAILURES = 16` per IPC client; `IpcClientState.bind_decode_failures` counter, IPC-сессия закрывается по достижении cap |
| **H22** | HIGH | `crates/veil-local-transport/src/lib.rs` | 11× `.unwrap()` on FS/token I/O в hot paths | ⊘ test-only — все 11 unwrap'ов в `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (с строки 791).  Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H23** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-transport/src/tcp.rs:189`](crates/veil-transport/src/tcp.rs) | Нет SO_REUSEADDR; rapid restart → 30-60s TIME_WAIT lockout | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — `bind_tcp_with_reuseaddr()` через `socket2::Socket` с `set_reuse_address(true)` + dual-stack `set_only_v6(false)`; листенер обходит TIME_WAIT-окно |

### Phase 6.45 batch-2 shipped (2026-05-03)

* **C2** — bounded peer-state HashMaps: `peer_sovereign_identities` capped at `MAX_PEER_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITIES = 4_096`, `per_session_mlkem_dk` at `MAX_PER_SESSION_MLKEM_DK = 4_096`.  Random eviction (HashMap iter is non-deterministic) — acceptable since cache miss only forces full handshake / full ML-KEM rekey ([`veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs:4489`](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs), [`veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs:1808`](veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs)).
* **C4** — chunk-reassembler entry cap: `MAX_TRANSFERS_CONCURRENT = 256` ([`crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs:181`](crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs)).  Idempotent retransmits (same `transfer_id`) bypass the cap so they don't false-reject.
* **H6** — fan-out cert cap: `MAX_FANOUT_CERTS = 16` (matches wire-level `MAX_INSTANCES`); new `MlkemFanoutError::TooManyCerts` ([`crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs`](crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs)).
* **H11** — pending-ack per-peer cap: `MAX_PENDING_ACK_PER_PEER = 16`; `PendingAckTracker` now keeps a `per_peer: HashMap<NodeId, u32>` counter so a single peer cannot starve the global retransmit budget.  Added 2 unit tests covering cap enforcement and slot release on `ack` ([`crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs`](crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs)).
* **C8** — explicit IPC payload size cap: `MAX_APP_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 16 MiB` (mirrors FFI `VEIL_MAX_DATA_LEN`); rejects oversized `data` BEFORE E2E encryption with new `ipc_send_err::PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE = 6`.  Defense-in-depth against a malicious local app speaking IPC directly to bypass the FFI cap ([`crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs:1761`](crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs)).
* **C5** deferred к batch-3 — proxy global memory budget — architectural decision (per-bridge backpressure + global accounting touches `VeilConnector` orchestration).

### Phase 6.45 batch-1 shipped (2026-05-03)

* **H7** — FFI alloc-DoS via `veil_send`: hard cap `VEIL_MAX_DATA_LEN = 16 MiB`, documented SAFETY contract for the 32-byte `dst_node_id`/`dst_app_id` buffers ([`crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs`](crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs)).
* **H8** — FFI panic-across-C-ABI: `std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(...))` around recv-handler и event-handler callbacks; panic logs к stderr, frame/event dropped, channel/stream stays alive.  Closes а UB risk on every callback that touches user-supplied state.
* **C7** — master-seed timing window: `cipher.decrypt(...)` result wrapped в `Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>` immediately ([`crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs:269-291`](crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs)).  Auto-wipe on every exit path.
* **C1 + H17** — bootstrap-decode hygiene: replaced `try_into().unwrap()` cluster в `signed_invite.rs` + `signed_bundle.rs` с explicit `read_u16_be`/`read_u32_be`/`read_u64_be` helpers; added defensive `checked_add` overflow check on `pos + n` (matters on 32-bit Android targets).

365 tests passing across the three audited crates (88 veilclient-ffi-related + 261 veil-identity + 16 veil-bootstrap).  Cluster healthy после deploy (planned in batch-1 commit).

### Batch ordering (remaining)

1. ~~**batch-2**: C2 + C4 + C8 + H6 + H11~~ — shipped 2026-05-03.  H3 (anonymity panics) deferred to batch-3 — verified all listed `panic!()` sites are inside `#[test]` blocks; reclassified.
2. **batch-3** (next): C5 + H1, H2, H4, H5, H10, H13, H14, H15, H16, H18, H19, H20, H21, H22, H23 — DHT eclipse hardening + remaining panic/unwrap cleanups + transport polish + proxy memory budget.
3. **batch-4**: MED + LOW + dead-code removal + duplicate-logic consolidation (audit §3 + §4) — ~1500 LOC removed.


---

## Phase 6.48 — Post-6.47 follow-up audit (closeout 2026-05-06)

После closeout Phase 6.47 запустили дополнительный 4-агентный security-аудит workspace.  Phase 6.45/6.47 закрыли крипто и сессии; этот скан нашёл **0 CRIT, 8 HIGH, 12 MED, 5 LOW + 4 дубликата + 10 attack-models** на boundaries / TOCTOU / unbounded state / panic-paths.

**Closure summary (2026-05-05 → 2026-05-06, 6 commits `44c45e8…e707113`):** all 8 HIGH + 7 panic + 6 memory + 8 attack-models + 5 D-polish + 1 of 5 R-refactor verified или fixed.  4 R-refactor + 2 X-architectural items remain в open backlog (cleanup, no security-relevant action required).  2 attack-models deferred к future epic (large redesign).

| Group | Total | ✅ Fixed | ❌ False positive | ⏸ Deferred |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| HIGH (H1-H8) | 8 | 5 (H1, H2, H4, H7, H8) | 3 (H3, H5, H6) | 0 |
| Panic (P1-P7) | 7 | 3 (P1, P6, P7) | 4 (P2, P3, P4, P5 — provably-infallible) | 0 |
| Memory (M1-M6) | 6 | 4 (M1, M2, M5, M6) | 2 (M3, M4 — already capped) | 0 |
| Attack-models (A1-A8) | 8 | 5 (A2, A3, A4, A5, A7) | 1 (A6) | 2 (A1 streaming-correlation + A8 intersection — see deferred backlog) |
| D-polish (D1-D5) | 5 | 4 (D1+A5 merged, D2+A7 merged, D4, D5) | 1 (D3) | 0 |
| Refactor (R1-R5) | 5 | 1 (R5 YAGNI delete) | 0 | 4 (R1, R2, R3, R4 — open backlog below) |
| Architectural (X1-X3) | 3 | 1 (X3 BoundedMap shipped в batch-2) | 0 | 2 (X1, X2 — open backlog below) |

**Closeout commits:**
- `44c45e8` batch-1 — HIGH + panic-hardening
- `e44448a` batch-2 — memory caps + perf hotpaths
- `3a8251a` batch-3 — attack-model coverage (A3-A7)
- `6a1588b` batch-4 — D-polish (D3 verified, D4 + D5 fixed) + A2 primitive
- `7cfc452` batch-5 — R5 YAGNI cleanup
- `e707113` Phase 6.45 H12 + A2 caller-wiring (final closeout)

**Open backlog (no security action — pure cleanup; не блокирует GA):**

| ID | Цель | Объём | Re-open trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| 6.48-R1 | Унифицировать HMAC-key-management в `veil-util::keyed_hash::HmacFile` | ~100 LoC dedup | **Still open** — 8 consumers exist (veil-update, veil-bootstrap × 2, veil-proto × 2, veilcore × 3) but each has bespoke key derivation around а core blake3-keyed-hash; consolidation would touch 8 unrelated callers с no shared invariant gained.  Re-open trigger: when adding а 9th consumer OR when а keyrotation bug is observed across 2+ existing consumers. |
| 6.48-R2 | Единый `RateLimiter` trait в `veil-util` | ~200 LoC dedup | **Still open** — 20 callers found; each has bespoke per-purpose rate-limit shape (token-bucket / sliding-window / leaky-bucket) с different metric exposure.  Consolidating into а single trait would force one shape on all sites, costing > saved LoC.  Re-open trigger: when 2+ callers want the SAME shape с the same metric exposure (i.e. genuine duplication, not just "all rate-limiting some way"). |
| 6.48-R3 | `BoundedDecoder` — replace ~50 hand-rolled decoders в `veil-proto/*` | ~−500 LoC | **Phase 6.49: 1 demo migration shipped (`mlkem_cert::decode`).** Primitive shipped в [`crates/veil-proto/src/cursor.rs`](crates/veil-proto/src/cursor.rs) (Phase 6.48); first migration shipped в Phase 6.49 (mlkem_cert.rs: ~25 lines deleted, equivalent surface preserved, 14 unit tests pass).  **Open backlog:** ~9 hand-rolled decoders remain в veil-proto (identity_proof, instance_registry, name_claim_v2, pairing_invite, prekey_bundle, identity_document, recipient, plus а few smaller ones).  Migration is mechanical но per-decoder due к each having custom error messages + custom validation rules.  Re-open trigger: when touching а decoder for а wire-format change OR when а new protocol family is added. |
| 6.48-R4 | `Ttl(Duration)` newtype across `*_TTL_SECS` constants | ~50 LoC | **Phase 6.49: primitive shipped + 1 demo migration.** Newtype lives в [`crates/veil-util/src/lib.rs`](crates/veil-util/src/lib.rs) (`Ttl::from_secs` / `from_millis` / `from_duration` / `as_duration` / `as_secs` / display formatting / saturating_add / checked_sub).  9 unit tests pass.  First migration shipped: `BROADCAST_SEEN_TTL_SECS: u64` → `BROADCAST_SEEN_TTL: Ttl` в [`crates/veil-mesh/src/forwarder.rs`](crates/veil-mesh/src/forwarder.rs) — eliminates two redundant `Duration::from_secs(BROADCAST_SEEN_TTL_SECS)` constructions at the consumer.  **Open backlog:** ~58 other `*_TTL_SECS` constants across 15 crates can opportunistically migrate when touched.  Re-open trigger: when adding а new TTL constant (use Ttl) OR when а seconds-vs-millis bug is observed. |
| 6.48-X1 | Newtype boundary-validation — `PeerReportedRtt(u32)`, `PeerReportedScore(u8)` со встроенным clamp | ~150 LoC | **Phase 6.49: PeerReportedRtt shipped + migrated.** Newtype lives в [`crates/veil-routing/src/probe.rs`](crates/veil-routing/src/probe.rs) (`PeerReportedRtt::from_raw_ms` clamps к `[RTT_FLOOR_MS, RTT_CEIL_MS]` at construction; `as_clamped_ms` to extract).  `RttTable::record` now requires `PeerReportedRtt` parameter — type system physically refuses raw `u32`, so а new wire-frame variant added later cannot bypass the clamp.  Audit-H26 fix moved out of `record()` body and into the type boundary (constructor).  All call sites updated (veilcore/control.rs, mesh_gateway tests, score tests, probe tests).  **`PeerReportedScore` skipped:** no peer-reported score field exists в the codebase — scores are computed locally в `NeighborScorer` from peer-reported RTT + locally-observed reachability.  Re-open trigger: when peers gain а protocol surface to advertise their own score (currently не on roadmap). |
| 6.48-X2 | Sweep `.expect()` outside tests → `?` propagation | ~50 sites | **Closure-rationalized Phase 6.49.** Audit-followup confirmed remaining `.expect()` sites fall into 3 justified categories: (1) test-only (legitimate); (2) provably-infallible-by-construction с adjacent SAFETY comment proving prior validation makes the call infallible (e.g. `try_into::<[u8;2]>` после `.get(..2)` succeeded); (3) lock-poisoning (where panic IS correct recovery — global state corrupted, restart restores invariants).  Phases 6.45 + 6.47 + 6.48 batch cleanups already removed the actionable subset.  Re-open trigger: production panic surfaces в logs OR а new `.expect()` is added on operator-supplied data without prior validation. |

**Deferred к future epic (large redesigns, ≫ 500 LoC each):**

| ID | Class | Why deferred |
|---|---|---|
| 6.48-A1 | Streaming traffic correlation (cover-traffic + chunk-equalization Loopix-style) | **Still deferred Phase 6.49.** ~600 LoC + traffic-pattern analysis; needs design pass.  Concrete blockers: (1) cover-traffic cadence policy must be measured against real adversary capabilities (constant-rate vs Poisson-modulated vs application-aware) — design choice depends on threat model that isn't currently quantified; (2) chunk-equalization requires а protocol layer above the existing padding (Epic 409.7 is per-frame; Loopix is per-flow); (3) bandwidth budget for cover-traffic conflicts с Epic 483.6 cellular caps — needs operator-policy framework to reconcile.  Re-open trigger: published attack paper showing the existing Epic 482 anonymity layer is correlation-vulnerable in а realistic deployment, OR when veil traffic patterns are observed deanonymizing users в production. |
| 6.48-A8 | Intersection attack (per-circuit hard-disjoint relay set + circuit lifetime cap) | **Still deferred Phase 6.49.** ~1500 LoC; couples к Epic 482 anonymity-layer redesign.  Specifically: needs (1) per-receiver relay-set memory (so two consecutive circuits to the same receiver land on disjoint relay sets) — currently relay choice is stateless; (2) circuit lifetime cap с graceful tear-down + re-establishment — currently circuits live until peer drop; (3) measurement of how often the existing per-round AS-prefix cap (Epic 485.1c) closes the same threat at lower cost.  Likely design conclusion: AS-prefix cap is sufficient for the realistic threat budget; full disjoint-set is over-engineering relative к the cellular-budget constraints (Epic 483.6).  Re-open trigger: external audit OR adversary-validation suite demonstrates AS-prefix cap insufficient against а concrete attack. |

Phase 6.45 H9 verified done в Phase 6.46 (Content-Length pre-check + streaming buffer cap shipped в `veil-bootstrap/src/https.rs::http_get_over_stream_with_timeout`); H12 shipped в `e707113` (CaptureEvent body truncation к 256 B + per-peer rate limit 100/s).  Phase 6.45 fully closed.

---

## Phase 6.49 — Multi-agent security/refactoring audit follow-up (closeout 2026-05-07)

После Phase 6.48 closeout запустили comprehensive audit — 4 параллельных агента покрыли zombie-code, runtime hazards (panic / memory / perf), duplicate functionality, attack-model gaps.  **61 finding total** (13 zombie + 20 runtime + 13 dup + 15 attack vectors).  Phase 6.49 closes ~30 of them; rest deferred с concrete re-open triggers.

**Closure summary (commits `6412ac1` + `<final>`):**

| Group | Total | ✅ Fixed | 🔄 Documented gap | ⏸ Deferred с trigger |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Zombie/stub (Z1-Z13) | 13 | 9 deleted, 2 gated к test, 1 docstring-fix | 1 (Z3 explicit back-compat) | 0 |
| Runtime hazards | 20 | 7 (H1-H7 + Z2/L2/L3 indirectly) | 0 | 13 (M-class + L1-L5 — sweep-when-touched) |
| Duplications | 13 | 1 (D6 read_array dedup, 7 sites) | 0 | 12 (D1-D5/D7-D13 — opportunistic) |
| Attack vectors | 15 | 0 closed-fully | 1 (AT8 documented gap с design dependency) | 14 (AT1/AT4 design-pass; AT2-AT7/AT9-AT15 follow-ups) |

**Closure commits Phase 6.49:**
- `6412ac1` Batches 1-4: deletes (Z1, Z2, Z4, Z5, Z6, Z7, Z9, Z11, Z13) + Z10/Z12 docstring fix + 32-bit overflow sweep H1-H6 (7 decoder sites) + H7 Argon2 OOM cap + AT8 honest-gap-doc

**Open backlog с re-open triggers:**

| ID | Class | Re-open trigger |
|----|-------|----------------|
| **AT1** anonymity-relay key rotation | CRIT — Tor NTor parity | Audit OR observed retroactive-decrypt incident; needs design pass coupling rotation cadence к forward-secrecy SLO |
| **AT4** m-of-n update issuer key | CRIT — anti-censorship | Single-key kill-switch unacceptable для production; design choice (m-of-n threshold sig vs separate revoke key vs Sigstore-style transparency); needs operator-trust model |
| **AT2** PEX BLAKE3-binding check | HIGH | Persisted PEX peers can lie about their pubkey; trivial fix (~30 LoC + tests) — ship next batch |
| **AT5** authenticated time source | MED — foundational | Network MITM intercepts NTP → freshness windows fall apart; design: signed-time gossip OR roughtime client (no live consumer yet) |
| **AT6** Falcon-blob upper bound | MED | `split_hybrid_sig` reads u16 fal_len без upper bound → CPU amplification; trivial — `MAX_FALCON_SIG_BYTES` const + check |
| **AT7** identity equivocation alarm | MED | Master signs 2 distinct valid IdentityDocs к disjoint replicas; needs gossip-of-equivocation-proof primitive |
| **AT9** Vivaldi coord range-clamp | MED | Peer publishes `{x:0,y:0,h:0,err:0}` → wins all latency selections; trivial — ingest-time clamp |
| **AT10** guard-relay rotation | MED — Tor pattern | Without persistent first-hop, `1-(1-f)^k` deanonymization over circuits; Epic 482 follow-up |
| **AT11** Falcon RNG injection | MED — mobile | Don't depend on libc RNG at boot; inject `OsRng` via pqcrypto-falcon API |
| **AT12** introduce-replay LRU eviction | MED | Current `g.keys().next()` non-deterministic; switch к LRU |
| **AT13** binary_sha256 pinning across mirrors | MED | Update mirrors should agree on hash; needs transparency log OR multi-mirror cross-check |
| **AT14** Falcon-sig DPI fingerprint | HIGH — fundamental | Variable 600-752 B sig blob внутри TLS-on-443 — undermines Epic 480 DPI evasion claim; design dependency on padding scheme |
| **AT15** onion-key TOFU pinning | MED | Sender re-resolves picks newest без continuity check; needs sender-side x25519_pk pinning per relay |
| **AT3** mobile-status side-channel | MED | Local sandboxed app reads battery/tier via IPC token; needs OS-level confinement |
| **M5** session/runner deferred Vec alloc | MED — perf | Pre-allocates 16 MiB per inbound frame before body arrives; needs measurement to know если worth complexity |
| **M6** IPC concurrent client cap | MED | No global cap; needs `MAX_IPC_CONCURRENT_CLIENTS` semaphore at accept |
| **M7** dht/iterative.rs HashSet rebuild | MED — perf | Per-round rebuild O(rounds × k); incremental insert ~10 LoC change |
| **M8** TieredStore::iter clones-under-write-lock | MED — perf | Streams hundreds MB; rewrite to callback-iter |
| **M1-M4** debug-build panic on cursor wraparound | MED | Same `checked_add` pattern as H1-H6; mechanical sweep when next touched |
| **L1** maintenance.rs `.unwrap()` × 3 | LOW | Internal-config decode panic on corrupt reload — `if let` defensive |
| **L4-L5** saturating_mul for capacity calc | LOW | Defensive; no current overflow risk |
| **D1** now_unix_secs (~80 sites) | LOW — chore | Single canonical clock; 1-day sweep when veil-util touched |
| **D2/D12** bytes_hex / hex-loop reimplementations | LOW — chore | 10 sites; mechanical replace by `veil_util::bytes_to_hex` |
| **D3** wire-decode prelude (6 crates) | MED | Common `WireHeader<E>` trait would help но requires generic-error mapping |
| **D4/D5** `read<'a>` / `read_u*_be` cursor helpers | LOW | Phase 6.48 R3 BoundedDecoder migration will absorb when each site is touched |
| **D7-D13** small per-pair dedups | LOW — opportunistic | Each ~10 LoC sweep when adjacent code is touched |
| **D11** Howard Hinnant date formatter | MED | Two divergent copies (date vs date+time с different `if z>=0` vs `div_euclid`); subtle bug source — fix when next touched |
| **Z3** CLI `--pow-difficulty` | EXPLICIT BACK-COMPAT | Drop at next major CLI version bump |
| **Z7** PendingAckTracker FETCHED-stage path | INVESTIGATION | Verify Epic 238.7 claim; if path uses `ack_and_get_info` instead — already done |
| **Z8 should_switch** | LOW | Possible premature-abstraction; investigate failover.rs path; delete если truly unused |

Concrete numbers: ~280 LoC dead code deleted, 7 32-bit overflow defences shipped, 1 Argon2-OOM cap shipped, 1 dedup сweep (7 read_array copies), 565+ tests pass на veil-proto, 1085 tests pass workspace-wide на затронутых крейтах.

> removed); S4 skipped с rationale (ML-KEM production-critical); **S3
> absorbed by Epic 477** — deterministic `device_id = BLAKE3(pubkey)`
> уже в `IdentityKey.device_id: [u8; 32]` field, `bound_instance_id`
> dropped полностью.  Comments в `mlkem_fanout.rs:127`,
> `sovereign_flow.rs:1708`, `pair_runtime.rs:719` подтверждают.

|---|---|---|---|
| 476.S1a | Drop in-band revocation gossip cluster: `proto/revocation_gossip.rs` (625) + `node/identity/propagate.rs` (489) + `node/identity/watcher.rs` (550) + три integration-сценария. (commit `d720372`, −1808 LoC) | identity/* + proto/* | ✅ done |

Wire format меняется (deployable network нет). (commit `5cf3609`, −4013 LoC) | identity/* + proto/identity_document.rs + cfg/sovereign_flow.rs + cmd/cli.rs + cmd/sovereign_identity.rs + node/runtime/mod.rs + node/session/handshake.rs + sim/scenarios.rs + node/runtime/tests.rs | ✅ done |
| 476.S3 | Replace `bound_instance_id: [u8; 16]` random на `device_id: [u8; 32] = BLAKE3(identity_pubkey)` deterministic. | proto/identity_document.rs + identity/sovereign.rs + handshake.rs | ✅ done — absorbed by Epic 477 (см. archived 2026-04-XX): `IdentityKey.device_id: [u8; 32] = BLAKE3(pubkey)` уже в `veil-proto/identity_document.rs:103`; `bound_instance_id` field полностью удалён.  Verifier rejects cert mismatch via DeviceIdMismatch error. |
| 476.S4 | ~~ML-KEM gate за feature-flag~~ | identity/mlkem_fanout.rs + Cargo.toml | ⊘ skip — audit показал ML-KEM активно используется в production: `runtime/mod.rs::1016` startup MlKemKeyCert publish + `sovereign_republish.rs` periodic re-publish + `e2e.rs` per-session encapsulation для forward secrecy + `node/runtime/debug.rs` test fixtures. Gating сломал бы multi-device fanout encryption + per-session FS. PQ остаётся on by default; Epic 486 (proper PQ-readiness) переоценит выбор алгоритма позже без gating. |

Mailbox subsystem дропнут в Epic 473 (`mailbox_anchor` стал orphan random bytes), transport hints перенесены в `SignedTransportAnnouncement` gossip в Epic 475.4c (вышли из `InstanceRegistry` целиком). Zero production consumers. **Дропнуто:** `node/identity/tier_b.rs` (590 LoC), `InstanceEntry.encrypted_contact: Vec<u8>` поле + `mailbox_anchor: [u8; 32]` поле + `MAX_ENCRYPTED_CONTACT_BYTES` константа + `TierBError` umbrella variant + 3 теста (tier_b roundtrip, encrypted_contact cap, random mailbox_anchor) + dead `OsRng/RngCore` import in publish.rs. `InstanceEntry` wire layout сократился: было `[16+2+1+n+8+32+2+m]`, стало `[16+2+1+n+8]`. Net: ~−700 LoC. Build clean; identity + instance_registry tests 152/152 green. |

**S1 progress:** ~6500 LoC удалены через S1a + S1b+c+d + S5. Identity ядро: `verify_identity_document(doc, now)` — 6 шагов, без cache, без PoW, без freshness-cert; `InstanceEntry` без mailbox/encrypted_contact. S4 skipped (ML-KEM production-critical).

**Acceptance:** ~2500 LoC delete; identity ядро ≤ 5000 LoC (с CLI); zero-regression на существующих identity-тестах после migrations.

## Epic 462. Sovereign identity: master_seed + subkeys + stable `identity_id` (design locked 2026-04-20, implementation closed 2026-05-05)

> ⚠️ **Архивная пометка (2026-05-05):** часть acceptance-criteria ниже
> устарела после Epic 473 (mailbox subsystem removed) и Epic 476.S1
> (revocation-gossip + freshness-cert + RevocationCache removed).
> В частности устарели все criteria, упоминающие `mailbox*`,
> `master_freshness_sig`, `revocation_seq`, `RevocationCache`,
> "gossip revocations", "self-document anomaly watcher".

> ✅ **Закрытие 2026-05-05:** все non-stale acceptance-criteria
> верифицированы тестами:
>
> | Criterion | Где |
> |-----------|-----|
> | Onboarding end-to-end | `integration_tests::scenario_onboarding_and_verify` |
> | BIP-39 paper backup round-trip | `integration_tests::scenario_bip39_roundtrip_survives_device_wipe` |
> | Encrypted-file (Argon2id+ChaCha20) round-trip | `integration_tests::scenario_encrypted_file_roundtrip` |
> | Multi-device fan-out (per-instance E2E) | `integration_tests::scenario_multi_device_fanout_messenger` |
> | Safety number deterministic + pair-symmetric | `integration_tests::scenario_safety_numbers_pair_symmetric` |
> | Full pair ceremony end-to-end | `integration_tests::scenario_full_pair_ceremony_end_to_end` |
> | QR contact import + cert chain validate | `integration_tests::scenario_qr_contact_import_then_full_cert_chain_validate` |
> | App-state sync across own instances | `integration_tests::scenario_app_state_sync_across_own_instances` |
> | Chat backup → device wipe → restore | `integration_tests::scenario_chat_backup_restore_roundtrip` |
> | Name survives identity_sk rotation | `sovereign_flow::tests::rotate_preserves_node_id` |
> | Per-identity DHT write quota (10/h cap) | `dispatcher::tests::identity_write_quota_caps_rapid_republishes` (Epic 462.44 wire-up — commit `2e6bc01`) |
> | Multi-replica resolution с quorum (eclipse-resistance) | Epic 491 — `sim::scenarios::epic491_quorum_resolve_survives_close_sybil_forgery` |
> | 462.33 messenger dev guide | `docs/messenger-dev.md` (architecture-level walkthrough; working <200 LOC Python example deferred until Python FFI bindings) |

**Цель.** Разделить cryptographic identity от ежедневного signing key. Текущая модель `node_id = BLAKE3(pubkey)` делает identity нестабильной к ротации / compromise — теряется name, reputation, sessions, все контакты. Также блокирует multi-device use case (shared `sk` между устройствами = AEAD nonce reuse при ticket resumption + session dedup rejects). Новая модель: **`identity_id = BLAKE3(master_pk)`** forever stable, daily signing через rotated subkeys, master_sk в cold storage используется ~5×/год.

### Архитектура (резюме)

```
master_seed (32 B random, BIP39 24-word backup, cold storage — ТОЛЬКО на primary device)
     │ HKDF-SHA256
     ▼
 master_sk  ─sign certifications/revocations (редко, ~5-10×/год)─►
     │
 master_pk (Ed25519 default / Falcon-512 optional)
     │ BLAKE3("veil.identity.v1" || len(pk) || pk)
     ▼
 identity_id [u8; 32]  ← STABLE FOREVER (pre-image resistant binding)
     │
     ├── IdentityDocument (DHT record)
     │     ├── master_pubkey (plaintext, для verify identity_id)
     │     ├── identity_keys[] (per-instance subkeys, каждый master-certified)
     │     │    ├── { pubkey: pk_laptop, master_sig, bound_instance_id: 0xAA.. }
     │     │    ├── { pubkey: pk_phone,  master_sig, bound_instance_id: 0xBB.. }
     │     │    └── { pubkey: pk_tablet, master_sig, bound_instance_id: 0xCC.. }
     │     ├── revoked_keys[] (revocation per-instance, each signed by master)
     │     ├── master_freshness_sig (renewal раз в ≤30d)
     │     └── document_sig by ANY active identity_sk (любой instance может update)
     │
     ├── InstanceRegistry (separate DHT, multi-device/LB)
     │     └── signed by ANY active identity_sk
     │
     └── NameClaim(s) (separate DHT, per-name)
           └── signed by ANY active identity_sk, cert_proof embedded
```

**Ключевые инварианты** (finalized 2026-04-20 после revalidation):
- `identity_id` меняется **только** при потере master_seed (catastrophic, ≡ потере Bitcoin wallet seed)
- **Каждое устройство имеет свой identity_sk**, master-certified отдельно (NOT shared). Compromise одного device = revoke только его subkey, остальные продолжают работать
- `master_seed` живёт **только на primary device** (laptop) с paper/encrypted backup. Новые устройства получают **не master_seed**, а собственный identity_sk через pairing ceremony с master_sk certification
- `identity_id` неизменен; compromise любого device → revoke через master → reissue subkey для того device, identity_id/names/reputation сохраняются
- Multiple нод под одним `identity_id` — by-design (load balancing + multi-device messenger); session dedup по `(identity_id, instance_id)`
- Любой active identity_sk может update shared records (IdentityDocument non-key fields, InstanceRegistry, NameClaim) — consistency через `document_version` + `revocation_seq` monotonicity
- Свободная регистрация: любой с master keypair + ~1 сек timestamp-bound PoW получает новый `identity_id`
- Zero external trust anchors: ни DNS, ни SMS, ни guardian'ов

### Задачи

| # | Задача | Приоритет | LOC est. | Файлы | Статус |
|---|--------|-----------|----------|-------|--------|
| 462.0 | **Design doc**: `docs/identity-model.md` со всеми wire schemas, threat model, cryptographic contexts, migration notes, BIP39 семантикой, lifecycle диаграммами (этот эпик — reference) | **P0** | — (docs) | `docs/identity-model.md` | ✅ done |
| 462.1 | **Proto**: `proto/identity_document.rs` — struct `IdentityDocument`, `IdentityKey`, `RevocationEntry`, `MasterCertProof`. Wire layout с length-prefix (анти-collision между Ed25519/Falcon-512 pubkey sizes). Magic `"ID"` + version byte. **Freshness fields** (см. 462.36): `issued_at: u64`, `valid_until: u64` (≤ issued_at + MAX_DOC_LIFETIME = 7d), `revocation_seq: u64` monotonic. **Two sig slots**: `master_freshness_sig` (master_sk подписывает `("veil.freshness.v1" || id || revocation_seq || valid_until)` — cert of freshness, renewed раз в 30 дней owner'ом), `document_sig` (identity_sk подписывает non-freshness parts для daily updates). Canonical bytes для каждого sig — отдельно. Cap: 8 identity_keys, 32 revoked_keys, total ≤ 1.5 KB. `encode`/`decode` + golden-bytes tests. Domain contexts: `CERTIFY_CONTEXT = b"veil.certify.v1"`, `REVOKE_CONTEXT = b"veil.revoke.v1"`, `DOC_SIG_CONTEXT = b"veil.identity_doc.v1"`, `FRESHNESS_CONTEXT = b"veil.freshness.v1"` | **P0** | ~500 | `proto/identity_document.rs` (new), `proto/mod.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.2 | **Crypto helpers**: `crypto/identity.rs` — `derive_master_sk(master_seed, algo)` через HKDF-SHA256 с info `b"veil.master.v1"`; `compute_identity_id(master_pk)` length-prefixed BLAKE3; `certify_msg(subkey_pk, valid_from)` / `revoke_msg(pk_hash, revoked_at, reason)` builders с domain separation. Tests: roundtrip derivation determinism, binding uniqueness, cross-algo isolation (Ed25519 pk vs Falcon pk не коллидит через length-prefix) | **P0** | ~200 | `crypto/identity.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.3 | **Verifier**: `node/identity/verify.rs` — `verify_identity_document(doc, cache: &RevocationCache) -> Result<ValidatedIdentity>`. Steps: (1) magic+version, (2) `computed_id == doc.id`, (3) PoW nonce, (4) `now ≤ valid_until` (freshness), (5) `master_freshness_sig` verifies с master_pk, (6) each identity_key has valid master cert, (7) each revocation has valid master sig, (8) `sig_key_idx` в bounds + **не revoked в doc AND не revoked в local cache (462.36)**, (9) **`doc.revocation_seq ≥ cache.min_seq_seen(id)` — replay/stale reject**, (10) document_sig verifies с DOC_SIG_CONTEXT. Returns `ValidatedIdentity { id, active_identity_pubkey, master_pubkey, active_key_idx, observed_revocation_seq }`. Post-verify: `cache.record(id, doc.revocation_seq, doc.revoked_keys)` автоматически. 20+ negative tests (stale-by-freshness, stale-by-seq, missing master_freshness_sig, tampered cert, revoked-in-local-cache-but-not-doc, etc.) | **P0** | ~650 | `node/identity/verify.rs` (new), `node/identity/mod.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.4 | **Master storage — BIP39 paper backup**: `cfg/identity_master.rs` — BIP39 encode/decode (32 B seed ↔ 24 words, standard English wordlist); `load_master_sk_from_phrase_prompt()` CLI helper для secure-input (stdin hidden, no echo) + confirmation typed 3 random words. Zeroize-on-drop для all plaintext seed/key material. Tests: BIP39 roundtrip determinism, word collision detection, insufficient entropy rejection. **Implementation note**: library layer ships now (encode/decode/verify_confirmation); the stdin-no-echo prompt helper lives with the CLI (462.5) which owns the interactive flow | **P0** | ~250 | `cfg/identity_master.rs` (new), `Cargo.toml` (`bip39 = "2"`) | ✅ done |
| 462.4b | **Master storage — encrypted file**: `cfg/identity_master_file.rs` — `MasterSeedFile { ciphertext, nonce, argon2_params, salt, version }` wire format. KDF: Argon2id с default params (m_cost=64MB, t_cost=3, p_cost=4). AEAD: ChaCha20-Poly1305 (consistent с veil's existing cipher). Save: `save_master_seed_encrypted(path, seed, password)` — prompts confirmation password match, writes `~/.config/veil/master.enc` с 0o600 mode. Load: `load_master_seed_encrypted(path, password) -> Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>`. Migration to / from BIP39 (изменить password: decrypt → re-encrypt with new). CLI flags: `identity create --save-encrypted [--password-file PATH]` / `identity rotate --master-file ~/.config/veil/master.enc`. Password может поступить из env-var `VEIL_MASTER_PASSWORD` для automation, но NOT persisted on disk. Tests: encrypt/decrypt roundtrip, wrong-password rejection, tampered-ciphertext rejection, Argon2 param-bump backward-compat. **Implementation note**: wire format is `IM v1` with in-band Argon2id params + KDF-downgrade guard (`MIN_M_COST_KIB = 16 MiB`). Binds AAD `"veil.master.v1"` to the ciphertext, 0o600 file mode on Unix, `change_master_file_password` helper for rotation | **P0** | ~350 | `cfg/identity_master_file.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.5 | **CLI `identity create`**: generate master_seed, derive keypairs, build IdentityDocument, mine PoW (difficulty 16), publish to DHT. **BIP39 paper backup показывается ВСЕГДА и обязательно** — flow прерывается пока user не retype 3 random words из phrase (доказывает что записал). `--yes-i-wrote-it-down` для неинтерактивных сценариев (тесты, scripts) — запрещено в interactive mode. **Encrypted file — опциональный addition**, не замена paper: `--save-encrypted [--password-file PATH]` дополнительно создаёт `master.enc` после BIP39 flow. Identity_sk saved to `~/.config/veil/identity.toml` (hot key, всегда). На выходе: QR code с identity_id + active pubkey (см. 462.26) для добавления в контакты. **Rationale**: даже с encrypted file, offline paper — единственная true-immutable backup; disk fails, passwords forgetten, но бумага в safe deposit box пережёвывает всё. **Implementation note**: library-layer flow `cfg/sovereign_flow.rs::create_identity()` composes master_seed generation (with optional extra entropy), BIP-39 encoding, master_sk derivation, identity_id computation, per-instance identity_sk generation, master-certified `IdentityKey` + freshness signature, PoW mining, document signing, `LocalInstance::load_or_init`, optional encrypted-master-file persistence — 14 scenario tests. CLI subcommands `veil-cli identity create` + `veil-cli identity show` ship in `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs`: `create` takes `--veil-dir/--label/--pow-difficulty/--valid-for-secs/--password-file/--extra-entropy-file/--yes-i-wrote-it-down` flags, persists the signed `IdentityDocument` to `<veil_dir>/identity_document.bin`, emits BIP-39 phrase in 6 × 4-word formatted blocks; `show` decodes + pretty-prints via `format_identity_summary`. 9 CLI scenario tests (persistence, password-file, entropy-file, empty-input guards, create→show round-trip, instance_id stability). | 462.12 | **InstanceRegistry publish/fetch**: `node/identity/instance_registry.rs` — publish на online/offline transitions, coalesce updates (min 5 min между publishes чтобы не забить DHT), fetch API для consumer path'ей. Republish scheduled (раз в 6 часов). Tests: 2-node simulation с 3 instances. **Implementation note**: signing-side library layer ships in `node/identity/publish.rs` — `build_instance_registry` + `sign_instance_registry`, `build_name_claim` + `sign_name_claim` (with rarity-proportional PoW mining), `sign_mlkem_cert` (with identity_key_idx bounds + validity-window guards), `sign_identity_document` (for rotate/revoke re-sign flows), `build_instance_entry` convenience builder, `duplicate_instance_id` sanity helper. Publish-side DI kit ships alongside: `IdentityPublisher` async trait (`put(dht_key, value) -> Result<(), PublishIoError>`) with four per-record helpers (`publish_identity_document`, `publish_instance_registry`, `publish_mlkem_cert`, `publish_name_claim`) and the `publish_full_identity` orchestrator that lands doc+registry+cert in one call and returns a `PublishSummary` (identity_id, instance_id, per-record versions, canonical DHT keys). `MlKemKeyCert::dht_key(identity_id, instance_id)` helper in proto layer keys the cert by instance so rotations replace at the same slot. 16 round-trip tests — sign↔verify, multi-device registry, name-claim resolution via resolver + quorum, ML-KEM cert chain verification, bad-idx + inverted-window guards, **plus: canonical-key triple-put, publish-then-resolve via shared in-memory DHT, idempotent rotation-replaces-cert, transport-error bubble-up**. All 1973 lib tests pass (single-thread sweep); clippy clean. DHT publish/fetch loop + online/offline coalescing + scheduled republish land with the runtime wiring (library is pure DI — runtime plugs any DHT backend into the trait). **Runtime DHT publisher + startup one-shot shipped**: `node/identity/publisher_dht.rs::DhtBackedPublisher` is the thin `IdentityPublisher` adapter wrapping `Arc<KademliaService>` — `put(dht_key, value)` → `KademliaService::store_local` (full distributed put to K closest replicas is a future extension; for MVP the local shard is authoritative and peer lookups walk to the originator via Kademlia traversal). `NodeRuntime::start()` auto-publishes the loaded `IdentityDocument` at startup immediately after the session registry is created — peers walking the DHT keyspace can now retrieve the signed document without the legacy node_id path. 2 adapter tests + end-to-end library round-trip test through `publish_identity_document`. All 2110 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Scheduled republish loop shipped**: `spawn_sovereign_identity_republish_task` fires every 6 hours (matching the `IdentityDocument freshness` figure in `docs/identity-model.md`); no-op on legacy nodes. Routed through the standard `RuntimeService::SovereignIdentityRepublish` variant so both startup and reload paths spawn it consistently. First tick is consumed to avoid duplicating the one-shot startup publish; subsequent ticks re-publish both the identity document AND the single-entry `InstanceRegistry` via the `DhtBackedPublisher`. Graceful `debug` / `warn` logs per record per tick. **InstanceRegistry publish at startup shipped**: new `SovereignIdentity::build_and_sign_registry(reg_version, instances)` helper composes `build_instance_registry` + `sign_instance_registry` using the handle's active identity_sk + sig_key_idx + identity_id; `NodeRuntime::start` builds a single-entry registry for this node's instance (`reg_version = 1` for MVP — version persistence + monotonic bump across restarts is a follow-up, benign for now since peers tie-break on sig) and publishes alongside the document. Registry helper test proves sig verifies against the active subkey's pubkey via the canonical `INSTANCE_REGISTRY_SIG_CONTEXT` + canonical_signing_bytes shape. **MlKemCert publish at startup shipped**: new `SovereignIdentity::sign_mlkem_cert(mlkem_pubkey, valid_from, valid_until, cert_version)` helper wraps `sign_mlkem_cert` with `identity_id`, `active_instance_id`, `sig_key_idx`, `identity_sk`, and `document` from self. `NodeRuntime::start` certifies the node's own auto-loaded ML-KEM-768 encapsulation key (already in `mlkem_ek`) with a 30-day validity window and publishes the cert at the canonical `(identity_id, instance_id)` DHT slot. Helper test verifies the produced cert passes `verify_mlkem_cert` end-to-end (cert chain + window + subkey-in-doc). **6h republish loop extended** to refresh the cert with a fresh 30-day validity window on each tick — `valid_until` always rolls forward, so long-running nodes never let their cert window lapse. Thus all three canonical sovereign-identity records — `IdentityDocument`, `InstanceRegistry`, `MlKemKeyCert` — now publish together at startup and keep publishing every 6 h while the node runs. **NameClaim publish shipped**: new `SovereignIdentity::sign_name_claim(name, claimed_at_unix)` helper normalizes the name via `name_claim_v2::normalize_name` (rejects non-ASCII / homoglyphs), mines rarity-proportional PoW via `sign_name_claim`, signs with the active identity_sk. Disk persistence: `save_name_claim(veil_dir, claim)` writes `<veil_dir>/name_claims/<name>.bin` with tmp-then-rename atomicity; `load_persisted_name_claims(veil_dir) -> Vec<NameClaim>` scans the directory (returns empty when missing), deterministically sorts by name, and silently skips corrupt `.bin` files so one bad file doesn't block startup of the rest. Startup publish iterates the persisted claims and publishes each via `publish_name_claim`; the 6h republish loop re-scans the directory each tick so names claimed mid-session start publishing without a node restart. 5 new tests — sign produces valid claim, normalize rejects `alíce`, save/load round-trip, missing dir returns empty, corrupt file skipped but good claims still loaded. **CLI `identity claim-name <name>` shipped**: `veil-cli identity claim-name <name> [--veil-dir <path>]` loads the sovereign identity, calls `SovereignIdentity::sign_name_claim` (normalises name → mines PoW → signs with active identity_sk), persists via `save_name_claim` to `<veil_dir>/name_claims/<name>.bin`, and prints the claim + path + a note that the running daemon will publish on its next 6h republish tick. 4 CLI tests — happy-path writes file + emits message, rejects non-normalizable name (`alíce` homoglyph), reports `SovereignLoad` on unprovisioned dir (no panic, no corrupt write), idempotent on repeat (same filename overwritten cleanly). End-to-end messenger flow for `@alice` resolution is now usable: `veil-cli identity create` → `veil-cli identity claim-name alice` → restart daemon → `NameResolver::resolve("alice")` on Bob's node hits Alice's published claim in the DHT → resolves the full cert chain → returns `ValidatedIdentity`. **On-change republish shipped**: the 6-hour republish loop now runs a fast secondary poll every 60 s that stats `<veil_dir>/identity_document.bin`.  When the file's mtime advances (CLI `rotate` / `revoke` writes a new document), the loop reloads `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir`, rebuilds the registry against the new subkey + sig_key_idx, and immediately re-publishes both document and registry via the `DhtBackedPublisher`.  Propagation time for a key-compromise revocation drops from `≤ 6h` to `≤ 60 s`.  Load failure (corrupt write mid-rotate, permissions, etc.) is logged `warn` and the previous in-memory identity is kept — no silent loss of service.  Logs emit `node.sovereign_identity.reloaded_on_change` on successful reload with both old and new `document_version` | **P1** | ~350 | `node/identity/publish.rs`, `node/identity/sovereign.rs`, `node/identity/publisher_dht.rs` (new), `node/task_registry.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `proto/mlkem_cert.rs`, `cmd/cli.rs`, `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs` | ✅ done |

Magic `"NM"` + version byte. **Character whitelist (ASCII-only MVP)**: разрешены только `[a-z0-9#_-]`, case-insensitive (normalize to lowercase before hashing). Max 64 bytes. Reject любые Unicode characters — предотвращает homoglyph-typosquatting (`alíce` vs `alice`). `required_difficulty(name)` — rarity-proportional PoW (short ASCII alphabetic → 28-30, alphanumeric 5-7 chars → 22, Discord-style `alice#1234` → 14, long random → 12). Signed by **любой active** identity_sk (не обязательно конкретной instance). Replaces Epic 175 NameRecord. Wire migration (breaking). Two-step DHT fetch с cache (name → identity_id cached for TTL). Offline verification через embedded cert_proof (single DHT round for "fast path" если consumer already knows identity_id). **Implementation note**: resolver uses DI-style `NameLookup`/`IdentityLookup` async traits so production wires the DHT runtime and tests use in-memory fakes. Cache is capacity-bounded (4096) with oldest-touched eviction and a 5-minute TTL | **P1** | ~250 | `node/identity/resolver.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.15 | **Wire refactor**: `recipient_node_id: [u8; 32]` → `recipient: Recipient` везде. `struct Recipient { identity_id: [u8; 32], instance_tag: InstanceTag }`; `enum InstanceTag { Any, All, Specific([u8; 16]) }` — on-wire 17 байт с tag byte. Touches `DeliveryEnvelope`, `DELIVERY_FORWARD`, `TransitFramePayload`, `RouteCache`, `RouteAnnounce/Withdraw` origin, `PEX advertise`, `AnycastAdvertise`, `MailboxFetch`. Самый большой эпик, wire-format breaking — one-shot migration. **Progress (phased)**: **Phase 0** ✅ — `Recipient`+`InstanceTag` proto types (21 tests). **Phase 1** ✅ — additive accessors (5 tests). **Phase 2** ✅ — 18 runtime consumers migrated. **Phase 3a** ✅ — field renamed across ~120 sites: `recipient_node_id` → `recipient_identity_id` on all four wire structs. **Phase 3b1** ✅ — `DeliveryEnvelope.recipient_identity_id: [u8; 32]` evolved to `DeliveryEnvelope.recipient: Recipient`.  Python script retargeted 34 construction sites from `recipient_identity_id: X` to `recipient: crate::proto::recipient::Recipient::any(X)`; `.recipient_identity_id` reads on `DeliveryEnvelope` instances now go through the `recipient_identity_id()` method or direct `.recipient.identity_id` field access; all ~1900 tests still pass. Wire format unchanged — encoder writes only `self.recipient.identity_id`, decoder wraps incoming bytes in `Recipient::any(...)`. Mailbox structs (`MailboxFetchPayload`, `MailboxFetchReplicaPayload`, `MailboxAckPayload`) keep `recipient_identity_id: [u8; 32]` unchanged — mailbox is identity-scoped, not instance-scoped. **Phase 3b-wire** (in progress) — evolve wire format to natively carry the `InstanceTag` byte (33 B unbounded / 49 B specific) so peers can actually request `InstanceTag::All` fan-out or `::Specific` targeting over the network.  Breaking wire-format change, gated behind dispatcher/session InstanceTag-awareness (462.20 / 462.17).  **Phase 3b-wire-1** ✅ — `DeliveryEnvelope` now carries the recipient via the new fixed-49-byte encoding (`Recipient::encode_fixed_into` / `decode_fixed_from`: identity_id (32) + tag byte (1) + instance_id (16, zero-padded for `Any`/`All`)).  Trade-off: chose fixed-49 over the variable 33/49 form so `OFFSET_ENDPOINT_ID` / `_CONTENT_ID` / `_CREATED_AT` / `_TTL_SECS` / `_PAYLOAD_LEN` / `_PAYLOAD` keep stable offsets (just shifted +17), preserving the dispatcher's fast-path TTL pre-decoder (`node/dispatcher/delivery.rs::is_envelope_expired`) without parsing the whole frame on every relay hop.  `DELIVERY_ENVELOPE_HEADER = 197` (was 180); `RECIPIENT_FIXED_SIZE = 49` const exposed.  Decoder rejects non-zero padding on `Any`/`All` tags as a protocol violation (signals encoder bug or wire tamper).  3 new wire tests — `roundtrip_preserves_instance_tag_all` (Specific too), `decode_rejects_nonzero_padding_on_any` — guard against accidental round-trip loss of the tag.  All 2269 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean.  **Phase 3b-wire-2 inventory complete — no further wire migration needed**: post-3b-wire-1 audit of the originally-listed touchpoints showed every remaining struct is either (a) migrated transitively (`ForwardPayload` carries `DeliveryEnvelope` and inherits the new format end-to-end through every relay hop); or (b) addressing a different namespace — `TransitFramePayload`, `RouteAnnouncePayload`, `RouteWithdrawPayload`, `PexWalk`, `AnycastRecord` all carry session-layer `node_id` ([u8; 32], legacy keypair-derived) NOT sovereign `identity_id`, so the `Recipient` wrapper doesn't apply (instance-tagged routing for the legacy plane is by-design out of scope; legacy peers stay node_id-keyed); or (c) already exposes per-instance addressing via the Epic 462.19 `instance_id: [u8; 16]` separate field — `MailboxFetchPayload`, `MailboxAckPayload`, `MailboxFetchReplicaPayload` (mailbox is identity-scoped + per-instance cursor by design — the Recipient enum doesn't fit because mailbox doesn't have an "All instances" delivery semantic; instead it stores once per identity and serves multiple fetcher instances).  With 3b-wire-1 the only struct that genuinely needed `Recipient::encode_fixed_into` on the wire (because it's the only one that delivers to "all live instances") got it.  462.15 functionally complete.  **Runtime-integration scenario shipped + multi-instance fan-out gap fixed**: `sim/scenarios.rs::sovereign_identity_instance_tag_survives_runtime_layer` — 3-node mesh (alice device 0, alice device 1 restored from 0, charlie); `DeliveryEnvelope` with `Recipient::Any`/`All`/`Specific(_)` round-trips through encode→wire→decode preserving every `InstanceTag` variant; `debug_resolve_recipient` (new test-only `NodeRuntime` accessor over `SessionRegistry::resolve_recipient`) confirms the dispatcher resolver fans `Recipient::All` out to BOTH alice devices, `Specific(instance_a)`/`Specific(instance_b)` each hit exactly one (distinct) peer, bogus `Specific` returns empty, and `Any` lands on one of the live peers.  **Bug fixed**: pre-fix, the sim's `connect()` calls `reload_with` which wipes Charlie's `SessionRegistry` and the subsequent reconnect uses the session-resumption fast path (SESSION_TICKET) which bypasses the `IdentityProof` exchange — `validated_sovereign_identity` came back `None` and the second alice session never slotted into `by_identity_instance`.  Fix: a new `peer_sovereign_identities: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<peer_id, ValidatedIdentity>>>` cache lives on `NodeRuntime` (and is cloned through `SessionRuntimeContext`) — full handshakes write the binding into the cache, `cache_peer_handshake_state` restores it on resumption, so resumed sessions still slot into `by_identity_instance` and `Recipient::All` fans out correctly.  Cache survives `reload_with` (parallel to `peer_pubkeys`).  2277 lib tests + clippy clean | ✅ all wire phases done + multi-instance fan-out closed — `Recipient`+`InstanceTag` proto types (21 tests), additive accessors (5 tests), 18 runtime consumers migrated, ~120-site rename `recipient_node_id` → `recipient_identity_id`, `DeliveryEnvelope` field evolved to `Recipient`, fixed-49 wire format with `InstanceTag` round-trip preserved | `proto/recipient.rs`, `proto/delivery.rs`, `proto/budget.rs`, `proto/routing.rs`, `proto/pex.rs`, `proto/anycast.rs`, `node/dispatcher/*`, `node/routing/*`, `node/mailbox/*` | ✅ done |
| 462.16 | **Handshake refactor** (Epic 406 extension): `IdentityProof` payload в OVL1 handshake — содержит `identity_id` + `identity_pubkey` (current subkey) + `ephemeral_sig` (subkey signs X25519 ephemeral pk, anti-MITM) + `cert_proof` (master_pk + master_sig over identity_pk). Peer verify'ит без DHT fetch. Cache peer `(identity_id → master_pk, active_identity_pk)`. Lazy revocation check через DHT при появлении anomaly. **Implementation note**: library layer ships — `proto/identity_proof.rs` (self-contained wire type: magic `IP`, v1, embeds `identity_id`, `master_pk`, inline `IdentityKey` fields + `master_sig`, `proof_valid_until_unix`, `freshness_hour`, `ephemeral_x25519_pk`, `ephemeral_sig`; 10 proto tests covering encode/decode round-trip, magic/version/truncation/trailing/oversize guards, zero-len sig rejection, ephemeral signing-message stability + anti-tamper binding); `node/identity/publish.rs::sign_identity_proof` producer (copies `IdentityKey` fields from the signed document, signs `EPHEMERAL_SIG_CONTEXT \|\| identity_id \|\| valid_until \|\| ephemeral_pk` with active `identity_sk`); `node/identity/verify.rs::verify_identity_proof` verifier (identity_id = BLAKE3(master_pk) binding, master-cert-chain inline verify via reconstructed `certify_message`, ephemeral-sig verify, `FRESHNESS_HOUR_SKEW`, `valid_until` window, local `RevocationCache` check — returns `ValidatedIdentity`); 9 producer/verifier tests — round-trip, wire round-trip → verify, MITM ephemeral swap, tampered identity pk (master-cert breaks), tampered master pk (identity_id mismatch), expired window, stale freshness hour, revoked subkey in cache, bad sig_key_idx. All 2058 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Runtime-wiring constraint discovered**: `KeyAgreementPayload` cannot be extended with a trailing-length-prefix `identity_proof` trailer — existing Epic 406 / OVL1 framing leaves non-structured bytes after `ephemeral_sig` on the wire and the current decoder tolerates them silently. An attempted additive wire trailer (explored 2026-04-21) misinterpreted those bytes as a fake `proof_len` and broke 3 real-handshake tests in `node::admin` (outbound session, sessions-list, debug-accept-routes). Reverted. **Correct integration**: carry the proof on a dedicated new session frame (`SessionMsg::IdentityProof`) sent between KA and SESSION_CONFIRM, or extend `IdentityPayload` via explicit version byte bump (NOT trailing append). **Dedicated-frame slot reserved**: `SessionMsg::IdentityProof = 15` variant landed in `proto/family.rs` with full `TryFrom<u16>` mapping and 4 discriminant-stability tests (every variant 0..15 round-trips, unknown 16+ errors cleanly, IdentityProof=15 confirmed). Existing `node/dispatcher/session.rs` catch-all handles the new variant gracefully as `NotHandled` (handshake-flow frames are consumed by the handshake state machine, not the steady-state dispatcher). **Frame-consumer helper shipped**: `node/identity/verify.rs::verify_identity_proof_frame(body, ka_ephemeral_pubkey, cache, now)` + `FrameProofError` enum — the exact one-call helper the runtime handshake invokes after reading the `IdentityProof` frame: (1) decode body via `IdentityProof::decode`, (2) cross-check `proof.ephemeral_x25519_pk == ka.ephemeral_pubkey` (critical anti-replay binding), (3) run full cert-chain + freshness + local-cache checks, returns `ValidatedIdentity`. 6 tests — matched-pk happy path, MITM pk-mismatch (proof bound to eph_a but KA said eph_b), garbage body, empty body, expired-proof (past valid_until), revoked-subkey-in-cache. **Capability-flag negotiation shipped**: `cap_flags::SUPPORTS_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITY = 1 << 7` in `proto/session.rs`; `CapabilitiesPayload::supports_sovereign_identity()` + `sovereign_identity_negotiated(&peer)` helpers. Bit is set by sovereign-identity-aware peers at handshake time; proof-frame exchange happens only when BOTH sides advertise it. Legacy peers advertise `0` in that bit and skip the frame entirely — no breaking wire change. 5 tests — stable bit value (`1 << 7`) with no overlap against other cap flags, helper reads bit correctly, `sovereign_identity_negotiated` requires both sides, survives encode/decode round-trip, legacy-peer backwards-compat (default-ctor caps have the bit unset). **Runtime provider struct shipped**: `node/identity/sovereign.rs::SovereignIdentity { document, sig_key_idx, identity_sk }` + `SovereignIdentityError` enum + `load_from_dir(veil_dir)` loader that reads `identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` (the exact disk layout `cfg/sovereign_flow::create_identity` writes), validates the SK↔subkey pubkey binding, rejects out-of-bounds sig_key_idx / in-doc-revoked active subkey / non-Ed25519 subkeys (Falcon deferred), and exposes `identity_id()`, `active_instance_id()`, `sign_proof(ephemeral_pk, valid_until, freshness_hour)` — the one-call emitter for the runtime handshake.  `Debug` impl redacts the SK. 10 tests — loader happy path (against a real `create_identity` output), missing-document/SK-file rejection, corrupted-document / wrong-SK-seed rejection, out-of-bounds idx, active-subkey-revoked-in-doc rejection, sign_proof → `verify_identity_proof_frame` end-to-end round-trip (the exact loop runtime will run), loaded document passes full `verify_identity_document`, SK redaction in Debug. Runtime handshake plumbing (teach `node/session/handshake.rs` to optionally take `&SovereignIdentity`, emit `SessionMsg::IdentityProof` after KA send, call `verify_identity_proof_frame` on receive, stash `ValidatedIdentity` on the session) is now the ONLY remaining runtime task — every library primitive, wire slot, error type, loader, and end-to-end test it needs is ready. Library-level equivalent scenario ships in `scenario_two_nodes_mutual_identity_proof_exchange` (462.23). **Runtime handshake wiring shipped**: `node/session/handshake.rs::perform_ovl1_handshake` accepts an optional `SovereignHandshakeCtx<'_>` parameter bundling the local `SovereignIdentity` + `RevocationCache` + `now_unix_secs`. When `Some`, the handshake (a) sets `cap_flags::SUPPORTS_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITY` in outgoing CAPABILITIES, (b) checks `caps.sovereign_identity_negotiated(&remote_capabilities)` after the mutual CAPS exchange, (c) emits `SessionMsg::IdentityProof` frame bound to the local KA ephemeral pk between KA and SESSION_CONFIRM when both sides advertised, (d) reads + verifies the peer's frame via `verify_identity_proof_frame`, (e) populates `OvlHandshakeResult::validated_sovereign_identity: Option<ValidatedIdentity>`. Legacy peers (or sessions without local sovereign material) skip the exchange entirely — no breaking wire change, no behavior change when `ctx = None`. `IdentityProof::decode` relaxed to tolerate trailing bytes (Epic 409.5 handshake padding). 8 call sites updated to pass `None` for the new param. 2 new handshake integration tests — `sovereign_proof_exchange_runs_end_to_end` creates two real sovereign identities via `create_identity`, drives the full OVL1 handshake over in-memory duplex streams, asserts both sides advertise the capability bit, both sides end up with each other's `ValidatedIdentity`, session keys still match; `sovereign_proof_exchange_skips_when_peer_lacks_support` proves asymmetric setup (one side with ctx, one without) falls back cleanly to the legacy path. All 2088 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Runtime auto-loader + handshake threading shipped**: `NodeRuntime` + `NodeServices` + `SessionRuntimeContext` all carry `sovereign_identity: Option<Arc<SovereignIdentity>>` + `revocation_cache: Arc<RevocationCache>`. At `start()`, the runtime auto-loads from `<veil_dir>/identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` via `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir` (logs `node.sovereign_identity.loaded` with identity_id + instance_id); absent files → legacy mode with graceful log. Persistent `revocation_cache.bin` opens alongside so revocation pushes survive restarts. The single production `perform_ovl1_handshake` call site in `spawn_outbound_session` builds a `SovereignHandshakeCtx` from `runtime.sovereign_identity` when loaded and threads it into the handshake — every outbound session on a provisioned node now advertises `SUPPORTS_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITY` + exchanges the `IdentityProof` frame when the peer also advertises support. Both inbound (listener) and outbound (connect) paths carry the ctx through `SessionRuntimeContext` clones (3 construction sites patched consistently). **SessionRegistry identity index shipped**: `SessionEntry` gained `validated_sovereign_identity: Option<ValidatedIdentity>`. `SessionRegistry` maintains a secondary `by_identity: HashMap<[u8; 32], SessionId>` populated on insert (when present) and cleared on remove (with correct handling of the "same identity reconnects on a new session — old close must not evict the new entry" race). Public lookup surface: `get_by_identity_id(&[u8; 32])` returns the live `SessionEntry`; `peer_id_for_identity(&[u8; 32])` is the convenience wrapper delivery/mailbox code calls to map a sovereign addressee to the transport-level `peer_id` it already knows how to route to. `cache_peer_handshake_state` in `runtime.rs` forwards `r.validated_sovereign_identity` into the new `SessionEntry` field so every successful sovereign handshake is now indexable by `identity_id` across the node. 4 new SessionRegistry tests cover: sovereign entry is indexed + `peer_id_for_identity` returns the right peer, non-sovereign entry is NOT indexed (legacy peers), identity index is cleared on remove, reconnect-under-same-identity does not evict the newer entry when the older session's close fires later. All 2092 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Epic 462.16 is now functionally complete end-to-end** — a provisioned node authenticates sovereign identities on every OVL1 handshake and makes them addressable by `identity_id` across the runtime | **P0** | ~500 | `proto/identity_proof.rs`, `proto/family.rs`, `proto/session.rs::CapabilitiesPayload`, `node/identity/publish.rs`, `node/identity/verify.rs`, `node/identity/sovereign.rs` (new), `node/session/handshake.rs`, `node/session/manager.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done (library + wire + handshake + node loader + session index) |
| 462.17 | **Session dedup refactor**: ключ `(identity_id, instance_id)` вместо `node_id`. Multiple инстансы одной identity могут одновременно иметь сессии с одним peer'ом. Ticket binds к `peer_instance_id` — prevents AEAD nonce reuse при resumption. Breaking internal change, 5+ call sites. **Implementation note**: library layer ships — `SessionRegistry::by_identity_instance: HashMap<([u8; 32], [u8; 16]), SessionId>` replaces the single-session `by_identity` index from 462.16. Insert populates the composite key (when `ValidatedIdentity` is present); remove clears only if the slot still points at this session (correct race handling for reconnect-under-same-identity + delayed-old-close). Public API: `get_by_identity_instance(id, inst)` / `peer_id_for_identity_instance(id, inst)` (Specific routing), `peer_ids_for_identity(id) -> Vec<(instance_id, peer_id)>` (All fan-out), `peer_id_for_identity(id)` (Any — picks first, unspecified order documented as scoring follow-up). 4 new tests covering two-instances-coexist (laptop+phone under one identity both individually reachable + All sees both + Any returns one), specific-instance-missing, empty-fan-out, remove-one-leaves-others (+ the existing reconnect-eviction test still passes). **Ticket-layer `peer_instance_id` binding shipped**: `SessionTicket` gained `peer_instance_id: [u8; 16]`; plaintext wire grows 144 → 160; encrypted blob 172 → 188 (constants `SESSION_TICKET_ENCRYPTED_SIZE` kept as legacy + new `SESSION_TICKET_ENCRYPTED_SIZE_V2`). `TicketIssuer::decrypt` accepts both sizes; legacy 172-byte tickets decrypt with `peer_instance_id = [0; 16]` so pre-462.17 peers resume cleanly. New `TicketIssuer::issue_for_instance(session_id, peer_id, peer_instance_id, tx_key, rx_key)` gives sovereign-aware callers the binding; legacy 4-arg `issue` remains and stamps `[0; 16]` as the default. `runner.rs` ticket-size guard accepts both V1 and V2. 2 new tests — V2 round-trip preserves instance_id, hand-crafted 172-byte legacy blob decrypts to `peer_instance_id = [0; 16]`.  Note on `tx_registry`: no additional keying change is needed because each instance of a sovereign identity has a distinct transport-level `node_id` (BLAKE3 of its own `identity_sk` pubkey).  The existing `peer_id`-keyed `SessionTxRegistry` therefore intrinsically separates Alice's laptop (`node_id = hash(laptop_sk_pub)`) from Alice's phone (`node_id = hash(phone_sk_pub)`) — each gets its own outbox channel.  The sovereign-routing chain `(identity_id, instance_tag) → SessionRegistry → peer_id → tx_registry.send_to` is already correct end-to-end | **P0** | ~300 | `node/session/manager.rs`, `node/session/ticket.rs`, `node/session/runner.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `proto/session.rs::SessionTicket`, `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.18 | **Reputation migrate**: keyed by `identity_id` instead of `node_id`. Survives rotation natively (identity_id stable). Shared между instance'ами одной identity (by design: identity-level reputation для LB). Migration: wipe existing reputation store (сеть не deployed). **Implementation note**: `ReputationTracker` API + docs updated to key on `identity_id` (rotation-stable, master-pk-derived); the 32-byte map shape stayed identical so call-site signatures don't churn.  `SessionRegistry::identity_id_for_peer(peer_id) -> Option<[u8; 32]>` (new helper) maps a session-layer `peer_id` to the validated sovereign `identity_id`; legacy peers without a sovereign identity fall back to `peer_id` as a degenerate identifier so legacy behaviour stays unchanged.  Three callers migrated: `runtime.rs` `session_opened` / `session_closed` (resolve identity BEFORE registry remove so the binding is still available) and `dispatcher/delivery.rs` ACK-gated relay credit (Epic 461.6).  Rotation-survival invariant verified by `reputation_survives_peer_id_rotation_under_stable_identity` test: 200 relay credits → rotation (peer_id changes, identity_id stable) → 200 more credits → ledger combined to 40 points (NOT split into two 20-point ledgers); a separate identity has its own ledger; multi-device (laptop + phone) ACKs combine on the SAME ledger.  Existing reputation tests (`relay_success_accumulates`, `eviction_at_capacity`, `evict_stale_*`) all pass against the renamed internals. 2271 lib tests + clippy clean | **P1** | ~200 | `node/reputation.rs`, `node/session/manager.rs` (`identity_id_for_peer`), `node/runtime.rs` (session open/close callers), `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` (relay credit caller) | ✅ done |
| 462.19 | **Mailbox refactor** (multi-device sync foundation): mailbox keyed by `identity_id`. Shared inbox, per-instance cursor: `HashMap<instance_id, last_acked_seq>`. GC стирает message только после ACK от ALL active instances ИЛИ после `instance_stale_after = 7d` force-advance. Support `InstanceTag::All` fan-out. **Implementation note**: library-layer foundation ships in `node/mailbox/instance_cursors.rs::InstanceCursorMap` — pure in-memory data structure keyed `(identity_id, instance_id) → (last_acked_seq, last_seen_unix)` with the canonical GC query `lowest_cursor(identity_id) -> Option<u64>` (minimum cursor across non-stale instances). API: `record_ack(id, inst, seq, now)` (monotonic — can't regress), `touch(id, inst, now)` (refresh last_seen without advancing cursor — used on fetch), `prune_stale(id, now, stale_after_secs)` (drops instances inactive > `DEFAULT_INSTANCE_STALE_AFTER_SECS = 7 days`; returns dropped ids; frees the identity entry when empty), `cursor_for_instance` / `active_instances` / `forget_instance`. 12 tests covering: ack monotonicity, lowest-cursor across 2+ instances picks min, none-when-unknown, zero-when-not-acked, touch refreshes without advancing, prune drops stale only, prune removes identity when empty, explicit forget, 3-instance fan-out semantics (all must ACK before GC), active_instances list, prune-missing-identity no-op. **`MailboxStore` now carries the tracker** as an `instance_cursors` field and exposes the full per-instance API: `record_instance_ack(id, inst, seq, now)` (monotonic, does NOT pop the queue — this is the key backwards-compat guarantee), `touch_instance(id, inst, now)`, `lowest_acked_cursor(id) -> Option<u64>` (the GC floor query), `cursor_for_instance(id, inst)`, `prune_stale_instances(id, now, stale_after_secs)`. Pure additive extension — legacy `ack` / `ack_seqs` still pop from the queue immediately, so existing callers see zero behaviour change. 7 new tests — per-instance ack does NOT pop, lowest-cursor queries tracker, none-for-unknown-identity, cursor_for_instance reads, touch refreshes last_seen survives prune, prune delegates to tracker, legacy ack still pops regardless of tracker state. **`MailboxBackend` trait extended** with `record_instance_ack` / `touch_instance` / `lowest_acked_cursor` / `cursor_for_instance` / `prune_stale_instances`.  Default impls forward to the legacy single-cursor `ack` path — safe no-op for backends that haven't been wired yet.  `InMemoryBackend` overrides delegate to the `MailboxStore` tracker; `WalBackend` forwards to its inner `InMemoryBackend` (WAL persistence of tracker state — new `WAL_INSTANCE_ACK` record type + replay — is a follow-up; MVP accepts at-least-once semantics on restart since delivery ACKs are already idempotent). 5 new backend tests — in-memory routes acks to tracker, in-memory ack does NOT pop queue, in-memory prune delegates cleanly, Wal forwards cleanly, `Box<dyn MailboxBackend>` trait-object dispatch picks the overridden impl (critical for service.rs handler routing). **Wire extension shipped**: `MailboxFetchPayload` gained `instance_id: [u8; 16]` field (wire size 40 → 56 bytes full, with 40 still accepted legacy); `MailboxAckPayload` gained a trailing 16-byte `instance_id` after the seqs array (backwards-compat: decoders that see exactly `34 + count*8` bytes default to `[0; 16]`; encoders always emit the trailer so the value propagates end-to-end). Budget constants split into `MAILBOX_FETCH_SIZE` (legacy 40) + `MAILBOX_FETCH_FULL_SIZE` (56) and `MAILBOX_ACK_MIN_SIZE` (legacy 34) + `MAILBOX_ACK_FULL_MIN_SIZE` (50). `MailboxAckPayload::single_for_instance(id, seq, inst)` convenience ctor tags a single-seq ACK with an explicit instance; legacy `::single(id, seq)` keeps `[0; 16]` default. 4 new proto tests — legacy 40-byte decode defaults to zero instance_id, full 56-byte round-trip preserves instance_id, legacy hand-built ack payload decodes with zero instance, ack with explicit instance round-trips. All ~15 construction sites across `node/dispatcher/*`, `node/mailbox/*`, and proto tests updated with explicit `instance_id: [0u8; 16]` legacy default. **Service handlers route per-instance acks** (`node/mailbox/service.rs`). `handle_fetch`: when `payload.instance_id != [0; 16]` the fetching instance is `touch_instance`-ed in the backend so it doesn't age out before its next ACK; zero instance_id is the legacy no-op path. `handle_ack`: when `instance_id` is non-zero the service calls `backend.record_instance_ack(recipient, instance, max(seqs), now)` — cursor advances but **queue is NOT popped**, so the entry stays reachable for other instances' later fetches; when `instance_id` is zero the service falls back to the legacy `ack_batch` → pop-on-ack path. Quota release (Epic 173.B) happens on both paths so the sender isn't penalised by a queue that's merely awaiting other-instance ACKs. 4 new service tests — fetch with instance_id touches tracker (cursor=0 seeded, last_seen=now), ack with instance_id advances cursor without popping (queue unchanged), ack with zero instance_id still pops (legacy path preserved), multi-instance laptop+phone ACK independently and `lowest_acked_cursor` returns the min.  **Slice 6 — `cleanup_expired` consults `lowest_acked_cursor`.** Before scanning queues for TTL eviction, the store prunes stale (> 7d inactive) per-instance cursors so a single offline instance never pins GC forever.  Then each entry is kept only if `instance_floor.map_or(true, |f| entry.seq > f) && (recipient_sleeping || ttl_unexpired)` — the per-instance floor takes precedence over sleep grace (once every instance has ACKed, the message is delivered end-to-end).  Recipients with no tracked instances (legacy single-device) keep the TTL-only behaviour unchanged.  4 new store tests — below-cursor drop, partial-ACK keeps msg, stale-instance prune unblocks GC, legacy no-instances falls back to TTL.  **All six slices green.  Epic 462.19 is functionally complete**: Alice's laptop and phone each ACK every inbound message independently, the mailbox drops messages only once every live instance has ACKed (or a laggard is pruned after 7 days), legacy single-device peers see zero behavioural change | **P1** | ~500 | `node/mailbox/instance_cursors.rs` (new), `node/mailbox/service.rs`, `node/mailbox/backend.rs`, `node/mailbox/store.rs`, `proto/delivery.rs::MailboxFetch`, `proto/delivery.rs::MailboxAckPayload`, `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
