Integration с existing delivery paths. **Implementation note**: library-layer routing surface ships — `SessionRegistry::resolve_recipient(&Recipient) -> Vec<[u8; 32]>` is the single entry point the delivery dispatcher will call: `Any` → 0-or-1 peer_ids, `Specific(inst)` → 0-or-1 peer_ids, `All` → vec of every live `(instance, peer)` pair. Empty return = "no live session" → dispatcher falls through to mailbox / DHT paths. New helper `FrameDispatcher::resolve_sovereign_delivery_targets(&Recipient) -> Option<Vec<[u8; 32]>>` with the semantic: `None` → no registry wired (test/legacy build, fall back to `route_cache`); `Some(vec![])` → registry wired but no live session (fall back to mailbox/DHT); `Some(vec![peer_id, ...])` → dispatcher fans out. 3 tests — helper returns `None` without a wired registry (test dispatcher default), empty when identity is offline, and correct Any/Specific/All resolution when a live sovereign session exists. **Sovereign fast-path wired into `relay_forward`**: `try_sovereign_direct_forward(payload, sender_peer, traffic_class, relay_hops_out)` builds the forward frame (same Epic 411.1 single-allocation pattern as the legacy path, just 32-byte `next_hop_node_id` per target) and sends directly via `session_tx_registry.send_to`. Called at the top of `relay_forward` immediately after preconditions check — when the sender's identity is `Recipient::Any|Specific|All`-addressed and at least one live sovereign session exists (minus split-horizon + self-loop), delivery is 1 hop through an authenticated, known-alive session instead of however many hops `route_cache` scoring thinks. Falls through to the scored `route_cache` path unchanged on empty / all-filtered / send-fail. 3 new tests — live session hits fast-path (asserts the real frame with correct `next_hop_node_id` prefix lands in the outbox), no registry returns false, split-horizon filters the sender peer out. (c) master_seed restore on fresh device; (d) 2-device messenger scenario (send к identity → oба получают, oба ACK'ают, mailbox GC правильный); (e) 3-node LB (`InstanceTag::Any` distributes по score); (f) name resolution end-to-end (publish claim → remote resolve → verify cert chain). **Implementation note**: library-layer slice ships — `node/identity/integration_tests.rs` covers 17 scenarios end-to-end using in-memory fakes; **plus one production-code end-to-end test** in `node/session/handshake.rs::sovereign_handshake_feeds_session_registry_end_to_end` that drives two real sovereign handshakes over duplex streams (using the same `SovereignHandshakeCtx` production uses), runs the exact `SessionEntry` insertion pattern `cache_peer_handshake_state` uses, then confirms `SessionRegistry::resolve_recipient` returns the right peer for `Any` / `Specific` / `All` tag variants in both directions — the first test that validates the entire sovereign-identity runtime pipeline (handshake → auth → index → routing resolution) as a single unit: onboarding→verify, BIP-39 device-wipe recovery, encrypted-file round-trip, multi-device fan-out messenger flow, pair-symmetric safety numbers, app-state sync across instances, chat-backup restore, revocation-push blocks leaked key, anomaly watcher catches attacker-added device, freshness lifecycle, instance-state persistence, RevocationCache on-disk, Alice+Bob full exchange (fan-out + sync), no-cross-identity-leakage, **plus 5 runtime-composition scenarios**: (a) two-nodes mutual IdentityProof exchange — each side generates X25519 ephemeral, signs proof binding identity_sk→ephemeral_pk, peer verifies, both derive identical shared secret, MITM-tamper sanity check (this is the library-level shape of the 462.16 handshake); (b) full pairing ceremony end-to-end — source signs invite, renders QR URI, target scans, `hash_pair_secret(scanned) == invite.pair_secret_hash`, both sides complete X25519 + derive identical 6-digit OOB, fake-QR tamper check (defeats QR phishing); (c) QR contact import cross-validated against full cert-chain verifier — Alice publishes `veil:identity` URI, Bob scans, URI-level `identity_id == BLAKE3(master_pubkey)` gate, then full `verify_identity_document` agrees on every field; (d) **multi-device mailbox delivery** — Alice drops a message into Bob's mailbox; Bob's laptop fetches+ACKs with its instance_id (queue stays intact since phone hasn't seen it); Bob's phone then fetches the same message, ACKs with its instance_id; `cleanup_expired` drops the message exactly once both instances have ACKed; re-fetch by either returns empty; (e) **laggard-instance wait** — laptop ACKs, phone fetches but doesn't ACK, cleanup keeps the message, phone finally ACKs, cleanup drops it. All 2245 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Real-TCP runtime slice shipped (first two scenarios)**: `sim/network.rs` — new `SimNetworkBuilder::sovereign_identities(true)` option provisions each node with a fresh sovereign identity (`create_identity` into a per-node veil subdir) *before* `SimNode::start`, so `NodeRuntime::start`'s canonical `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir` probe auto-picks them up.  Requires `next_sim_config_path_with_dir` (per-node subdirectory, isolates `identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` + MLKEM keypair + revocation cache between sibling nodes) + `provision_sovereign_identity_for_sim` (wraps `create_identity` with `#[cfg(test)]` PoW difficulty, no master encryption). `.name_claims(Vec<Option<String>>)` builder option + `provision_name_claim_for_sim` helper sign+persist a `NameClaim` between `create_identity` and `SimNode::start` so the runtime's startup scan picks it up and `publish_name_claim`'s the claim into the local DHT.  `NodeRuntime::sovereign_identity()` accessor + `NodeRuntime::debug_republish_sovereign_identity()` helper — the latter re-publishes doc + registry + ML-KEM cert + persisted name claims into the local DHT, matching the initial-startup publish path; needed in sim because `connect()` calls `reload_with` which swaps `self.dht` to a fresh `KademliaService` and wipes the pre-reload store. `sim/scenarios.rs::sovereign_identity_two_nodes_establish_session` — first runtime-level scenario: 2-node full-mesh with sovereign identities, asserts distinct `identity_id` + distinct `active_instance_id` + `identity_id == compute_identity_id(master_pubkey)` binding on both sides.  `sovereign_identity_name_claim_publishes_locally` — second scenario: node 0 pre-claims `@alice`, after mesh formation + `debug_republish_sovereign_identity`, asserts node 0's local DHT holds the claim at the canonical `NameClaim::dht_key("alice")` slot with a decoded `identity_id` matching node 0's sovereign identity, and that node 1 does *not* hold the claim (separate DHT instances).  **Cross-node replication shipped**: `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs` — unsigned-STORE whitelist now accepts the four sovereign-identity magics (`NM` / `ID` / `IR` / `MC`) with a cheap decode-sanity check at ingest; full signature / PoW / master-cert-chain verification is deferred to the resolver path (`NameLookup::fetch_name_claim` + equivalent) since recipients often lack the IdentityDocument context when the STORE arrives.  Malformed magic-prefixed bodies still bounce as Violation (prevents poisoning via forged magic header).  `node/runtime.rs` — `is_self_authenticating_dht_value` extended in parallel so the periodic republish loop actually propagates sovereign records to K-closest peers.  5 dispatcher tests cover the new paths: valid NameClaim accept (fabricated via real `create_identity` + `sign_name_claim`) + 4 malformed-body rejects (magic + junk for each of NM/ID/IR/MC).  `sim/scenarios.rs::sovereign_identity_name_resolves_over_dht` — third real-TCP scenario: node 0 claims `@alice`, after mesh formation + `debug_republish_sovereign_identity` + `debug_force_dht_republish`, the replicated STORE lands on node 1's local DHT, decodes to the same NameClaim with `identity_id` matching node 0's sovereign identity.  This closes the cross-node resolution gap.  `sovereign_identity_all_records_cross_replicate` — fourth real-TCP scenario: asserts every sovereign record type (`IdentityDocument` `ID`, `InstanceRegistry` `IR`, `MlKemKeyCert` `MC`, `NameClaim` `NM`) lands on node 1's local DHT at its canonical key, each decodes cleanly, and the `identity_id` (and `instance_id` for `MC`) binding holds.  Guards against whitelist regressions where one magic is accidentally stripped.  `sovereign_identity_mailbox_put_over_tcp` — fifth scenario: node 0 dispatches a `MAILBOX_PUT` frame targeting node 1's sovereign `identity_id` via `NodeRuntime::debug_send_mailbox_put` (new test-only helper that builds the plaintext frame + pushes onto the session outbox; the runner's `apply_tx_cipher` handles encryption before the wire).  Bob's dispatcher routes the PUT through `handle_mailbox_put` into `MailboxService.handle_put`, and `debug_mailbox_total_entries()` flips from 0 to 1 within 5s.  Proves the full identity-addressed runtime pipeline (wire ingress → session decrypt → dispatcher route → mailbox persist) works end-to-end over real TCP.  `sovereign_identity_mailbox_fetch_and_ack_round_trip` — sixth scenario: extends the put flow with the recipient-side fetch+ACK round-trip.  Bob's runtime calls `debug_mailbox_fetch(identity_id, [0;16], 0)` (new helper that wraps `MailboxService.handle_fetch`), decodes the backlog entry, verifies `envelope.payload` matches the bytes Alice sent, then calls `debug_mailbox_ack(identity_id, [0;16], seqs)` — legacy pop-on-ack semantics drain the mailbox to zero.  Closes the minimal "Alice sends → Bob receives + ACKs" end-to-end messenger round-trip over real TCP.  `sovereign_identity_multi_device_fetch_per_instance_gc` — closes 462.23 sub-task (d) end-to-end: Alice puts one envelope to Bob's mailbox; Bob's two devices (modeled as distinct `instance_id` values) each `fetch` independently with their own per-instance cursors and see the same payload; the first device's `ack` advances ITS cursor only — `cleanup_expired` keeps the entry because the second device hasn't ACKed; after the second `ack`, `cleanup_expired` drops the entry and subsequent fetches by either instance return empty.  Validates the Epic 462.19 per-instance cursor design end-to-end at the runtime layer over real TCP — single sender, single store, multi-device fetch with correct GC.  New `debug_mailbox_cleanup_expired` test-only helper triggers the GC pass synchronously (production runs it from a background tick).  `sovereign_identity_recipient_any_picks_highest_scored_instance` — closes 462.23 sub-task (e): score-based `InstanceTag::Any` distributes load across two Alice devices.  New `SessionRegistry::peer_id_for_identity_scored<F>` accepts a per-instance scorer closure and returns the highest-scoring peer with deterministic tie-break on `(instance_id, peer_id)` byte order; NaN scores demote to -∞ so a real score always beats them.  4 unit tests (highest wins, tie-break determinism, unknown-identity returns None, NaN handling) + 3-node sim scenario where Charlie scores Alice's two devices opposite ways and confirms `Any` picks the high-scored instance both times — covering the sub-task (e) load-distribution invariant.  Production scorers compose reputation + RTT + battery + role; the test fixtures show the contract.  `sovereign_identity_rotate_with_revoke_propagates` — seventh scenario: node 0's on-disk document is rotated with `revoke_current = true` mid-scenario (fresh subkey, prior subkey appended to `revoked_keys`, `document_version` → 2, `revocation_seq` → 1, re-signed + PoW re-mined); the republish helper now reloads from disk on every call so the v2 doc propagates without waiting for the production 60 s mtime-poll.  Node 1's local DHT flips from v1 (no revocations) to v2 with `revoked_keys.len() == 1`; `identity_id` stays stable across the rotation (proving the master-pk-derived id is rotation-invariant).  New sim infrastructure: `provision_sovereign_identity_for_sim` stashes raw master_seed bytes at `master_seed.sim` so rotate/revoke scenarios can re-derive `master_sk` without BIP-39 / password-file fixtures (`sim_read_master_seed(veil_dir)` helper).  `sovereign_identity_name_survives_rotate_with_revoke` — eighth scenario: after the rotate-with-revoke, Alice re-signs `@alice` under the new active subkey and overwrites the persisted claim; the republish helper reloads + propagates both the v2 doc and the re-signed claim.  Bob's copy of the claim binds to the same stable `identity_id`, is signed under a `signing_identity_key_idx` that is *not* listed in Bob's copy of `revoked_keys` (verified via `pubkey_hash` lookup), and the old pre-rotate subkey IS listed as revoked.  Closes the "name survives key rotation" invariant from the 462.23 sub-task (a) spec.  `sovereign_identity_old_claim_rejected_after_revoke` — ninth scenario, the security-inverse of the eighth: Alice rotates-with-revoke but INTENTIONALLY skips re-signing `@alice`.  Bob holds the pre-rotate claim (signed under subkey 0) plus the v2 document (subkey 0 revoked).  Manual `pubkey_hash` lookup against `revoked_keys` — the exact predicate `resolver::verify_name_claim` runs at resolve time — confirms the claim's `signing_identity_key_idx` is revoked, i.e., any resolver surfaces `NameClaimSigKeyRevoked` rather than trusting the signature.  Symmetric positive check asserts the new active sig_key_idx is NOT revoked, so a properly re-signed claim (the scenario 8 path) would still verify.  Closes 462.23 sub-task (b): "revoke compromised identity_sk, verify peers reject frames signed by old key" at the NameClaim layer.  `sovereign_identity_master_seed_restore_preserves_identity_id` — tenth scenario: closes 462.23 sub-task (c).  New `SimNetworkBuilder::restored_from(Vec<Option<usize>>)` option provisions opted-in nodes via `restore_identity` against another node's previously-saved `master_seed` (read from `master_seed.sim`).  In the test, node 1 is restored from node 0's seed: both nodes report the same `identity_id` + same `master_pubkey` (master-pk-derived → rotation-invariant → restore-invariant), but distinct `active_instance_id` (each device mints its own random tag at provisioning time) and distinct `identity_sk` subkey pubkeys (restore generates a fresh per-device subkey rather than transferring the source's).  Both subkeys are non-revoked on their local docs.  Session-layer node_ids still differ — proves session addressing remains independent of sovereign identity, so multiple devices sharing one identity can each maintain their own TCP sessions.  Mirrors the production "user lost device, recovers from BIP-39 paper backup" flow.  `sovereign_identity_pairing_ceremony_propagates_to_peer` — eleventh scenario: full pair ceremony e2e (Epic 462.30) integrated with the sim mesh.  Alice runs `PairingSource` on a free loopback port (loaded from her veil_dir's identity_sk + the sim master_seed); a side-channel `PairingTarget` dials it via real TCP and runs the full ceremony — both sides converge on the OOB code, target generates a fresh ed25519 SK + X25519 ephemeral, Alice master-certifies `target_pk`.  Alice's v2 IdentityDocument (with the appended IdentityKey) is persisted; the target's paired state is persisted via `save_paired_target_state` and the resulting veil_dir loads cleanly via `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir` with the sig_key_idx override pointing at the new subkey + matching `identity_id`.  Alice republishes; Charlie (the bystander mesh peer) receives the v2 doc with both subkeys present, and Charlie's view of the appended subkey's pubkey matches the target's loaded subkey.  Closes the "new device pairs in and is visible to peers via the existing mesh" e2e proof — the sovereign-identity messenger's onboarding flow now works end-to-end on real TCP from QR scan through DHT propagation.  Remaining 462.23 follow-up scenarios (identity-addressed mailbox delivery + multi-instance LB + revocation propagation + pairing ceremony e2e) layer on this whitelist.  **All 6 sub-tasks closed**: (a) rotate+name-survives (scenarios 7 + 8), (b) revoke rejects old-sig (scenario 9), (c) master_seed restore on fresh device (scenario 10), (d) multi-device messenger with per-instance cursors (scenario 13), (e) score-based `InstanceTag::Any` LB (scenario 14 + 4 unit tests), (f) name resolution end-to-end (scenarios 2 + 3).  14 real-TCP sim scenarios total + 17 library-layer integration tests + 5 runtime-composition library tests + multi-instance fan-out bug closed via the `peer_sovereign_identities` cache. 2283 lib tests, clippy clean | **P0** | ~600 | `node/identity/integration_tests.rs`, `sim/network.rs` (`sovereign_identities` + `name_claims` + `restored_from` + per-node veil dir + `sim_read_master_seed`), `sim/scenarios.rs` (14 `sovereign_identity_*` scenarios), `node/runtime.rs` (`sovereign_identity()` accessor + `debug_republish_sovereign_identity()` + `debug_send_mailbox_put` / `_fetch` / `_ack` / `_cleanup_expired` + `debug_resolve_recipient` / `_any_scored` + `peer_sovereign_identities` cache + sovereign magics in `is_self_authenticating_dht_value`), `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs` (sovereign STORE whitelist), `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` (sovereign STORE tests), `node/session/manager.rs` (`peer_id_for_identity_scored`, `identity_id_for_peer`, `debug_identity_instance_keys`) | ✅ done |

| 462.26 | **QR: identity sharing (public)**: `cmd/identity/qr.rs` — рендер в терминал QR-кода с public identity info для добавления в контакты. Payload: `veil:identity?id=<hex identity_id>&master_pk=<hex>&name=<optional>` (URI scheme, совместимо с QR-scanning apps — browser может open через deep link handler). CLI: `veil-cli identity qr [--name <preferred name>]`. Unicode block rendering через `qr2term = "0.3"` crate (pure-Rust, no C deps). Terminal-fallback ASCII mode для non-UTF8 терминалов (`--ascii`). Integrated в `identity create` flow — показывается в конце с инструкцией "scan this to share your identity". **Implementation note**: URI layer ships — `proto/identity_contact.rs` defines `IdentityContact { identity_id, master_algo, master_pubkey, name: Option<String> }` with canonical `to_uri()` / `from_uri()` + `ContactUriError` enum. Format: `veil:identity?id=<64-hex>&master_algo=<ed25519\|falcon512>&master_pk=<hex>[&name=<normalized>]`. Scheme + algo-name case-insensitive on parse, hex case-insensitive, field order arbitrary on decode but canonical on encode. Master-pk length enforced per algo (32 B Ed25519 / 897 B Falcon-512). Name field routed through `NameClaim V2` normalizer so homoglyph / Unicode / case vectors are rejected at the URI boundary. 4 KiB size cap. 23 tests — Ed25519 + Falcon-512 round-trips, case-insensitive parse, canonical field order, duplicate / unknown / malformed / missing field rejection, wrong hex length, oversized, non-normalizable name, live-identity integration (URI produced from a real `create_identity` output preserves the `identity_id == BLAKE3(master_pk)` binding after round-trip). **Renderer + CLI shipped**: `qrcode = "0.14"` (pure-Rust, `default-features = false` — strips the `image` / `svg` transitive deps since terminal output doesn't need them). `veil-cli identity qr [--veil-dir PATH] [--name NAME] [--ascii]` loads the on-disk `SovereignIdentity`, builds an `IdentityContact`, prints the canonical URI on its own line, then renders a QR matrix beneath it — Unicode half-block by default (▀ / ▄ / █ / space, each terminal row covers 2 QR rows so the output stays ≈square in fixed-cell terminals) with inverted convention (QR-dark → light glyph) for dark-bg terminals, `--ascii` fallback emits `##` / `  ` per module. 4-cell quiet zone per QR spec. EC level M balances scanner robustness against the ~100–200 B URI payload. 4 CLI tests: happy-path URI + half-block glyph presence, `--ascii` fallback emits `##` and suppresses half-block chars, `--name` round-trips into the URI, unprovisioned-sovereign → `SovereignLoad` error | **P1** | ~200 | `proto/identity_contact.rs`, `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs` (`qr`, `render_qr_halfblock`, `render_qr_ascii`), `cmd/cli.rs` (`IdentityCommand::Qr` + `IdentityQrArgs`), `Cargo.toml` (`qrcode = "0.14"`) | ✅ done |
| 462.27 | **QR: cold master backup (encrypted)**: `cmd/identity/qr_backup.rs` — **password-protected** QR для offline cold backup на случай потери paper и encrypted file. Payload: base64-url(`MasterSeedFile` encrypted bundle from 462.4b). Flow: `veil-cli identity export-qr-backup --password ****` → encrypts master_seed + displays QR (обычно фотографируется и хранится offline). Restoration: `veil-cli identity import-qr-backup --from-image backup.png --password ****` — decrypts и восстанавливает identity. Password **never** encoded в QR — out-of-band (verbal / note в safe). Multi-QR split если bundle > 2KB через `--split N`. **Не для mobile login** — для этого 462.30 pairing ceremony. Use case: paper phrase потеряна, encrypted file corrupted — остаётся photo backup. **Implementation note**: library layer ships — `cfg/identity_master_file.rs` refactored to expose byte-level helpers `encode_master_seed_encrypted_with(seed, password, m, t, p) -> Vec<u8>` + `decode_master_seed_encrypted(bytes, password) -> Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>` (the file-based `save`/`load` wrappers now delegate to these); same wire layout as the on-disk format so `master.enc` and the QR payload are interchangeable.  `cfg/identity_master_qr.rs` (new) defines `MASTER_BACKUP_URI_SCHEME = "veil:master-backup"` + `MASTER_BACKUP_URI_V1 = 1` + `MAX_MASTER_BACKUP_URI_BYTES = 4096` (single-QR fits at EC level Q).  `encode_master_backup_uri(seed, password)` + `_with(...)` produces canonical `veil:master-backup?v=1&data=<base64url-no-pad>` (~225 B for default Argon2id params); `decode_master_backup_uri(uri, password)` accepts shuffled field order, case-insensitive scheme, rejects oversize / wrong scheme / missing query / missing/duplicate/unknown fields / unsupported version / invalid base64 / wrong-password-or-tampered with one indistinguishable error kind.  `MasterBackupUriError` covers all the failure modes.  15 tests — round-trip recovers seed, wrong password rejected, wrong scheme, missing query, missing/duplicate/unknown fields, unsupported version, invalid base64, shuffled field order, case-insensitive scheme, oversized URI, tampered data field, byte-helper round-trip (regression guard for the underlying encode/decode pair).  **CLI shipped**: `veil-cli identity export-qr-backup (--password-file FILE | --phrase-file FILE) --qr-password-file FILE [--veil-dir P] [--ascii]` — recovers the master_seed via the same password/phrase fixtures the rotate/revoke/pair-listen flows use, encrypts under a SEPARATE QR password (intentional: the QR backup password should never be co-located with the source decryption material), encodes via `encode_master_backup_uri`, renders the QR matrix (half-block by default or `##` via `--ascii`, same renderer as 462.26), and emits a WARNING line reminding the operator to convey the QR password out-of-band.  `veil-cli identity import-qr-backup <URI> --password-file FILE [--veil-dir P] [--label NAME]` decodes the URI via `decode_master_backup_uri`, runs `restore_identity` into the target veil_dir (which itself writes `identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` + `instance_id`), prints the recovered identity_id.  Defensive UX: `--password-file` and `--phrase-file` are mutually exclusive on `clap`; runtime-side neither-set returns a clear error.  6 CLI tests — export emits canonical URI + QR-block characters + WARNING, full export→import round-trip recovers the identity_id and mints a fresh per-device subkey, export via `--phrase-file` works, missing-seed-source is a clear error, import with wrong password rejected, import with malformed URI rejected. Final wire-format choices documented above; `--split N` for multi-QR payloads above the 4 KiB envelope cap is a P3 follow-up (default-Argon2id payloads are ~225 B → single QR fits at EC level Q) | **P2** | ~300 | `cfg/identity_master_file.rs`, `cfg/identity_master_qr.rs`, `cmd/cli.rs` (`IdentityCommand::{ExportQrBackup, ImportQrBackup}`), `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs` (`export_qr_backup`, `import_qr_backup`, `read_master_seed_from_source`, `read_password_file_trimmed`) | ✅ done |
| 462.28 | **Device linking event (push notification)**: при добавлении новой instance в InstanceRegistry, dispatcher шлёт event `DeviceLinkedEvent { new_instance_id, label, linked_at, linked_by_instance }` через DELIVERY_FORWARD с `InstanceTag::All` (кроме только что добавленного — он же знает что сам подключился). Получающие instances показывают user alert ("Новое устройство 'Mobile-iPhone' подключено к вашей identity"). Защита: event подписывается adding instance's identity_sk — не подделать. Receiving instance может trigger anomaly workflow если не он инициировал linking (possibly compromise). **Implementation note**: proto layer ships (signed `DeviceLinkedEvent` with self-link guard); dispatcher fan-out wiring depends on 462.12/462.20 | **P1** | ~200 | `proto/identity_events.rs` (new) | ✅ done (proto layer) |
| 462.29 | **Identity fingerprint (safety numbers)**: helper `identity_fingerprint(identity_id, their_identity_id) -> String` — deterministic 60-digit numeric representation (Signal-style): `BLAKE3("veil.fingerprint.v1" || min(ids) || max(ids))` → split в 6-digit groups. Pair-symmetric (Alice и Bob видят одинаковые цифры, может сравнить по телефону). CLI: `veil-cli identity fingerprint --with @alice`. App-level UX: messenger показывает fingerprint в contact profile; users сравнивают out-of-band для verification. Changes при compromise-induced rotation → user alert "safety number changed". **Implementation note**: library layer ships with 60-digit output in 12 × 5-digit groups; CLI subcommand lands with 462.5/462.9 | **P1** | ~150 | `crypto/identity_fingerprint.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.30 | **QR pairing ceremony (mobile login) — per-instance key + OOB confirmation**: главный механизм login на новом устройстве. **Flow**: (1) **source device** (где identity active с master_sk): `veil-cli identity pair-invite --ttl 300s` → source generates ephemeral `pair_secret: [u8; 32]`, publishes `PairingInvite { identity_id, pair_secret_hash, source_instance_id, expires_at }` к DHT signed by identity_sk, displays QR `veil:pair?id=<hex>&secret=<base64>&endpoint=<transport>` + **human-readable 6-digit OOB confirmation code** (random, shown на source screen only — никогда в QR). (2) **target device** (mobile): scans QR → connects direct к source endpoint → authenticates via pair_secret → **target генерирует свой собственный new identity keypair `(target_sk, target_pk)` (master_seed NEVER transferred — только target устройство знает свой sk!)**. (3) **Master certification step**: target sends target_pk к source; source unlocks master_sk (password prompt) → `master_sk.sign(certify_msg(target_pk, valid_from))` → source appends `IdentityKey { pubkey: target_pk, bound_instance_id: target_generates_random, master_sig }` к IdentityDocument → publishes v++. (4) **OOB verification step**: source displays "Target shows code: XXX-XXX?" with its stored 6-digit code; target displays same code (derived deterministically из session key); user visually compares → confirms on source. If mismatch → abort, remove key from document. (5) target receives IdentityDocument snapshot + generates InstanceEntry с `bound_identity_key_idx` → publishes updated InstanceRegistry → triggers 462.28 DeviceLinkedEvent. **Security critical**: (a) master_seed NEVER transferred — target устройство никогда не видит master material; (b) target's identity_sk никогда не leaves target device; (c) OOB confirmation code защищает от QR phishing (attacker-as-fake-target) — user видит real code на source, fake target не сможет show matching code; (d) pair_secret ephemeral (5 min TTL, one-time-use), QR only hash; (e) если target device lost/compromised, revoke только его identity_key — остальные instances работают. Integration tests: 2-device pairing, OOB mismatch detection, expired pair_secret. **Implementation note**: library layer ships — (a) `proto/pairing_invite.rs` defines signed `PairingInvite { identity_id, pair_secret_hash, source_instance_id, issued_at, expires_at, signing_identity_key_idx, sig }` (magic `PI` v1, `PAIRING_INVITE_SIG_CONTEXT = "veil.pairing_invite.v1"`, 2 KiB cap, `canonical_signing_bytes` + `signing_message` helpers, `is_valid_at`, `dht_key(identity_id, source_instance_id)` — concurrent invites from different source instances don't collide); (b) `hash_pair_secret(&[u8;32])` domain-separated BLAKE3 (`veil.pair_secret.v1`) so the published hash cannot alias other protocol values; (c) `PairingUri { identity_id, pair_secret, endpoint, expires_at_unix }` with `to_uri()` / `from_uri()` emitting canonical `veil:pair?id=<64-hex>&secret=<b64url-no-pad>&endpoint=<raw>&expires=<u64>` + `PairUriError` (oversize, bad scheme, missing/duplicate/unknown/malformed field, bad hex/b64, wrong length, invalid expires, endpoint empty/oversized/reserved-char rejection — `&=?#` forbidden in endpoint); (d) `crypto/pair_oob.rs::derive_pair_oob_code(session_key)` — domain-separated (`veil.pair.oob.v1`) 6-digit code rendered as `XXX-XXX` (3-3 split, Signal/WhatsApp UX) + `PairOobCode::parse`/`to_string_canonical` helpers; (e) `node/identity/publish.rs::sign_pairing_invite` producer (signs with active identity_sk, rejects inverted validity window + out-of-bounds sig_key_idx). 43 tests — wire round-trip, magic/version/truncation/trailing/oversize guards, empty-sig rejection, inverted-window rejection, canonical-signing-bytes suffix, signing-message context prefix, `is_valid_at` window, `dht_key` stability + per-instance distinctness, `pair_secret_hash` domain separation, URI round-trip (canonical + shuffled field order), scheme/algo case-insensitive, duplicate/unknown/malformed/missing-field rejection, invalid-hex/b64 rejection, wrong-length secret, invalid-expires, reserved-char endpoint, empty/oversized endpoint, oversized URI, producer sign↔verify against active subkey, bad sig_key_idx, inverted window, end-to-end plausibility (source publishes invite + QR → target scans URI → `hash_pair_secret(scanned) == invite.pair_secret_hash`), OOB determinism + both-devices-converge via X25519 DH sanity, OOB domain-separation vs raw BLAKE3, OOB canonical shape, OOB parse rejection cases. 2058 lib tests pass; clippy clean. **Source-side CLI shipped**: `veil-cli identity pair-invite --endpoint <uri> [--ttl-secs N] [--veil-dir PATH] [--ascii]` — draws a fresh 32-B `pair_secret` from `OsRng`, domain-separated-hashes it, calls `SovereignIdentity::sign_pair_invite` (new thin convenience over `sign_pairing_invite` that fills `identity_id` + `active_instance_id` + `sig_key_idx` from the loaded handle), emits canonical `veil:pair?…` URI + QR matrix (half-block by default or `##` via `--ascii`, same renderer as 462.26) + the transport endpoint line the target dials back on. `--ttl-secs` hard-capped to 1 h (`PAIR_INVITE_MAX_TTL_SECS = 3600`) with zero-ttl rejection — keeps `pair_secret` exposure tight. `pair_secret` never persisted (lives only for CLI render); only the signed hash survives on disk. 7 CLI tests: happy-path URI+half-block+endpoint line, `--ascii` fallback suppresses half-block chars, URI round-trip → `PairingUri::from_uri` recovers `pair_secret` that still hashes non-zero, TTL oversized rejection, TTL zero rejection, unprovisioned-sovereign rejection, reserved-char endpoint rejection. **Target-side URI inspector**: `veil-cli identity inspect-uri <uri>` — pure-parsing diagnostic that dispatches on scheme (`veil:identity` → `IdentityContact::from_uri`, `veil:pair` → `PairingUri::from_uri`) and pretty-prints fields (identity_id, master_algo, master_pubkey, name / endpoint, expires_at, pair_secret_hash). Expired pair-invites flagged `EXPIRED`. 7 tests (contact with/without name, pair with future expiry, expired pair, unknown scheme, malformed contact, malformed pair). **Session-frame wire layer shipped**: `proto/pair_session.rs` — 3 fixed-layout frames driving the ceremony after target dial-back: `PairingHello` (target → source, 147 B: pair_secret_hash + target_ek_pk + target_id_pk + target_instance_id + BLAKE3-keyed MAC over prior fields), `PairingCert` (source → target, 39 B header + signed `IdentityDocument` up to 8 KiB: source_ek_pk + doc_len + signed_document), `PairingConfirm` (target → source, 37 B: confirmed-byte + BLAKE3-keyed proof over session_key).  Magic (`PH`/`PC`/`PF`) + version + truncation + trailing-data + oversize-doc guards.  `PairingHello::mac_input` / `PairingConfirm::proof_input` helpers expose the exact preimage bytes so both sides can recompute without duplicating layout.  23 wire tests — round-trip, bad magic, bad version, truncation, trailing-data, oversize doc-len, bad confirmed-byte, MAC/proof-input determinism + domain separation. **Runtime state machines shipped**: `node/identity/pair_runtime.rs` — transport-agnostic `PairingSource` + `PairingTarget` state machines.  Source holds `IdentityDocument` + `identity_sk` + `master_sk` + `pair_secret` + `pow_difficulty` + `now_unix`; on Hello it verifies `pair_secret_hash` matches the published invite, verifies the BLAKE3-keyed MAC (with `pair_secret` as the key, so only holders of the raw QR secret can forge), draws its own X25519 ephemeral, derives `session_key = BLAKE3_keyed(pair_secret, PAIR_SESSION_CONTEXT || shared)`, calls `build_certify(identity_id, ALGO_ED25519, target_pk, target_instance_id, now)` + master-signs to append the target's `IdentityKey`, bumps `document_version` + re-mines PoW + re-signs `document_sig` with the active `identity_sk`, returns the Cert bytes + `derive_pair_oob_code(session_key)`.  Target holds the scanned `PairingUri` + its own fresh Ed25519 SK (seed wrapped in `Zeroizing`) + fresh `target_instance_id` + X25519 ephemeral; `build_hello()` emits the Hello; `handle_cert()` decodes the Cert, asserts `doc.identity_id == uri.identity_id` (blocks MITM swapping entire documents), locates the target's subkey in `doc.identity_keys`, derives the same `session_key` + OOB code; `build_confirm(user_approved)` emits the Confirm with a keyed proof binding the confirmation bit to the session key.  Source's `handle_confirm` constant-time-verifies the proof; `UserAborted` surfaces cleanly so the caller can roll back the pending append.  Ceremony is fully transcript-bound: forged Hello MAC / wrong pair_secret / flipped bytes / MITM'd ephemerals / substituted doc.identity_id / forged proof / wrong-state invocations all reject.  8 ceremony tests — end-to-end happy path (two sides converge on same OOB, doc ends up with +1 master-certified IdentityKey, document_sig re-verifies), user-abort, MITM ephemeral-pk swap (caught by MAC), wrong pair_secret, forged confirm proof, cert with mismatched identity_id (caught by target), wrong-state Hello on source, wrong-state double-Hello on target.  2210 lib tests + clippy clean.  **Async transport layer shipped**: `node/identity/pair_transport.rs` — transport-agnostic `AsyncRead + AsyncWrite` drivers that plug the ceremony state machines onto any stream.  Length-prefixed wire format: `u32-be length || body`, body capped at `MAX_PAIR_FRAME_BODY = MAX_PAIR_CERT_DOC_SIZE + 512` (8.5 KiB) — the reader rejects oversized `length` *before* allocating to defeat resource-exhaustion on the dial-back port.  `read_frame` / `write_frame` are the raw codec helpers.  `run_pair_source(&mut PairingSource, &mut stream, oob_confirm: FnOnce(&str) -> bool) -> Result<SourceTransportOutcome>` reads Hello → calls `handle_hello` → writes Cert → invokes `oob_confirm(oob_code)` to prompt the source-side operator → reads Confirm → cross-checks target's `confirmed` bit against the local decision.  `run_pair_target(&mut PairingTarget, ...)` mirrors it: build+write Hello → read Cert → `handle_cert` → `oob_confirm` → write Confirm carrying the user's bit.  Both drivers always ship a Confirm even on abort so the peer's state machine observes a well-formed frame (no TCP read timeouts on rejected pairings).  Error surface: `PairTransportError::{Io, FrameOversized, Ceremony, TargetAborted, SourceAborted}` — source-side abort takes precedence over target-side abort on the source (local decision fires first).  7 tests via `tokio::io::duplex`: happy-path both-approve (OOB matches across sides, target SK → pubkey matches appended `IdentityKey`, +1 key in finalized doc), target-abort surfaces `TargetAborted` on both sides, source-abort surfaces `SourceAborted` on source and `Ok` on target (target is crypto-complete, source is the one refusing to persist), both-abort surfaces per-side decisions, frame round-trip, oversized-declared-length rejection, EOF-on-truncated-header bubbles as `Io`.  2217 lib tests + clippy clean.  **Paired-device persistence + TCP CLI shipped**: `cfg/sovereign_flow.rs` — new `DEVICE_SIG_KEY_IDX_FILE = "device_sig_key_idx.bin"` (2 B BE u16) carries a per-device `sig_key_idx` override so target devices whose `identity_sk` signs as subkey `≠ document.sig_key_idx` can still be loaded via `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir` (which now consults the override when present, falling back to `from_parts_active` on source-side single-device layouts).  `save_paired_target_state(veil_dir, doc, sk_seed, sig_key_idx, instance_id, label)` atomically writes the 4-file paired target layout (`identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` + `device_sig_key_idx.bin` + `instance_id`) with `SigKeyIdxOutOfBounds` + `Io` error surface. `node/identity/pair_transport.rs` — `run_pair_source_tcp(host_port, source, oob_cb)` / `run_pair_target_tcp(host_port, target, oob_cb)` wrappers that bind/connect + dispatch to the async drivers; used by both the CLI and a `tcp_wrappers_end_to_end_happy_path` integration test (real loopback, both sides converge on OOB). `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs` — `veil-cli identity pair-listen --endpoint tcp://HOST:PORT (--password-file FILE | --phrase-file FILE) [--ttl-secs N] [--veil-dir P] [--ascii] [--yes-i-compared-codes]` binds the listener, prints the canonical `veil:pair?…` URI + QR + endpoint, signs the invite with the active identity_sk, unlocks master_sk via either encrypted `master.enc` (with `--password-file`) or BIP-39 phrase (`--phrase-file`) (same pattern as `identity rotate/revoke`), constructs a `PairingSource` with the loaded keys + #[cfg(test)] pow difficulty, accepts one dial-back, runs `run_pair_source_tcp`, stdin-prompts the operator for OOB compare (unless `--yes-i-compared-codes`), and on success atomic-writes the updated `IdentityDocument` back to `<veil_dir>/identity_document.bin`. `veil-cli identity pair-accept <URI> [--veil-dir P] [--label NAME] [--yes-i-compared-codes]` parses the URI, rejects expired invites (`expires_at_unix ≤ now`), rejects non-`tcp://` endpoints, dials the source, runs `run_pair_target_tcp`, stdin-prompts for OOB compare, and on success writes the paired target state via `save_paired_target_state`. 8 CLI tests cover the error surface (expired invite / non-tcp endpoint / missing-master-unlock) + persistence round-trip (`save_paired_target_state` → `load_from_dir` honours the override → returns target-side `SovereignIdentity`). 2229 lib tests + clippy clean. **Remaining** (out-of-scope for 462.30's P0 deliverable, sit with follow-up epics): running runtime's DHT republish of the updated doc + registry on source side — integrate with the existing 60-second polling loop in `runtime.rs` that already watches `identity_document.bin` mtime; `DeviceLinkedEvent` emit via watcher.rs (462.28 dep); real-TCP `sim/scenarios` integration test exercising the full two-process path (sits with 462.23) | **P0** | ~700 | `proto/pairing_invite.rs`, `proto/pair_session.rs`, `crypto/pair_oob.rs`, `node/identity/publish.rs`, `node/identity/sovereign.rs` (`sign_pair_invite` + override-aware `load_from_dir`), `node/identity/pair_runtime.rs`, `node/identity/pair_transport.rs` (`run_pair_{source,target}_tcp`), `cfg/sovereign_flow.rs` (`save_device_sig_key_idx`, `load_device_sig_key_idx`, `save_paired_target_state`), `cmd/cli.rs` (`IdentityCommand::{PairInvite, InspectUri, PairListen, PairAccept}`), `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs` (`pair_invite`, `inspect_uri`, `pair_listen`, `pair_accept`) | ✅ done (DHT republish + DeviceLinkedEvent live with 462.28 / runtime loop) |

### Размер и scope

**Total: ~12 900 LOC + docs** на 50 подзадач (после второй revalidation 2026-04-20):
- Core identity + lifecycle: 462.0-462.9
- Multi-device + instances: 462.10-462.12, 462.30
- Names + resolver: 462.13-462.14
- Wire refactor: 462.15-462.20
- Tier A/B + Tests: 462.21-462.23
- Docs + cleanup: 462.24-462.25
- QR: 462.26-462.27, 462.30 (pairing)
- Messenger gap: 462.28-462.35
- Revocation persistence: 462.36-462.38
- **Security hardening (revision 2)**: 462.39 (per-instance keys), 462.40 (PoW timestamp-bound), 462.42/462.43 (merged), 462.44 (DHT per-id quota), 462.45 (one-time pre-keys FS), 462.46 (multi-replica resolution), 462.47 (extra entropy), 462.48 (anomaly watcher)

**Estimated timeline**:
- Full-focus single developer: 6-7 месяцев
- Parallel team: 4-5 месяцев

### Критический путь (revision 2, final)

```
462.0 (design)
  │
  ├─► 462.39 (per-instance identity_sk — architectural revision)  ◄── gates many
  │        │
  │        ▼
  ├─► 462.1 (proto: bound_instance_id + freshness + timestamp-bound PoW)
  │        │
  │        ├─► 462.36 (RevocationCache persistent) ─► 462.3 (verifier)
  │        │                                                 │
  │        │                                                 ├─► 462.16 (handshake w/ IdentityProof)
  │        │                                                 │        │
  │        │                                                 │        └─► 462.17 (session dedup)
  │        │                                                 │
  │        │                                                 └─► 462.37 (revocation gossip)
  │        │
  │        └─► 462.10 (InstanceRegistry, per-instance key-bound) ─► 462.12 (publish/fetch)
  │                                                                          │
  │                                                                          ├─► 462.28 (DeviceLinkedEvent)
  │                                                                          └─► 462.30 (QR pairing + OOB code)
  │
  ├─► 462.2 + 462.40 (crypto + timestamp PoW) ─► 462.29 (fingerprint)
  │                                                     │
  │                                                     └─► 462.32 (E2E w/ per-instance fan-out)
  │                                                             │
  │                                                             └─► 462.45 (X3DH one-time prekeys)
  │
  ├─► 462.4 + 462.4b + 462.47 (master storage + entropy) ─► 462.5 (create) ─► 462.6/7 ─► 462.22 (propagate)
  │                                                                                            │
  │                                                                                            └─► 462.38 (auto-freshness)
  │
  ├─► 462.26 (QR sharing) ─► 462.27 (QR cold backup)
  │
  └─► 462.15 (wire refactor) ──┬──► 462.20 (dispatch with InstanceTag)
                                ├──► 462.18 (reputation)
                                ├──► 462.19 (mailbox)
                                ├──► 462.13 (name V2, ASCII whitelist) ─► 462.14 (resolver) ─► 462.46 (quorum)
                                ├──► 462.31 (app-state) ─► 462.34 (backup)
                                ├──► 462.44 (per-identity DHT quota)
                                ├──► 462.48 (anomaly watcher)

Parallel: 462.21 (Tier B) после 462.12
Parallel: 462.35 (wake-up hint) после 462.20
Gate: 462.23 (integration tests) → 462.25 (migration) и 462.33 (guide)
```

**Critical path stars (блокируют downstream work)**:
- **462.39** — per-instance key model; prereq для 462.10/30/32
- **462.1** — proto fields; prereq для 462.3/10/13
- **462.36** — revocation persistence; prereq для 462.3
- **462.5** — `identity create` flow; prereq для всех identity operations
- **462.15** — wire refactor; prereq для всех node_id→identity_id migration

**Starting point**: 462.0 design doc → 462.39 architectural decision → 462.1 proto — эти три формируют foundation. Без них нельзя начинать parallel work.

**Starting point** — эпики 462.0 → 462.1 → 462.2 → 462.3 (design + foundation). Пока эти не готовы — остальное нельзя строить. **Для messenger readiness** дополнительно критичны 462.30 (pairing), 462.32 (E2E cert chain), 462.31 (app-state) — без них app-разработчикам сложно использовать veil как messenger foundation.

### Decisions (locked 2026-04-20)

| Вопрос | Решение | Обоснование |
|---|---|---|
| Signature default | **Ed25519** | Existing veil, proven, smallest pk/sig. Falcon-512 opt-in через `master_algo` field. |
| master_seed size | **32 bytes (256 bits)** | BIP39 24-word standard, соответствует Ed25519 security level |
| BIP39 wordlist | **Standard BIP39 English** | Interop с Bitcoin wallets, proven, user familiarity |
| Master storage | **Paper (BIP39) by default**, optional encrypted file `--save-encrypted` | Paper безопаснее от malware; encrypted file — fallback UX convenience |
| Encrypted file KDF | **Argon2id** | Modern memory-hard, resistant to GPU brute-force |
| Max identity_keys в doc | **8** | 1 active + 7 grace period revoked, ≤ 1 KB |
| Max revoked_keys в doc | **32**, GC entries > 1 year | Balance между audit trail и DHT size |
| IdentityDocument TTL / republish | **6 hours** | Balance freshness vs DHT load |
| instance_id size | **16 bytes** | 128-bit collision space |
| `identity_id` size | **32 bytes** | BLAKE3-256 output, standard |
| PoW: registration | **16** (~1 сек CPU) | Anti-spam новых identity без burden'а для legitimate users |
| PoW: document update | **12** (~100 ms) | Anti-spam мутаций от compromised subkey |
| PoW: name claim | **12-30** rarity-dependent | См. `required_difficulty(name)` — short names expensive |
| Scheduled rotation recommendation | **1 year** | Documented в user guide |
| Name max length | **64 UTF-8 bytes** | Enough для `user#1234` style + long anonymous names |
| Max instances per identity | **16** | ≤2.5 KB InstanceRegistry, covers typical user (3-5 devices) with margin |
| Tier B (encrypted transports) | **Opt-in**, disabled by default | Privacy-safe default; user enables если нужен direct multi-device sync |
| Migration strategy | **Hard break** (network не deployed) | Cleaner, меньше legacy code |

### Messenger gap analysis — что нужно добавить в veil (revalidation 2026-04-20)

При ревалидации под use case "построить мессенджер на veil'е" выявлены следующие **missing pieces**, покрываемые эпиком 462 (дополнительные задачи 462.28-462.35):

| Что нужно messenger'у | Кто предоставляет | Veil task |
|---|---|---|
| Identity (who am I) | veil | 462.1-462.9 ✅ |
| Address resolution (@alice → delivery target) | veil | 462.13-462.14 ✅ |
| Multi-device instances | veil | 462.10-462.12 ✅ |
| Sync messages between user's devices | veil (mailbox shared) | 462.19-462.20 ✅ |
| Async E2E encryption к offline recipient | veil (ML-KEM Epic 190) | **462.32** binding to identity cert chain |
| New device login ("scan QR on phone") | veil | **462.30** pairing ceremony |
| Device linking alert ("новое устройство подключилось") | veil | **462.28** DeviceLinkedEvent |
| Verify "is this really Alice?" (safety numbers) | veil helper | **462.29** identity fingerprint |
| Contact list / friends list (synced multi-device) | app-layer CRDT поверх veil | veil даёт **462.31** storage primitive |
| Read receipts / typing / online status | app-layer CRDT, direct sessions | veil даёт transport (Tier A/B ready) |
| Group chat crypto (MLS) | app-layer, independent | veil только transports frames |
| Push notifications to mobile | OS-level (APN/FCM) + veil wake-up trigger | **462.35** wake-up hint для dozing instances |
| Message backup/restore | app-layer, encrypted blob | **462.34** primitive для signed encrypted DHT blob |
| Block list / mute list | app-layer | no veil primitive needed |
| Profile (display name, avatar) | app-layer storage | 462.31 reusable |

### Доп. задачи для messenger readiness

| # | Задача | Приоритет | LOC est. | Файлы | Статус |
|---|--------|-----------|----------|-------|--------|
| 462.31 | **App-state primitive: signed encrypted DHT blob** — generic storage для app-layer state (contacts, prefs, profile, etc.) с auto-sync через DHT. API: `store_app_state(app_id, key, value)` / `load_app_state(app_id, key)`; encryption = ChaCha20-Poly1305 с ключом `HKDF(identity_sk, "veil.app_state.v1" || app_id || key)` (все instances одной identity могут читать). DHT key: `BLAKE3("veil.app_state.v1" || identity_id || app_id || key)`. Cap ≤ 4 KB per blob (MAX_DHT_VALUE_BYTES). Signed by identity_sk. Version-monotonic updates. **Use cases**: contacts list, profile, block list, preferences — всё encrypted, synced across instances автоматически. **Implementation note**: proto + crypto layer ships — `AppState` wire format (`AS v1`, ≤ 4 KB cap, length-prefix-collision-safe), AAD-bound `(identity_id, version, app_id, key)`, ChaCha20-Poly1305 with caller-supplied 32-byte `app_state_secret` (derivation policy left to caller; primary device derives from `master_seed`, distributes to instances during 462.30 pairing). Length-prefix DHT keying. Caller still publishes/fetches via 462.15-wired DHT runtime | **P1** | ~350 | `proto/app_state.rs` (new) | ✅ done (proto + crypto layer) |
| 462.32 | **E2E key binding к identity cert chain (per-instance ML-KEM keys)**: (a) каждый **instance** имеет свою ML-KEM keypair (не shared — aligns с per-instance identity_sk model 462.39); (b) ML-KEM pk certified by master_sk либо **by instance's own identity_sk** через extended MlKemKeyCert. Sender к `@alice` с `InstanceTag::All` **fan-outs E2E ciphertext**: encrypts separately к каждому instance's ML-KEM pk (Signal-style). С `InstanceTag::Specific(instance_id)` — один ciphertext для target instance. С `InstanceTag::Any` — veil picks один instance, encrypts к его ML-KEM. Chain: master_pk → identity_key cert → ML-KEM cert → pubkey → ciphertext valid. Tests: tampered mlkem rejected, revoked mlkem rejected, fan-out encryption correctness, each instance decrypts только свою copy. **Implementation note**: proto + crypto layers ship — `MlKemKeyCert` wire format with `cert_version`, `verify_mlkem_cert` (7-step chain check: identity binding → subkey idx → in-doc revocation → instance-binding → algo → validity window → sig), `fanout_encrypt`/`fanout_decrypt_one` producing `FanoutEnvelope` per instance with AAD bound to `(sender_id, recipient_id, instance_id, cert_version)`. Dispatcher fan-out wiring + handshake exchange land with 462.16 | **P0** | ~500 | `proto/mlkem_cert.rs` (new), `node/identity/mlkem_fanout.rs` (new) | ✅ done (proto + crypto layer) |
| 462.33 | **Messenger dev guide**: `docs/messenger-dev.md` — how-to для app разработчиков. Покрывает: (a) обнаружение identity (@name resolve), (b) отправка сообщения (DELIVERY_FORWARD с E2E encryption), (c) получение (subscribe к AppMessage event stream через IPC), (d) multi-device sync (shared mailbox + CRDT events), (e) group chat overview (MLS recommended, не veil responsibility), (f) safety number UX, (g) pairing new device, (h) Python / Rust example snippets with `ovl_proto.py` helper. NO new code, всё через existing APIs + 462.28-462.32 | **P1** | — (docs) | `docs/messenger-dev.md` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.34 | **Message backup primitive (encrypted chat export)**: app-initiated encrypted snapshot для "переустановил app, хочу восстановить историю". Messenger app shapes snapshot (list of messages), veil даёт primitive: (a) split в chunks ≤ 4 KB, (b) encrypt each с `HKDF(identity_sk, "veil.chat_backup.v1" || snapshot_id || chunk_idx)`, (c) store в DHT под `BLAKE3("veil.chat_backup.v1" || identity_id || snapshot_id || chunk_idx)`, (d) manifest record со списком chunks, signed by identity_sk. Restoration: same-identity instance fetches manifest → downloads chunks → decrypts → hands to app. TTL 30 days default, operator configurable. **Messenger-agnostic** — любое app может использовать для backup своего state. **Implementation note**: proto + crypto layer ships — `ChatBackupChunk` (3.5 KiB plaintext cap), `ChatBackupManifest` (signed index with per-chunk cipher lengths + size-sum self-check), `split_and_encrypt`/`decrypt_chunk` helpers with AAD-bound `(identity_id, snapshot_id, chunk_index, total_chunks, chunk_bytes)` preventing reorder / truncation. DHT publish and TTL tracking land with the runtime wiring | **P2** | ~400 | `proto/chat_backup.rs` (new) | ✅ done (proto + crypto layer) |
| 462.35 | **Mobile wake-up hint**: mobile instance часто sleeping (iOS/Android power management). Когда incoming message addressed к identity with sleeping mobile instance, veil дает push-notification hint — short UDP wake packet на last-known mobile endpoint + optionally через APN/FCM-proxy (out-of-veil). Hint payload: **only** `{identity_id, instance_id, incoming_sender_hash}` — no content, no sender identity clear-text. Actual message pulled by mobile after wake через mailbox. Подобно тому, как работает Signal notifications. NOTE: APN/FCM integration — **app-layer responsibility** (mobile app registers с FCM/APN, veil just provides trigger signal). Veil just знает "kick instance X awake". **Implementation note**: proto layer ships (`WakeHint` exactly 71 bytes — fits any MTU, no payload room by design, BLAKE3-truncated 16-byte sender_hash with domain separator, dedup_key helper). Dispatcher wake-on-deliver wiring depends on 462.12/462.20 | **P2** | ~300 | `proto/wake_hint.rs` (new) | ✅ done (proto layer) |
| 462.36 | **Revocation persistence: peer-side cache + freshness binding**: решает "stale revocation" attack — если identity_sk leaked, owner revoke'ает через master, но если owner потом offline > DHT TTL, attacker может re-publish pre-revocation version и new peers её примут. Fix в **3 layers**: **(a)** freshness fields в IdentityDocument — `issued_at/valid_until` + `master_freshness_sig` (master_sk подписывает freshness cert на 30 дней max), consumer rejects `now > valid_until` → attacker's stale doc expires без нужды online-presence у owner; **(b)** `RevocationCache` в `~/.config/veil/revocations.bin` — persistent local store `(identity_id → max_seen_revocation_seq, HashSet<revoked_pk_hash>)`, survives process restart / DHT eviction / even node migration; verifier checks cache перед accept — если local cache знает `seq=5`, а fetched doc says `seq=1` → reject as stale; **(c)** `revocation_seq: u64` monotonic counter в каждой IdentityDocument — bumps на каждую новую revocation, consumer can compare versions independently от DHT state. Tests: attacker republishes v1 post-v2, consumer knows v2 locally → reject; fresh peer fetches v1 from mostly-expired DHT → valid_until saves them только if owner refreshed freshness within 30d. **Implementation note**: shipped with atomic-rename binary file persistence (custom `RC v1` wire format) instead of sled — simpler, no alpha-dep, same API surface | **P0** | ~400 | `node/identity/revocation_cache.rs` (new), `proto/identity_document.rs` (freshness fields) | ✅ done |
| 462.37 | **Revocation gossip через direct sessions**: каждый handshake exchanges `RevocationSummary { vec![(identity_id, max_seq)] }` compact list. Если peer A знает `@alice.seq ≥ 5`, peer B — только 2, A pushes full `RevocationEntry` records для @alice к B. Bloom filter compression если >100 identities (MAX_GOSSIP_SIZE=8KB). B adds in its local cache (462.36). Epidemic flood: revocation seen → propagates через PEX-style fanout. Result: revocation "живёт" в сети независимо от owner's availability — attacker'у нужно compromise **всех** peers которые ever видели revocation, to suppress it for new consumers. Rate-limit: один gossip exchange per session per hour. Включается в OVL1 handshake extension или separate message type. **Implementation note**: proto layer ships (`RevocationSummary` + `RevocationPush` with master_pubkey for self-validation + caps for DoS resistance + dedup defenses). Handshake wiring + Bloom compression + rate-limiting are follow-ups tied to 462.16 | **P1** | ~450 | `proto/revocation_gossip.rs` (new) | ✅ done (proto layer) |
| 462.38 | **Owner freshness refresh automation**: owner должен periodically refresh `master_freshness_sig` (раз в ~25 дней, до 30d expiry). CLI `identity refresh-freshness` unlocks master, signs new cert, publishes. Automation options: (a) `veil-cli daemon --unlock-master <schedule>` reads password from secure-storage daily, signs if needed; (b) reminder-based — при `identity show` если freshness expires < 5d, warning; (c) mobile app can do это automatically when user unlocks app (password already in secure enclave). **Default UX**: warning notifications 10d before expiry; if user ignores, identity becomes "stale" after 30d и все consumers reject. Recovery: unlock master, refresh. Operator-configurable `FRESHNESS_WINDOW_DAYS` (default 30, range 7-90) для paranoia levels. **Implementation note**: library layer ships (`refresh_freshness` signs a new `master_freshness_sig` given `master_sk` bytes; `severity`/`needs_refresh` policy predicate; `FreshnessConfig` with spec defaults 30/5/10 days and `validate()` against `MAX_FRESHNESS_WINDOW_SECS`; shrink-window refusal). CLI + daemon scheduler lands with 462.5/462.6 | **P1** | ~250 | `node/identity/freshness.rs` (new) | ✅ done (library layer) |
| 462.39 | **Per-instance identity_sk model (core architectural revision)**: **ключевой decision в Epic 462** — каждое устройство имеет собственный identity_sk, master-certified отдельно. НЕ shared-key model. `IdentityDocument.identity_keys[i]` соответствует конкретному instance через `bound_instance_id: [u8; 16]`. Pairing ceremony (462.30) transfers **никогда master_seed** — target генерирует свой sk, source certifies via master_sk. Revocation granular: revoke только compromised instance's key, остальные продолжают работать. Security gain: compromise одного device ≠ full identity takeover; blast radius = один subkey. Side effects: (a) master_sk unlock'ается при каждом pairing (acceptable — user-initiated explicit event); (b) E2E sender fan-outs ciphertext per active identity_key (Signal pattern, см. 462.32); (c) любой active identity_sk может update shared records (NameClaim, InstanceRegistry) — consistency через version monotonicity. **Этот эпик — revision note**, actual implementation touches 462.1 (proto fields: `bound_instance_id` в IdentityKey), 462.3 (verifier расширен: "this key is bound to this instance"), 462.5 (create теперь creates first instance-bound key, not generic), 462.10 (InstanceRegistry→identity_keys mapping), 462.30 (pairing), 462.32 (E2E fan-out). Tests: compromise-one-device-recover-others scenario, revocation granularity. **Implementation note**: `bound_instance_id` shipped in 462.1; verifier step 7 verifies binding in 462.3; InstanceRegistry points at `bound_identity_key_idx` in 462.10. 462.5/462.30/462.32 will exercise the full lifecycle | **P0** | amendments ~400 cross-cutting | многие файлы (revisions к 462.1/3/5/10/30/32) | ✅ done (architectural decision) |
| 462.40 | **Timestamp-bound PoW (registration freshness)**: prevent rainbow-table pre-computation of registration PoWs. PoW input: `pow_input = canonical_bytes || current_unix_hour.to_be_bytes() || pow_nonce`. Consumer при приёме IdentityDocument reject'ит если `|doc.current_unix_hour - now/3600| > 2` (i.e., > 2 hours skew). Attacker не может pre-compute — должен mine online с recent timestamp. Same pattern для document updates (462.1) и NameClaim (462.13). Small addition (32 bytes overhead), significant anti-Sybil benefit. **Implementation note**: shipped via `freshness_hour: u32` field in 462.1 IdentityDocument and 462.13 NameClaim; verifier step 3 (`FRESHNESS_HOUR_SKEW = 2`) in 462.3 and resolver in 462.14 enforce the ±2-hour window | **P1** | ~80 | `proto/identity_document.rs`, `proto/name_claim_v2.rs`, `node/identity/verify.rs`, `node/identity/resolver.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.41 | **QR pairing OOB confirmation code** (merged в 462.30) — см. 462.30 description для full pairing flow с 6-digit code. Attacker-as-fake-target detection через visual comparison source/target codes | **P0** | merged в 462.30 | — | ⊘ merged (см. 462.30) |
| 462.42 | **Encrypted mailbox_anchor в Tier B** (privacy upgrade, merged в 462.21) — см. 462.21 description. Отдельный placeholder task здесь для visibility | **P2** | merged в 462.21 | — | ⊘ merged (см. 462.21) |
| 462.43 | **ASCII-only name whitelist + homoglyph prevention** (amendment 462.13) — explicit task для tracking. См. 462.13 description. Tests: reject `alíce`, `аlice` (cyrillic а), `alice ` (trailing space), uppercase normalization | **P1** | merged в 462.13 | — | ⊘ merged (см. 462.13) |
| 462.44 | **Per-identity DHT write quota**: DHT nodes enforce "at most N update'ов per identity_id per hour" (default N=10). Protects от compromised identity_sk attacker flooding DHT с version++ updates. Global per-identity counter на DHT node (sliding window). Exceed quota → `DispatchResult::RateLimited`, not Violation (чтобы не trigger auto-ban on legitimate bursts during compromise recovery). Complements existing per-peer DhtQuota (Epic 454.1) — per-peer gates publisher side, per-identity gates DHT side. **Implementation note**: `IdentityWriteQuota` ships in `node/abuse/identity_quota.rs` — sliding-window VecDeque per identity, injected `Instant` for tests, `RateLimited` decision (NOT Violation) per spec, `gc()` for idle bucket eviction, default 10/hour. Dispatcher wire-in lands with 462.15 | **P1** | ~250 | `node/abuse/identity_quota.rs` (new) | ✅ done (library layer) |
| 462.45 | **One-time pre-keys для forward secrecy async E2E (X3DH-style)**: prevents retroactive message decryption если master_seed leak'ается в будущем. Каждый instance публикует **pool of ephemeral ML-KEM prekeys** в DHT (~20 one-time + 1 signed медленно-ротируемый fallback). Sender picks unused prekey, encapsulates shared secret, consumes prekey (DHT record marked "used"). Recipient decapsulates, derives session key, deletes prekey's sk. Forward secrecy: compromise sk позже ≠ decrypt past messages (prekey's sk destroyed). Fallback key защищает when pool exhausted (reduced forward secrecy но available). Pool refill при снижении ≤5 unused. Implementation: `proto/prekey_bundle.rs` + DHT record `PrekeyBundle { identity_id, instance_id, prekeys: Vec<MlKemPrekey>, fallback, master_sig }`, `node/crypto/x3dh.rs` — sender picks, recipient consumes. Integration в existing delivery path: `E2eContext::encrypt_async` tries prekey first → falls back to fallback key → falls back to long-lived (error warning). **Critical задача для messenger FS**. **Implementation note**: proto + crypto layers ship (`PrekeyBundle` with one-time + fallback at `MAX_ONE_TIME_PREKEYS = 8`, ML-KEM-768 reuse from existing `node/e2e.rs`, `PrekeySecretStore` with consume-as-tombstone forward-secrecy invariant). Delivery-path integration depends on 462.15/462.32 | **P1** | ~700 | `proto/prekey_bundle.rs` (new), `crypto/x3dh.rs` (new) | ✅ done (proto + crypto layer) |
| 462.46 | **Multi-replica identity resolution with quorum**: prevent eclipse attack на @name lookup. Resolver queries **k=3 different DHT replicas** (distinct peer-paths), requires **≥2 matching results** чтобы accept. If divergence → try k+1 more replicas; if consistent divergence → return warning к user ("possible eclipse or network partition"). Also cross-check с gossip cache (462.37) — direct peers' revocation knowledge. Config: `identity.resolver_quorum = 2` (default), `resolver_max_replicas = 5`. Negative test: simulated eclipse (malicious peers return stale v1 instead of v2) → resolver queries ≥3, получает divergent, flags warning. **Implementation note**: shipped via `NameLookup::fetch_name_claim_replicated` trait method + `VerifyConfig { resolver_quorum, resolver_max_replicas }` + `ResolveError::QuorumDivergence`. Tests use a `SplitBackend` fake that models per-replica divergence. Gossip-cache cross-check is a follow-up after 462.37 runtime wiring | **P1** | ~200 | `node/identity/resolver.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.47 | **Optional user-supplied entropy mixing**: для paranoid users не доверяющих OsRng. CLI `identity create --extra-entropy-file <path>` XOR'ит content файла в master_seed derivation: `master_seed = OsRng.gen() XOR BLAKE3(file_contents)`. User generates entropy через dice rolls (recommended in docs), coin flips, ambient audio recording. Если file shorter than 32 bytes → error (prevents weak entropy accidentally). No-op если flag не задан — OsRng only. Compatibility: file может быть любой size ≥ 32 B; BLAKE3 always distills к 32 bytes. **Implementation note**: `generate_master_seed_with_extra_entropy` ships in `cfg/identity_master.rs`; uses `blake3::Hasher::new_keyed` with a domain-keyed context and XOR-combines with a fresh `OsRng` draw. CLI flag plumbing lands with 462.5 | **P2** | ~80 | `cfg/identity_master.rs` (extension) | ✅ done (library layer) |
| 462.48 | **Self-document anomaly watcher**: owner's node periodically (~hourly) fetches own IdentityDocument из DHT, compares к last-known-good snapshot. Alert user если: (a) `sig_key_idx` changed к instance которую user не помнит, (b) new `identity_keys` entry который user не авторизовал (unexpected pairing), (c) new `revoked_keys` entry без соответствующего user-initiated revoke action, (d) `document_version` gap > expected (possibly attacker pushed rapid updates). Alerts surface через CLI `identity status` (check on boot) + log WARN event + optional push-notification hook для app integration. If anomaly detected: prompt user "rotate immediately?" с automation shortcut. **Implementation note**: stateless `detect_anomalies(current, last_known_good, user_authorized_state)` ships with 6 anomaly kinds (sig_key_idx→unknown, unauthorized key add, unauthorized revocation, version jump, seq regression, master_pubkey change). Scheduler + snapshot persistence + CLI glue land with 462.5/462.9 | **P2** | ~250 | `node/identity/watcher.rs` (new) | ✅ done (library layer) |

### Что НЕ входит — отдельные эпики backlog (остаётся app-layer или future)

1. **Shamir Secret Sharing для master_seed** — advanced backup (split N shares, recover M). Nice-to-have, не MVP.
2. **Hardware key integration** (YubiKey / TPM) для master storage. Follow-up.
3. **Multi-sig master** (threshold signature для rotation requires M-of-N pre-committed keys). Advanced resilience.
4. **Social recovery via guardians** — Signal-style social trust network. Explicitly rejected в этой версии (no external trust anchors).
5. **DNS-verified handles** (Tier 2) — explicitly rejected: no external dependencies.
6. **Anonymity transport layer** для IdentityDocument / InstanceRegistry publications (Tor routing). Opt-in future work.
7. **Group chat crypto (MLS)** — app-layer responsibility, independent RFC 9420 implementation or via `openmls` crate. Veil just transports.
8. **Identity migration proof** (signed "I am migrating from id_A to id_B" as user-initiated proof) — optional future feature для softer compromise recovery.
9. **App-layer read receipts / typing / presence CRDT** — messenger responsibility using 462.31 primitive + direct sessions.
10. **APN/FCM proxy integration** для real iOS/Android push — separate service, not veil core. Veil provides wake-hint trigger (462.35).
11. **Voice / video real-time streams** — Epic 406 существует; messenger может layer поверх, но не MVP.

### Порядок реализации

**Phase 1 — Foundation + Architectural decisions** (~3 weeks)
- 462.0 design doc
- **462.39 per-instance identity_sk model** (architectural revision — prereq для multiple других)
- 462.1 IdentityDocument proto (с bound_instance_id + freshness fields + timestamp-bound PoW)
- 462.2 crypto helpers
- 462.40 timestamp-bound PoW primitive
- 462.3 verifier (cache + freshness + revocation_seq checks)
- 462.36 RevocationCache (persistent) — начинается early для integration в verifier

**Phase 2 — Master key management + QR** (~3 weeks)
- 462.4 BIP39 paper backup (mandatory display, always)
- 462.4b encrypted file storage (optional addition, password + Argon2id)
- 462.26 QR identity sharing (public identity_id + pubkey для контактов)
- 462.27 QR cold master backup (encrypted, для disaster recovery)
- 462.30 **QR pairing ceremony** (главный mobile-login flow)
- 462.5 CLI create (integrates all: always BIP39, opt-in encrypted, QR display на выходе)
- 462.8 CLI restore (from BIP39 / encrypted file / QR backup / pairing QR)

**Phase 3 — Document lifecycle** (~2 weeks)
- 462.6 CLI rotate
- 462.7 CLI revoke
- 462.9 CLI show
- 462.22 revocation propagation

**Phase 4 — Multi-device + instances** (~2 weeks)
- 462.10 InstanceRegistry proto
- 462.11 instance_id state
- 462.12 publish/fetch

**Phase 5 — Names** (~1 week)
- 462.13 NameClaim V2
- 462.14 resolver

**Phase 6 — Wire refactor** (~3-4 weeks, biggest)
- 462.15 wire migration
- 462.16 handshake IdentityProof
- 462.17 session dedup
- 462.18 reputation
- 462.19 mailbox
- 462.20 dispatch с InstanceTag

**Phase 7 — Messenger-readiness + security hardening + cleanup** (~5-6 weeks)
- 462.28 device linking event
- 462.29 identity fingerprint helper
- 462.31 app-state primitive (encrypted synced DHT blob)
- 462.32 E2E ML-KEM cert binding **with per-instance fan-out**
- 462.37 **revocation gossip** через sessions (resilience; 462.36 уже в Phase 1)
- 462.38 owner freshness refresh automation
- 462.44 per-identity DHT write quota
- 462.45 **one-time pre-keys X3DH FS** (critical — retroactive decryption protection)
- 462.46 multi-replica identity resolution с quorum
- 462.47 optional extra entropy
- 462.48 self-document anomaly watcher
- 462.21 Tier B encrypted transports + encrypted mailbox_anchor
- 462.33 messenger dev guide
- 462.34 message backup primitive
- 462.35 mobile wake-up hint
- 462.24 identity docs + **opsec user guide**
- 462.25 old NameRecord removal

**Phase 8 — Validation** (~2 weeks)
- 462.23 integration test suite
- End-to-end scenarios
- Security review

### Success criteria

**Identity lifecycle**:
☐ User может `identity create` за < 5 сек (PoW + DHT publish)
☐ BIP39 phrase **всегда** показывается на `create` и user retype'ает 3 random words (confirms written down)
☐ User может `identity rotate` без потери name / reputation / sessions
☐ User может `identity revoke` + propagate к 10+ peers за < 10 сек
☐ Recovery из total device loss через BIP39 (mandatory) OR encrypted file (optional) OR QR cold backup

**Security (core)**:
☐ `master_seed` никогда не hit'ает disk в plaintext (even с encrypted file — zeroize-on-drop)
☐ `master_seed` **только на primary device** — других instance'ов не имеют master; pairing transfers сertified identity_sk, не master
☐ Encrypted master file readable только с правильным password (Argon2id + AEAD)
☐ Password для QR transfer / encrypted file НЕ encoded в сам QR / file (всегда out-of-band)
☐ Все domain-separated sig contexts проверены (не cross-usable)
☐ Pre-image resistance BLAKE3 binding formally documented в threat model
☐ Compromised identity_sk не может ротировать (нет master_sk), только DoS window

**Security (hardening — revision 2 additions)**:
☐ Per-instance identity_sk: compromise одного device → revoke только его subkey, остальные работают (blast radius containment)
☐ Timestamp-bound PoW: rainbow-table pre-computation невозможна (required hour ±2)
☐ QR pairing OOB confirmation code: attacker-as-fake-target заблокирован (source shows code, target must echo same code)
☐ Name homoglyph защита: ASCII-only whitelist rejects `alíce`, `аlice` (Cyrillic) и т.п.
☐ Per-identity DHT write quota: compromised key не может spam DHT с version++ updates (rate-limited к 10/hour)
☐ One-time pre-keys (X3DH): past messages decryptable NOT позже после sk compromise (prekey consumed, deleted)
☐ Multi-replica resolution с quorum: eclipse attack detectable (≥2 matching из ≥3 replicas)
☐ Self-document anomaly watcher: unauthorized update detected → user alerted within ~1 hour

**Revocation persistence (critical — предотвращает stale-revocation attack)**:
☐ `IdentityDocument.valid_until` ≤ issued_at + 30 days; consumer rejects stale docs
☐ `master_freshness_sig` обновляется owner'ом раз в ≤30 дней; attacker с identity_sk не может forge (не владеет master_sk)
☐ Local `RevocationCache` persists beyond DHT TTL + process restart; verifier consults cache first
☐ Consumer rejects IdentityDocument с `revocation_seq < cached_seq` для того же identity_id
☐ Gossip revocations exchange в каждом handshake — attacker'у нужно compromise ALL peers-seen-revocation чтобы suppress для new consumers
☐ Test scenario: owner offline 60 days, attacker republishes pre-revocation doc, new peer connecting 60d later → peer fetches via multiple replicas + asks directly connected peers (gossip) → at least one has revocation → attacker rejected
☐ Worst case: owner's master_freshness_sig expired → attacker's doc also expired → all clients reject; legitimate recovery требует master unlock

**Multi-device / LB**:
☐ 2 ноды под одним identity_id работают как LB (Any) и как multi-device (All)
☐ Каждая нода имеет **собственный** identity_sk; compromise one → revoke only that instance
☐ Device add/remove в InstanceRegistry propagate'ится < 6 hours (TTL)
☐ QR pairing ceremony: source → display QR + OOB code → mobile scan + user compares code → identity live < 90 сек
☐ pair_secret ephemeral (TTL ≤ 5 min, one-time-use)
☐ master_seed **never** transmitted через pair ceremony (target generates own sk)
☐ OOB confirmation code: mismatch aborts pairing с clear user message
☐ Device linking event достигает existing instances < 30 сек через direct push
☐ Any active identity_sk (любого instance) может update shared records (NameClaim, InstanceRegistry) — version monotonicity обеспечивает consistency

**Names**:
☐ `@alice` resolve к identity_id через DHT + cert chain verify offline
☐ Name squatting deterrence: rare names require ≥15 min PoW, long random ≤1 сек
☐ Name survives identity_sk rotation (сохраняется под identity_id)

**Messenger readiness**:
☐ E2E message к offline recipient работает через mailbox + ML-KEM (cert chain verified)
☐ **E2E fan-out**: sender encrypts к каждому active identity_key (per-instance); recipient instance decrypts только свою copy
☐ Multi-device shared mailbox: обе instance'а получают message, GC correct после ACK от всех
☐ **One-time pre-keys**: forward secrecy для async messages — compromise master_seed в будущем не декриптует past messages (prekeys consumed and destroyed)
☐ Safety number (fingerprint) определяется detrministically, pair-symmetric
☐ `store_app_state` / `load_app_state` работает для arbitrary app_id, auto-synced across instances
☐ Chat backup → restore: export snapshot → destroy local state → import → recover all data
☐ Mobile wake-up hint достигает sleeping instance, triggers mailbox pull on resume

**Operational**:
☐ 462.23 integration suite passes 100% в CI
☐ DHT records size ≤ `MAX_DHT_VALUE_BYTES = 4096` во всех realistic scenarios
☐ 462.33 messenger dev guide имеет working end-to-end example (text chat 2-device messenger в <200 LOC Python)

### Open blockers

- **None внутри эпика** — все core зависимости (Ed25519/Falcon crypto, DHT infrastructure, PoW, HKDF, ChaCha20-Poly1305, ML-KEM) уже в veil
- External crates для добавления в `veilcore/Cargo.toml`:
  - `bip39 = "2"` — 24-word phrase encoding (Phase 2)
  - `argon2 = "0.5"` — memory-hard KDF для encrypted master storage (Phase 2)
  - `qr2term = "0.3"` или `qrcode = "0.14"` — QR rendering (Phase 2)
  - `image = "0.25"` — QR-scanning из image files (Phase 2, optional)
  - `sled = "0.34"` — persistent KV для RevocationCache (462.36)
  - Все `no_std`-compatible и audited

---

### Phase 6.48 — Epic 462 closeout note (2026-05-05)

**Status:** ✅ implementation closed.  Все 50 task-rows закрыты:
47 ✅ done + 3 ⊘ merged (462.41→462.30, 462.42→462.21, 462.43→462.13).
External crates (bip39, argon2, chacha20poly1305, qrcode) уже в Cargo.toml.

**Acceptance-checklist обновлён**: ряд ☐ items устарели после Epic 473/476:

**Stale (mailbox-dependent, Epic 473 dropped mailbox subsystem entirely):**
- ~~"E2E message к offline recipient через mailbox + ML-KEM"~~ — async-delivery вынесен из network layer; будет в отдельном `veil-mailbox` crate
- ~~"Multi-device shared mailbox: GC after all-ACK"~~ — mailbox dropped
- ~~"Mobile wake-up hint triggers mailbox pull on resume"~~ — mailbox dropped

**Stale (revocation-dependent, Epic 476.S1a/b dropped revocation cache + gossip + freshness-cert):**
- ~~"`master_freshness_sig` обновляется owner'ом раз в ≤30 дней"~~ — `master_freshness_sig` field удалён из IdentityDocument
- ~~"Local `RevocationCache` persists beyond DHT TTL"~~ — `revocation_cache.rs` удалён (714 LoC)
- ~~"Consumer rejects IdentityDocument с `revocation_seq < cached_seq`"~~ — `revocation_seq` field удалён
- ~~"Gossip revocations exchange в каждом handshake"~~ — `revocation_gossip.rs` удалён (625 LoC)
- ~~"Self-document anomaly watcher"~~ — `node/identity/watcher.rs` удалён (550 LoC)
- ~~"Test scenario: owner offline 60 days, attacker republishes pre-revocation doc"~~ — revocation gone
- ~~"Worst case: owner's master_freshness_sig expired"~~ — freshness gone
- ~~"`IdentityDocument.valid_until` ≤ issued_at + 30 days; consumer rejects stale docs"~~ — strict revocation removed

Заменено на упрощённую модель: short-lived identity_keys (≤7d validity), auto-reissue от master.

**Still relevant (covered by `crates/veil-identity/src/integration_tests.rs` — 12 scenarios):**
- ✅ Timestamp-bound PoW
- ✅ QR pairing OOB confirmation code
- ✅ Name homoglyph защита (ASCII whitelist)
- ✅ One-time pre-keys (X3DH) — covered by `prekey_bundle.rs`
- ✅ Multi-replica resolution с quorum (Epic 491)
- ✅ 2 ноды под одним identity_id (LB + multi-device)
- ✅ Per-instance identity_sk; revoke только compromised instance
- ✅ QR pair ceremony end-to-end (`scenario_full_pair_ceremony_end_to_end`)
- ✅ pair_secret ephemeral (TTL ≤ 5 min)
- ✅ master_seed never transmitted через pair
- ✅ OOB confirmation mismatch aborts (`scenario_safety_numbers_pair_symmetric`)
- ✅ Any active identity_sk updates shared records (version monotonicity)
- ✅ `@alice` resolve через DHT + cert chain (`scenario_qr_contact_import_then_full_cert_chain_validate`)
- ✅ Name survives identity_sk rotation
- ✅ E2E fan-out — sender encrypts к каждому active identity_key (`mlkem_fanout.rs`)
- ✅ Safety number deterministic + pair-symmetric

**Outstanding (not yet integration-tested, defer to consumer-driven phase):**
- store_app_state / load_app_state auto-synced across instances — нет dedicated integration scenario, но primitives (NameClaim/InstanceRegistry write APIs) shipped
- Chat backup → restore — out-of-scope для network layer; nf separate `veil-backup` crate
- 462.33 messenger dev guide — будет sealed после mailbox crate появится

**Производственно**: identity-слой Epic 462 ready.  Open items требуют consumer (messenger app) для validation, не дополнительной работы в veil.
