## Epic 479 — Latency-aware end-to-end routing — closed (absorbed by 137/142/144)

> ✅ **Закрытие 2026-05-05:** premise эпика устарел.  Tasks 479.1-479.3
> были фактически реализованы параллельно через другие эпики, ещё до
> того как 479 был запланирован.  Verified в коде:
>
> | # | Status | Где реально реализовано |
> |---|--------|--------------------------|
> | 479.1 multi-path probing | ✅ done | **Epic 142** Multipath delivery — ECMP + redundant send + flow pinning (`delivery.rs:374` "Epic 136/137: weighted random hop selection") |
> | 479.2 end-to-end RTT samples | ✅ done иначе | **Epic 137/143** ROUTE_PROBE собирает RTT в `RttTable`, который feed'ится в `effective_score` (`delivery.rs:367-370`).  Wire-protocol-level timestamps в Forward+ACK — отвергнуто как лишнее: probe-based RTT уже работает |
> | 479.3 RouteCache by latency_class | ✅ done иначе | **Epic 144** QoS classes (RT/IN/BK/BG) реализованы как per-request score modifiers, не отдельные кэши: `is_realtime` 2× jitter weight, `is_bulk_or_bg` narrow-bw penalty (`delivery.rs:439-447`).  Acceptance-test goal "interactive picks low-latency 2-hop, bulk picks 1-hop" достигается тем же скорингом |
> | 479.4 latency-spike failover | ✅ soft-mitigated | **Epic 137** RTT EWMA автоматически penalizes spikes — high-RTT paths валятся в score без explicit RouteWithdraw.  Soft-mitigation медленнее (один EWMA tick vs sub-second withdraw) но проще, и hard trigger не блокирует production |
> | 479.5 5-node sim scenario | ⬜ deferred (verification only) | Useful as а regression test, не блокирует функциональность.  Existing routing scoring tests covers сама logic; sim-scenario был бы integration test, не unit |
>
> Premise "Vivaldi сейчас ранжирует только в FIND_NODE" был **неверен**:
> Vivaldi distance используется в data-path delivery как fallback когда
> RTT неизвестен (`delivery.rs:423-424`: `lv.distance_estimate(rv)`).
> Это пришло из Epic 142 ECMP scoring и Epic 218.10 cap-flag filter.

**Цель:** Vivaldi сейчас ранжирует только в FIND_NODE (топология DHT-walk-а). Нужно расширить на **data-path forward** — выбор маршрута до получателя по реальной end-to-end latency, не только по XOR-distance.

| # | Задача | Файлы | Статус |
|---|---|---|---|
| 479.1 | Multi-path probing: при наличии ≥ 2 next-hop кандидатов до target — параллельный probe (small ping), pick lowest-latency. | node/routing/* + node/dispatcher/control.rs | ✅ done — Epic 142 |
| 479.2 | End-to-end RTT samples через forward path — каждый Forward-frame несёт `sent_at_unix_ms`, ACK содержит `received_at_unix_ms` + `processed_at_unix_ms`. | proto/delivery.rs + node/dispatcher/delivery.rs | ✅ done иначе — Epic 137/143 ROUTE_PROBE |
| 479.3 | RouteCache теперь ключ-по-`(target, latency_class)` — bulk-traffic vs interactive разные пути. | node/routing/cache.rs | ✅ done иначе — Epic 144 QoS modifiers |
| 479.4 | Failover triggered by latency-spike (текущий маршрут вырос > 2× базы) — RouteWithdraw + repick. | node/dispatcher/control.rs | ✅ soft-mitigated — Epic 137 RTT EWMA |
| 479.5 | Sim-scenario: 5-узловая сеть с разной latency между парами — interactive traffic выбирает путь с min latency несмотря на больший hop-count. | sim/scenarios/* | ⬜ deferred — verification only |

**Acceptance:** при наличии 2 путей (1-hop 200ms vs 2-hop 50ms+50ms=100ms) interactive traffic выбирает 2-hop; bulk traffic выбирает 1-hop (меньше overhead-а). Failover при latency-spike < 500ms.

## Epic 481 — Out-of-band bootstrap — closed (3 ✅ done + 2 sliced ship; 2 sub-pieces deferred)

> ✅ **Закрытие 2026-05-05:** 5 acceptance items shipped напрямую или
> absorbed:
>
> - 481.1 QR-invite ✅
> - 481.2 Password-URL ✅
> - 481.3 Friend-to-friend: **CLI signed-invite ✅** (operator publishes signed URL, recipient verifies); in-band wire-frame deferred (см. ниже)
> - 481.4 Multi-source seeds: **HTTPS ✅** + **signed-bundle ✅ via Epic 492**; `.onion` deferred (см. ниже)
> - 481.5 Cache ✅ (per-node disk cache; DHT-record publish/fetch не нужен — ноды без bootstrap до DHT не доходят)
>
> Acceptance ("новый user входит за < 1 мин через QR / HTTPS даже если
> публичный seed-IP заблокирован") **достигнут** через 1+2+4+5.
>
> ## Deferred sub-pieces (large-scope, parked)
>
> | # | Что | Почему deferred | Re-open trigger |
> |---|------|----------------|------------------|
> | 481.3-inband | New wire-frame `IntroduceRequest{introducer, sponsoree, expiry, sig}` для in-band peer vouching через PEX | ~200-300 LoC новой инфраструктуры (proto + dispatcher + sig verify + rate-limit + composability with PEX trust) с маргинальным ROI: bootstrap-проблема уже решена 5 другими механизмами; transitive-trust signal не даёт нового value поверх post-handshake trust model.  YAGNI до конкретного use-case | Появится сценарий, который не покрывается existing 5 layers (например, mass-onboarding флешмоба без операторского URL) |
> | 481.4-onion | `.onion` seed-source через Tor SOCKS proxy | Требует Tor integration — external dep ~1000+ LoC OR external `torsocks`-style transparent proxy.  Censorship coverage уже сильная без `.onion` (HTTPS на CDN + signed bundle на DHT + cache); `.onion` добавляет defense-in-depth для high-paranoia scenarios но не закрывает уникальный gap | Operator deployment решит "нужен Tor для конкретной угрозы" |

**Цель:** Censor блокирует hardcoded seed-list. Новый user должен войти в сеть через канал, который censor не контролирует: QR от друга, password-protected URL, бумажная записка.

| # | Задача | Файлы | Статус |
|---|---|---|---|
| 481.1 | QR-code invite — содержит `(node_id, transport_uri, signed_introduction)` + опц. PoW-challenge для anti-spam. CLI: `veil invite --qr`. | cmd/invite.rs (new) + identity/sovereign.rs (existing pair-* infra) | ✅ done — новый модуль `node/bootstrap/invite.rs` с canonical URL `veil:bootstrap?pk=<b64>&t=<endpoint>&a=<algo>&nc=<b64>[&tls_cert=<b64>][&tls_ca_cert=<b64>]`. Field set 1-к-1 матчит `cfg::BootstrapPeer` (transport, public_key, nonce, algo, optional TLS), так что `decode_uri()` напрямую appends в `[[bootstrap_peers]]`. **No new wire format / no signature** на самом invite — out-of-band channel (QR scan / paper note / signed text) считается uncensored, pubkey из URL верифицируется handshake-ом дальше; replay не угроза т.к. `bootstrap_peers` discarded после initial FIND_NODE. Canonical encode-order гарантирует stable QR caching; decode принимает arbitrary order, case-insensitive scheme prefix. New CLI surface: `veil-cli bootstrap {invite [--qr] \| join --uri <...> \| decode --uri <...>}`. `invite` берёт первый `[[listen]]` entry (preferring `advertise` если задан) + `[identity]` keypair → URL [+ ASCII halfblock QR via shared qrcode-crate path]. `join` идемпотентно appends в config (skip если pk уже есть). `decode` — preflight без mutation. Limits: `MAX_BOOTSTRAP_URI_BYTES = 4 KiB` (Falcon-512 + TLS chain fits). 13 unit тестов (round-trip minimal + with_tls / arbitrary field order / case-insensitive scheme / bad scheme / missing pk / dup field / unknown field / oversized / endpoint reserved chars / unknown algo / canonical encoding stable / handler-layer encode-decode) + 1 helper test. Build clean. |
| 481.2 | Password-protected pairing URL — `veil://pair/<base64>?p=<password-hash>`, recipient вводит password, decode invite. | cmd/invite.rs | ✅ done — новый модуль `node/bootstrap/encrypted_invite.rs` оборачивает существующий 481.1 invite в Argon2id + ChaCha20-Poly1305 envelope. **Censorship-resistance pattern:** оператор постит URL на large public channel (forum, paste, social media — easy to censor), пароль шарит на small private channel (Telegram, in-person, Signal — hard to censor at scale). Censor видит URL но не может decrypt без password. **Wire format:** `veil:pair?b=<base64url>` где body = magic("EI") + version + kdf-id + Argon2id-params(m_cost_kib, t_cost, p_cost) + salt(16B) + nonce(12B) + ciphertext+tag. KDF params ship in-band → future tightening (e.g. m_cost = 64MiB) loads older URLs transparently. **Defaults:** m_cost = 32 MiB / 2 passes / 1 par (interactive-friendly даже на бюджетном телефоне, ≈ 200-400 ms на modern hardware, raises offline brute-force to ≈ 10⁹ attempts/$ для moderately complex password). **Anti-downgrade:** decoder rejects KDF params below MIN_M_COST_KIB(8MiB)/MIN_T_COST(1)/MIN_P_COST(1) — censor can't re-publish URL с m_cost=1 и brute-force на laptop. **AAD = "veil.pair.v1"** binds ciphertext to purpose. **Salt + nonce per encryption are random** so two postings of the same invite produce distinct URLs (observer cannot correlate "это тот же invite reissued" without decrypting). **Threat model docs** are explicit что НЕ defend: weak passwords, compromise of password channel itself, replay (inner URL is static — issuer needs one-shot semantics → rotate `[identity]` keypair after redemption). **CLI integration:** existing `veil-cli bootstrap invite/join/decode` extended с `--password <PW>` flag — без пароля emit/decode plain `veil:bootstrap?…` (Epic 481.1 path), с паролем emit/decode `veil:pair?…`. Dispatcher `decode_either_uri` routes encrypted vs plain based on URL scheme prefix; **rejects `--password` on plain URI loudly** (operator likely pasted wrong URL from clipboard, fail-loud beats silently joining wrong network). 11 unit tests cover roundtrip + TLS-fields preserved + wrong-password (Aead error indistinguishable from tamper, no oracle) + tampered-ciphertext + bad-scheme + malformed-base64 + KDF-downgrade-rejection + truncated-body-no-panic + size-cap + distinct-ciphertexts; 4 CLI dispatcher tests cover encrypted-routes-correctly + missing-password-error + plain-with-password-rejected + plain-without-password-works. Build clean. |
| 481.3 | Friend-to-friend gossip: уже подключённый peer может выдать одноразовый bootstrap-token (signed) для своего друга — friend подключается через peer-introducer-а. | proto/discovery.rs + node/dispatcher/discovery.rs | 🔄 partial — **CLI signed-invite slice done** (the highest-impact zero-rework piece; network-layer in-band token issuance + transitive trust via PEX deferred — both require new wire frames which carry rework risk and the CLI-only slice already covers the operator's primary use case). New `node/bootstrap/signed_invite.rs` complements Epic 481.2 encrypted-invite: 481.2 protects channel (password = shared secret per recipient — doesn't scale), 481.3 attests issuer (signature verifies single-issuer-many-recipients — operator signs ONCE, posts publicly, anyone with issuer pubkey verifies). **Wire format:** `veil:signed-invite?b=<base64url>` body = magic("SI") + version + algo + issuer_pk + issued_at_unix(u64) + expiry_unix(u64) + signature + inner_uri. Inner URI = existing 481.1 `veil:bootstrap?…` so signed-invite is a thin wrapper, not a redesign. **Canonical signed message** = `"veil-signed-invite:v1\n" + inner_uri + "\n" + issued_at + "\n" + expiry` — domain-separated (prevents cross-protocol signature reuse — an identity_proof sig from elsewhere can't be repurposed), pins validity window in the signed payload (attacker who captures envelope cannot extend lifetime). **Anti-tamper:** `tampered_signature_rejected`, `tampered_inner_uri_rejected`, `tampered_validity_window_rejected` tests verify each field is signature-protected. **Validity cap:** MAX_VALIDITY_SECS = 1 year (operator typo `--expiry-secs 10` getting 10 years is impossible). **MAX_SIGNED_INVITE_BYTES = 8 KiB** (Falcon-512 envelope sanity ceiling — Ed25519 typical ≈ 600 B). **Algos:** Ed25519 + Falcon-512, matches existing `SignatureAlgorithm` enum. CLI surface extends 481.1: `bootstrap invite --sign [--expiry-secs N]` signs with local `[identity]` keypair (1h default validity); `bootstrap join --uri T --verify-issuer <PK>` (REQUIRED for signed URIs — without expected issuer, accepting an envelope is meaningless because the envelope's claimed issuer is attacker-controlled); `bootstrap decode --uri T [--verify-issuer <PK>]` (preflight; without `--verify-issuer` shows `UNVERIFIED — claimed issuer: <PK>` block so operator compares against OOB-known pubkey before trusting). **Mutual exclusion:** `--sign + --password`, `signed URI + --password`, `encrypted URI + --verify-issuer`, `plain URI + --verify-issuer` all rejected loudly (not silently accepted) — operator likely pasted wrong URL from clipboard, fail-loud beats joining wrong network. 13 module unit tests cover sign/verify roundtrip + verify-without-expected-issuer (internal-consistency mode) + expired/wrong-issuer/tampered-sig/tampered-inner-uri/tampered-window/inverted-window/validity-too-long/bad-scheme/oversized-URL rejections + canonical-message-domain-separator. 6 dispatcher tests cover all CLI flag-combination paths. **Composition with 481.2:** since both are wrappers around `BootstrapPeer`, an operator that needs BOTH attestation AND channel encryption can `sign(encrypt(invite, pw), sk)` manually as two-pass — defaults are mutually exclusive at the single-flag layer to keep CLI surface obvious. |
| 481.4 | Multi-source seed-list: config принимает `[bootstrap]` секцию с DNS-name (resolves to IP пула), HTTPS URL (returns signed seed-list), `.onion` (через Tor если установлен). | cfg/model.rs + node/bootstrap/* | 🔄 → ✅ done (mostly) — **HTTPS slice done** (this entry); **signed seed-list ✅ absorbed by Epic 492** (`crates/veil-bootstrap/src/signed_bundle.rs` — sign/decode/verify implemented, sim-tested cross-node distribution). Только `.onion` deferred (Tor integration scope). Original entry text: New `node/bootstrap/https.rs`: hand-rolled HTTP/1.1 GET over the existing TLS stack — **no new deps**, reuses `connect_tls_client_stream` (rustls default OR `tls-boring` Chrome fingerprint under feature flag) so the bootstrap fetch carries the same JA3 as veil traffic — DPI sees ordinary HTTPS, not a fingerprintable rust-HTTP-client pattern. **Wire format reused:** response body parsed via existing `decode_bootstrap_bundle` (Epic 455.4 JSON shape) → operator can serve the same file from HTTPS, DHT publish, and local config. **Hard caps:** `MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES = 64 KiB`, `MAX_TOTAL_BYTES = 72 KiB` (defends against adversarial CDN returning multi-MB blob), `DEFAULT_FETCH_TIMEOUT = 10 s` (best-effort — fall through to other layers, never block startup). **HTTP minimal-by-design:** GET only, `Connection: close`, no chunked encoding, no compression, no redirects, no cookies, no auth — operators that need any of those host a redirect-free static endpoint. **`http://` rejected** at parse time (cert chain is the operator's authentication that the response is theirs, not a censor-injected forgery). **`user@host` userinfo rejected** as phishing footgun. New `cfg::GlobalConfig::bootstrap_https_urls: Vec<String>` (default empty; multiple URLs allowed for redundancy across CDNs). Wired into `spawn_bootstrap_task`: fires UNCONDITIONALLY when any URL is configured (operator may have stale `bootstrap_peers` AND fresh HTTPS endpoint), each URL fetched concurrently, results dedup'd by pubkey, registered + dialed via the same outbound-connector path the DNS layer uses (peer_id namespace `0x8800_0000` — between DNS at `0x8000_0000` and synthetic/gateway range `≥ 0xC000_0000`, so the discovered-peer cache from Epic 481.5 properly skips ephemeral bootstrap peers). **Censorship-resistance value:** operator hot-rotates seed-IPs by editing one JSON file on a CDN — no binary rebuild, no DNS TTL to wait out. Censor would need to (a) block the operator's HTTPS hostname (CDN-fronting + multiple hostnames defeats this), (b) block the entire CDN (high collateral damage — Cloudflare/CloudFront/Fastly host millions of legit sites), or (c) compromise the cert authority (out-of-band, expensive). **Diag updated:** `node bootstrap-status` now shows 5 layers (added L3 HTTPS bootstrap URLs, with `curl -v <url>` verification hint), verdict criteria recomputed against `total_layers = 5`. 17 unit tests cover URL parsing (all rejection paths), HTTP response parsing (status/body extraction, non-200, missing-separator, bad proto, truncated stream no-panic), end-to-end via `tokio::io::duplex` fixture (request shape verified, response decoded into BootstrapPeer list, 503 propagates as BadStatus, oversized response triggers TooLarge), bundle-format compat with layer-2/3. Build clean. **Hardening (post-ship dedup fix):** new `filter_already_known(seeds, known_pubkeys)` helper prevents the HTTPS task from registering peers that ALREADY appear in `[[bootstrap_peers]]`, `[[peers]]`, or the discovered-peer cache — fixes a regression where peer X listed in BOTH operator config AND HTTPS bundle (or BOTH cache AND HTTPS bundle) was getting two PeerConfigEntry registrations and double-dialed. Real-world impact: an operator who hosts the same seed list at two CDN endpoints, or pins a friend in `bootstrap_peers` while also fetching them from HTTPS, was burning double the battery + creating twice the DPI-visible handshake traffic per startup. Snapshot of known pubkeys is taken SYNCHRONOUSLY before spawning the HTTPS task (no race vs concurrent reloads / cache upserts). When dedup drops > 0 entries, an `bootstrap.https.dedup` info log surfaces the count for operator visibility. 5 new unit tests cover: drops pubkey already in bootstrap_peers, drops pubkey already in cache, keeps all when known set is empty, handles empty input, drops all when every pubkey known (pathological case). |
| 481.5 | Seed-list как DHT record — после первого успешного входа узел кэширует расширенный seed-list, использует на следующих входах. Resilience против takedown of original seeds. | node/dht/* | ✅ done — scoped to **discovered-peer cache** as the highest-impact, lowest-rework slice (DHT-record publish/fetch deferred — ноды без bootstrap'а вообще не дойдут до DHT, поэтому per-node disk cache беспалый ROI vs DHT-side wiring). Новый `node/bootstrap/cache.rs` с `DiscoveredPeerCache` (HashMap, keyed by pubkey, LRU eviction at `MAX_DISCOVERED_PEERS = 32`, atomic-write JSON envelope `{version: u32, peers: [...]}`). Fields persisted = wire-compatible `BootstrapPeer` + `last_seen_unix`. Wired across runtime: (a) new `cfg::GlobalConfig::discovered_peers_cache_path: Option<String>` knob; (b) `NodeRuntime` and `NodeServices` hold `Arc<Mutex<DiscoveredPeerCache>>`, init at startup from configured path (or in-memory variant если path == None); (c) `outbound_connector.rs` upserts cache after every successful handshake-complete (skipping synthetic peer_ids `≥ 0xC000_0000` so gateway-bridge ephemeral peers не загрязняют cache); (d) `service_tasks.rs::spawn_bootstrap_task` splices cached snapshot as 4-я ступенька fallback chain — после `config.bootstrap_peers` / `builtin_seeds()` / DNS, перед "give up"; (e) maintenance loop calls periodic `tick_save_discovered_peers_cache()`; (f) `do_stop_flushes` calls final flush so даже crash между ticks не теряет свежие entries. 9 unit tests (empty load / upsert+snapshot / same-pubkey-refresh / freshest-first ordering / LRU at cap / save-load round-trip / corrupt-file → empty cache / in-memory save no-op / disk-format envelope shape) — все проходят. Build clean. **Censorship resistance:** authoritarian-state censor can take down operator seed-IPs (1), poison shipped binary's `builtin_seeds()` (2), blacklist DNS domain (3) and node still bootstraps from layer (4) — peers it has personally proved reachable. Censor would need per-user state (every IP this user has ever talked to) plus simultaneous block of all of them — dramatically more expensive than blocking a published seed list. |

**Acceptance:** новый user, у которого пара/группа друзей уже в сети, входит за < 1 минуты через сканирование QR; новый user без друзей-в-сети входит через bootstrap URL (HTTPS) даже если публичный seed-IP заблокирован.

## Epic 482 — Optional anonymity (Tor-like circuits) — closed (functionally complete + integration-tested)

> ✅ **Закрытие 2026-05-05:** anonymity stack functionally closed
> end-to-end + 2 integration sim tests + drop_subscriber wired +
> AS-diversity picker shipped (commit `8ae42a7`).  7 sub-tasks earlier
> shipped как primitives/slices; integration tests confirm полный
> pipeline работает на real wire.
>
> Real deferred items (parked, не stale):
> - **Anti-loop TTL field** — wire-format break за маржинальный win;
>   existing PER_HOP_OVERHEAD=92 даёт natural payload shrinkage per
>   loop iteration → max ~5-6 self-loops before unusable.
> - **Stateful CircuitId** — persistent circuits ~1500 LoC scope;
>   defer until perf-driven need.
> - **Path-product latency optimization** — pairwise RTT discovery; own slice.
> - **Bandwidth-quota tracking + claim verification + reputation** —
>   abuse-prevention follow-ups.

**Цель:** Опциональный режим: send_anonymous() строит 2-3 hop circuit, sender / receiver IP взаимно неизвестны, observer не может корреллировать по timing/size.

| # | Задача | Файлы | Статус |
|---|---|---|---|
| 482.1 | Circuit abstraction: `CircuitId`, per-hop layer encryption (X25519 ECDH с relay-pubkey, AEAD layer). | node/anonymity/circuit.rs (new) | 🔄 partial — **two primitives shipped (single-hop AEAD + multi-hop circuit envelope); CircuitId for stateful circuits, return-path, relay-directory lookup deferred**. **(a) `node/anonymity/onion.rs`** — single-hop AEAD primitive (described below). **(b) `node/anonymity/circuit.rs`** — multi-hop circuit envelope on top of `onion`. Each layer's plaintext is `[32B next_hop_id][inner]` where `next_hop_id` is the destination of the next forward (or `FINAL_HOP_SENTINEL = [0u8; 32]` meaning "I am the destination, what follows is the payload"). All-zeros sentinel is safe because real `node_id`s are BLAKE3(pubkey) → all-zeros pre-image is cryptographically unreachable. **API:** `build_circuit(payload, hops: &[Hop]) -> Vec<u8>` (sender side; wraps in REVERSE hop order — innermost hop first), `peel_circuit(envelope, my_sk) -> Result<PeelResult>` (relay side; `PeelResult::Forward { next_hop, inner }` for intermediate hops or `PeelResult::Final { payload }` for destination). **`PER_HOP_OVERHEAD` = 92 B** (32 next-hop-id + 60 onion). For 510 B cell + N hops, max user payload = `510 - 92*N` (sentinel is internal overhead, not user-visible). N=1 → 418 B, N=2 → 326 B, N=3 → 234 B, N=5 → 50 B, N=6 rejected at build time. **(c) `node/anonymity/packet.rs`** — user-facing API combining cell + circuit + onion. `build_anonymous_cell(payload, hops) -> Result<[u8; CELL_SIZE]>` and `peel_anonymous_cell(cell, my_sk) -> Result<CellPeelResult>` where `CellPeelResult::Forward { next_hop, outbound_cell }` already carries a 512-byte cell — relay forwards as-is, no further packing. Re-packing through `cell::pack` after each peel is the LOAD-BEARING anonymity property: without it, observer sees cell sizes shrink monotonically along the circuit (512 → 420 → 328 → 236 ...) and can immediately infer hop position, deanonymizing. 11 packet tests including `epic482_1_observer_sees_uniform_cell_sizes_through_all_hops` (collects byte lengths of every transmission in a 3-hop circuit, asserts all == 512) and `epic482_1_observer_cannot_correlate_two_sends_of_same_payload` (same payload + same hops sent twice → distinct cells, fresh ephemeral keys). **Anonymity property** verified end-to-end by `epic482_1_intermediate_hop_cannot_see_payload_or_final_destination`: hop2 in a 4-hop circuit sees neither the payload bytes nor hop4's node_id in its plaintext (only hop3's node_id + hop3's still-encrypted onion). **Tight scope (deferred to follow-ups):** NO `CircuitId` for stateful circuits (this module ships stateless single-message circuits — one onion = one message; persistent circuits need a separate session-keyed mode), NO return-path / reply-path encoding (rendezvous-style return-onion is Epic 482.5 or a one-shot `replyable: true` flag), NO padding to fixed size at circuit layer (cell layer provides that), NO relay-directory lookup (caller supplies hop tuples; DHT-based relay discovery is Epic 482.4). 8 circuit unit tests cover: build rejects zero hops, single-hop yields Final at the only hop (degenerate base case useful as building block), 3-hop walks through all hops correctly, intermediate hop cannot see payload or final destination (THE anonymity property), per-hop overhead constant matches components (92 B), envelope size grows by exactly PER_HOP_OVERHEAD per added hop, wrong hop sk at intermediate fails loudly with AEAD error (no silent forwarding-decision leak), empty payload through 1 hop works (circuit liveness ping). Combined with **(a) `node/anonymity/onion.rs`** primitive: single-hop X25519 ECDH + ChaCha20-Poly1305 + BLAKE3-derived AEAD key, wire format `[32B ephemeral_pk][12B nonce][ciphertext + 16B tag]` = 60 B overhead, ephemeral_pk + nonce bound into AEAD AAD with `"veil-onion-v1\0"` domain prefix (anti-tamper + anti-cross-protocol-replay), fresh ephemeral keypair per call (forward secrecy), 12 onion unit tests covering roundtrip / all four tamper modes / multi-hop composition / hop-cannot-skip-layers negative test. New `node/anonymity/onion.rs`: single-hop X25519 ECDH + ChaCha20-Poly1305 + BLAKE3-derived AEAD key. **Wire format per layer:** `[32B ephemeral_pk][12B nonce][ciphertext + 16B tag]` = 60 B overhead. **Anti-tamper:** ephemeral_pk + nonce bound into AEAD AAD with `"veil-onion-v1\0"` domain prefix → tampering ANY field (ephemeral_pk, nonce, ciphertext) fails AEAD verification (defense in depth on top of AEAD's natural integrity). Domain prefix prevents cross-protocol signature reuse. **Forward secrecy:** fresh ephemeral keypair per call, secret half consumed on diffie_hellman, never stored. **API:** `wrap_for_hop(payload, hop_pk_bytes) -> Vec<u8>` + `unwrap_at_hop(envelope, hop_sk) -> Result<Vec<u8>>`. **Multi-hop composition** is `wrap → wrap → wrap` in REVERSE hop order; demonstrated by `epic482_1_layered_three_hop_composition` test. Each hop unwraps ONE layer, sees only the next layer's bytes, cannot decrypt past — verified by `epic482_1_layered_hop_cannot_skip_to_inner_layer` negative test. **Composes with cell (Epic 482.2):** for N-hop circuit fitting in 510 B cell, max innermost payload = `510 - 60 * N` (N=3 → 330 B). **Tight scope (deferred):** (a) NO multi-hop wire format — circuit module owns hop ordering, next-hop addressing, return-path. (b) NO padding — output is `len(payload) + 60`; cell layer provides fixed-size envelope. (c) NO replay protection — circuit owner does sequence numbers + freshness. **CircuitId, hop directory, circuit construction, send_anonymous() API** all still ⬜ todo — but the cryptographic floor is now stable + tested. 12 unit tests cover roundtrip (small / empty), envelope-size invariant, wrong-hop-sk / tampered-ciphertext / tampered-ephemeral_pk / tampered-nonce all fail AEAD, too-small envelope pre-AEAD rejection, two-wraps-of-same-payload yield distinct envelopes (anti-correlation), 3-hop layered composition correctness, layered hop cannot skip to inner layer (the load-bearing onion property). |
| 482.2 | Fixed cell size (512 байт) с padding — sender отправляет cell-of-cells, observer видит только uniform 512-byte chunks. | node/anonymity/cell.rs (new) | 🔄 partial — **primitive shipped, wire integration deferred to circuit work (Epic 482.1)**. New `node::anonymity::cell` module (first occupant of new `node/anonymity/` directory). **Wire format:** exactly 512 bytes per cell — `[u16 BE payload_len][payload bytes][zero padding to 512]`. **Censorship-resistance value:** observer sees a stream of identical-size chunks regardless of actual message size; cannot infer message length, cannot tell two cells belong to the same logical message (when paired with circuit layering from 482.1). Combined with layered AEAD (also 482.1), observer also can't tell sender / final destination. **API:** `pack(payload) -> Result<[u8; 512]>` + `unpack(cell) -> Result<Vec<u8>>`, `MAX_PAYLOAD_PER_CELL = 510`. **Anti-tamper:** `unpack` validates EVERY padding byte is 0x00 (full walk, not short-circuit — a short-circuit would technically leak a one-byte position oracle though at this layer cells are AEAD-authenticated by the caller so practical impact is nil; the full walk costs 512 byte compares per cell which is negligible vs the AEAD verify). Catches bit-rot (caller forgot AEAD-wrap), malleability attempts, and pack-implementation bugs in the network. **Tight scope (deferred to follow-ups, not this commit):** (a) NO multi-cell messages — caller must fit ≤ 510 B; multi-cell sequencing belongs in a higher layer that owns sequence numbers + reassembly. (b) NO encryption at this layer — caller wraps cells in AEAD before the wire (separation keeps the primitive testable without crypto fixtures and lets the circuit owner pick its own AEAD shape: single-key vs onion). (c) NO timing controls — cell-rate shaping (constant-rate emit vs natural traffic) belongs in dispatcher integration. **Why ship the primitive standalone:** zero-rework foundational piece that every Epic 482.x sub-piece will depend on; getting the wire format settled now means later integration work is just plumbing, not redesign. 10 unit tests cover: roundtrip (small / empty / max-size), pack rejects oversize, unpack rejects wrong-size input + declared-len overflow + nonzero padding (including first-padding-byte edge case for off-by-one), distinct-payloads-distinct-cells sanity, payload-size-does-not-leak (1-byte vs 510-byte both produce 512-byte cells). |
| 482.3 | Relay role: opt-in, узел декларирует «я готов быть relay» в `CapabilitiesPayload.flags`. Bandwidth-quota separate от обычного трафика. | proto/session.rs + node/abuse/* | 🔄 partial — **declaration shipped, separate bandwidth-quota deferred to follow-up**. Новый bit `cap_flags::ANONYMITY_RELAY = 1 << 2` в `CapabilitiesPayload.flags` — distinct from existing `CAN_RELAY` (= forwarding arbitrary frames для indirect-delivery), потому что anonymity-relay карьер несёт другие costs (constant-rate cells, anti-correlation timing requirements, extra bandwidth budget) и требует explicit opt-in. Новая config-секция `[anonymity]` с `relay_capable: bool` (default false — anonymity relay не должен включаться по умолчанию). Поле кэшируется на `NodeRuntime`, `NodeServices`, и `SessionRuntimeContext` (snapshot at startup, propagated через 3 каркасных типа), читается во время handshake `complete_handshake_from_capabilities` и устанавливает bit рядом с существующим `SUPPORTS_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITY`. **Wire-format change** (без backward-compat per user permission): функция `perform_ovl1_handshake` получила 13-й аргумент `anonymity_relay_capable: bool`. Релейная directory layer (Epic 482.4) будет фильтровать circuit-кандидатов по этому биту — operator который не opt-in'ил никогда не будет выбран как hop. **Tight scope (deferred to follow-ups, отдельный slice):** separate bandwidth-quota для anonymity traffic vs обычного traffic (нужна tracking infra на per-session level — добавляет rework risk на текущей итерации); cell-rate shaping (constant-rate emit) — это уже dispatcher integration (Epic 482.x main); per-session counter "сколько circuits я forward'ю" — abuse-prevention follow-up. **Тесты:** 3 end-to-end handshake теста через `tokio::io::duplex` exercise wire format в обе стороны: bit OFF by default (most common case — operator не opt-in'ил), bit ON when opted in by both peers (Epic 482.4 будет matching), asymmetric case (A opted in, B did not — каждая сторона видит TRUTH peer'а, no information leak across handshake). |
| 482.4 | Relay directory — DHT-stored signed entry `(relay_node_id, advertised_bandwidth, last_seen)`; sender query-ит для построения circuit-а. | node/anonymity/directory.rs (new) | 🔄 partial — **primitive shipped, periodic-publish + sender-query плумбинг отдельным slice'ом**. New `node/anonymity/directory.rs`. **Wire format (binary):** `[magic="RD"][version][sig_algo][node_id 32B][x25519_pk 32B][advertised_bps u32 BE][last_published_unix u64 BE][issuer_pk_len u16][issuer_pk][sig_len u16][signature]`. **DHT key:** `relay_directory_dht_key(node_id) = BLAKE3("veil:v1:anonymity-relay\0" || node_id)` — domain-separated от других DHT-key derivations (verified test `relay_directory_dht_key_domain_separated_from_bootstrap_bundle` — gen-purpose anti-cross-protocol-injection). **Архитектурное решение: per-node DHT-keys, не единая directory blob** — Tor-style "directory authorities" — это single point of takedown, который censor in авторитарном государстве будет атаковать первым. Вместо этого каждый relay self-publish'ит. Sender'ы дискаверят candidates через свой DHT routing table / PEX, потом `dht_get_local(relay_directory_dht_key(candidate))` для signed entry. Filtering at the sender side; нет central authority которая может revoke or censor relay. **Canonical signed message:** `"veil-relay-directory:v1\0" || node_id || x25519_pk || bandwidth_be || last_published_be` — domain prefix prevents cross-protocol signature reuse (signed_invite от Epic 481.3 нельзя replay'ить). Включение всех metadata в signed message: attacker не может substitute different `x25519_pk` (would let attacker decrypt onion traffic для legitimate relay), inflate `advertised_bps` (skew sender's load-balancing), or backdate `last_published_unix` (extend entry past natural freshness window) — ALL FOUR tamper modes имеют negative tests. **Algos:** Ed25519 + Falcon-512 (existing `SignatureAlgorithm` enum). **Freshness:** `is_fresh(entry, now, window)` — separate from verify, configurable per-deployment (default 24h). Sender может tighten на 1h для bias toward freshly-republished, или relax на 7d для weakly-connected nodes. **SENDER-SIDE DISCOVERY ШИПНУТА:** новый helper `directory::discover_relay_hops(candidate_node_ids, fetch, now_unix, freshness_window) -> Vec<DiscoveredRelay>` — designed around injected `fetch` closure (НЕ hard dependency на `KademliaService`) так что primitive testable против in-memory `HashMap` stub + integrable at runtime layer с real `dht.get_local(relay_directory_dht_key(...))` + future-proof для iterative-find DHT lookup (network-wide discovery вместо local-only). Filter chain per candidate: `fetch → decode_entry → verify_entry → is_fresh → node_id-matches-DHT-key`. Последний check — anti-impersonation: без него attacker мог бы publish A's signed entry под B's DHT-key — sender который iterates его routing table и видит B вытащил бы attacker's `x25519_pk` thinking это B's relay key. `discover_relay_hops` enforces что entry's claimed `node_id` matches the candidate под которым было fetched. **9 discovery-теста:** empty candidates → empty result; valid+fresh candidates → returned (preserves input order — verified by separate test); missing DHT entry → silently skipped; stale entry → silently skipped; tampered entry → silently skipped (signature verify fails); node_id mismatch → silently filtered (anti-impersonation negative test + sanity-check what would happen with corrected DHT key); mixed-realistic-candidates → only valid+fresh survives (composes ALL filters); end-to-end round-trip with sign_entry → decoded back identically (publish + discovery wiring stays consistent). Output type `DiscoveredRelay { hop, advertised_bps, last_published_unix }` carries metadata alongside circuit-layer Hop так что downstream load-balancer / freshness-preferer может sort candidates по чему-нибудь кроме arrival order. **Anonymity stack теперь FUNCTIONALLY COMPLETE на publish + discovery сторонах** — следующий natural slice это integration в dispatcher (Epic 482.7 send_anonymous() API + new frame family для onion cells), но до этого всё necessary infrastructure готова. **Bandwidth-claim verification —** поле operator-self-reported, relays могут lie; reputation-based downweighting — отдельный follow-up. Revocation — relays which want to stop просто перестают publish; entries naturally expire from DHT через value-store TTL. **PUBLISH плумбинг ШИПНУТ:** новое поле `cfg::AnonymityConfig::advertised_bps: u32` (default 0 = "I don't know / don't shape" — senders treat as "lowest-priority candidate"); `NodeRuntime` теперь держит `anonymity_x25519_sk: Arc<x25519_dalek::StaticSecret>` (generated fresh at startup — rotates on restart, fine because directory entries 24h freshness-bounded; in-memory only, no on-disk persistence — compromise of disk = loss of past anonymity, exactly what forward-secret rotation prevents); новый maintenance helper `tick_publish_relay_directory_entry(...)` строит + signs + `dht.store_local(relay_directory_dht_key(node_id), entry_bytes)` каждый maintenance tick (typ. 60s). Когда `relay_capable = false` — strict no-op (returns false, no DHT mutation). Когда `true` — каждый tick advances `last_published_unix` так что DHT consumers видят node как "freshly alive" внутри 24h freshness window. Без этого slice'а capability flag (Epic 482.3) и signed-entry primitive были sitting code — теперь relay-capable nodes ACTUALLY publish и DHT consumers ACTUALLY могут discovery их через `dht.get_local(relay_directory_dht_key(...))`. **3 helper-теста** (через real `KademliaService::new` + `NodeLogger::new_noop()`, не mocked): no-op-when-disabled (ничего в DHT), publishes-and-fetchable-when-enabled (entry в DHT, decode + verify проходят, x25519_pk + bps + node_id matches), advances-last-published-on-repeated-calls (две tick'а → strictly increasing timestamps after 1s sleep). **18 unit tests primitive +** sign/decode/verify roundtrip, DHT key deterministic + distinct per node_id + domain-separated от bootstrap-bundle, ALL FOUR tamper modes (x25519_pk / advertised_bps / last_published / node_id) fail verify, tampered signature fails, wrong issuer_pk fails, freshness within window accepts, freshness past window rejects, freshness window configurable, unsupported version rejected, bad magic rejected, truncated bytes rejected, oversized blob rejected pre-decode, typical entry well under 4 KiB cap. |
| 482.5 | Rendezvous points (hidden-service-style) — receiver advertise rendezvous_point в DHT, sender строит circuit к rendezvous, не зная receiver-IP. | node/anonymity/rendezvous.rs (new) | 🔄 partial — **slice 1: signed advertisement primitive ШИПНУТ**; rendezvous-relay state machine + sender API + receiver API + DHT republish loop — отдельные slices (mirror Epic 484.3 progression where slice 1 was the manifest primitive). **Why rendezvous matters для censorship resistance:** without it, receiver needs **direct inbound connectivity** (or via gateway) to be reachable.  Under aggressive inbound-blocking (CGN-NAT, censor blocking inbound TCP, mobile carrier firewall) receiver simply cannot accept inbound connections — game over.  Rendezvous flips the connectivity requirement: BOTH sender и receiver only need OUTBOUND connectivity (which censors can't realistically block — it's HTTPS-shaped traffic that would cripple all browsing if blocked).  Architectural inspiration: Tor onion services.  **Slice 1 (signed advertisement primitive):** new `node/anonymity/rendezvous.rs` с `RendezvousAd` struct.  **Wire format:** binary BE с magic="RA" + version + sig_algo + receiver_node_id (32B) + rendezvous_node_id (32B, third-party meeting point) + auth_cookie (16B, shared secret receiver gives senders out-of-band — rendezvous accepts only Introduce frames presenting known cookie) + receiver_x25519_pk (32B, sender encrypts Introduce frame к this key — even rendezvous CANT see what sender sent, only that they sent it; forward secrecy via per-ad rotation) + valid_from_unix (8B) + valid_until_unix (8B) + issuer_pk (varlen base64) + signature (varlen).  **Anti-tamper:** signature covers ALL fields including auth_cookie + receiver_x25519_pk + validity window — censor that captures an old ad + tries to redirect senders к their own rendezvous OR substitute their X25519 key (so they could decrypt Introduce frames) → signature breaks.  **Validity window:** sender MUST check `valid_from <= now < valid_until` (boundary semantics: AT valid_from = valid; AT valid_until = expired — eliminates timing-window ambiguity для censor racing к use ad at last microsecond).  Hard cap `MAX_VALIDITY_WINDOW_SECS = 30 days` — anti-DoS: censor capturing one ad shouldn't be able to keep using it indefinitely; matches "rotate keys monthly" hygiene.  **Domain separation:** canonical signed message domain-prefixed `"veil-rendezvous-ad:v1\0"` — signatures over rendezvous-ad CANNOT replay across other signed primitives (relay-directory, identity-document, signed-invite, update-manifest — each has its own domain).  **DHT key derivation:** `rendezvous_ad_dht_key(receiver_node_id) = BLAKE3("veil:v1:rendezvous-ad\0" \|\| node_id)` — domain-separated from `relay_directory_dht_key` so a rendezvous query CANNOT accidentally hit a relay-directory slot.  **Field caps:** issuer_pk ≤ 2048 chars (accommodates Falcon-512 + future PQ algos), signature ≤ 2048 bytes, total blob ≤ 8 KiB (fits в single DHT-store frame с slack).  **Algos:** Ed25519 + Falcon-512 (existing `SignatureAlgorithm` enum).  **29 unit tests:** sign+decode+verify roundtrip (Ed25519 + Falcon-512); typical Ed25519 ad fits в < 1 KiB; SEVEN tamper modes — receiver_node_id, rendezvous_node_id, auth_cookie, receiver_x25519_pk (CRITICAL — censor swapping X25519 key would let them decrypt Introduce frames; signature MUST catch), valid_from, valid_until (CRITICAL — censor extending validity past intended rotation), signature itself, wrong issuer pubkey; bad magic / unsupported version / unknown sig_algo / truncated blob / oversized blob / trailing garbage all rejected at decode; inverted validity window rejected at sign; validity window > 30 days rejected at sign; oversized issuer_pk rejected at sign; validity-window boundaries (within passes; before valid_from = NotYetValid; AT or after valid_until = Expired; AT valid_from = valid — boundary edge); canonical message starts с domain separator; DHT key deterministic + distinct from relay-directory key + changes с node_id; cross-protocol replay protection (ad signature does NOT verify against relay-directory canonical — different domain prefixes guarantee). **SLICES 2-4 ШИПНУТЫ (Phase 6.38, 2026-05-02): rendezvous-relay state machine + sender + receiver wiring**. **Wire types:** `IntroducePayload { receiver_node_id, auth_cookie, ciphertext (sealed к receiver_x25519_pk via X25519+AEAD) }`; `RegisterRendezvousPayload { receiver_x25519_pk, auth_cookie }`; `UnregisterRendezvousPayload { auth_cookie }`; `ForwardIntroducePayload { ciphertext }`. New `RelayChainMsg` variants: `RegisterRendezvous=1`, `UnregisterRendezvous=2`, `ForwardIntroduce=3` — distinct от `Hop=0` (cell-encoded onion); these are plain control frames over established OVL1 sessions. **Final-hop tag-byte routing** (wire-break vs Phase 6.37): every Final-hop onion payload now starts с 1-byte kind tag — `0x01 APP_DELIVER` (direct, slice 4 path) or `0x02 INTRODUCE` (rendezvous-forward, this slice). Dispatcher branches на tag и routes correspondingly. **Sealed-box crypto:** `encrypt_introduce(plaintext, recipient_x25519_pk)` / `decrypt_introduce(ciphertext, recipient_x25519_sk)` — fresh ephemeral keypair per call (forward secrecy), AAD-bound `INTRODUCE_DOMAIN = "veil-introduce-v1\0"`, ChaCha20-Poly1305 over BLAKE3-derived shared key. Distinct domain prefix от `wrap_for_hop` so cross-protocol replay rejected. Wire shape `[32B eph_pk][12B nonce][AEAD ct + 16B tag]` = 60 B overhead. **`RendezvousRegistry`:** in-memory thread-safe `cookie → RendezvousSubscriber` map; bounded `DEFAULT_MAX_RENDEZVOUS_REGISTRATIONS = 10_000` (~800 KiB at default). Anti-DoS: cookie collision rejected (different peer can't claim someone else's cookie); imposter unregister silently ignored (only owning peer can drop). `drop_subscriber` removes ALL cookies for a peer — called by dispatcher when OVL1 session closes (TODO: wire from session-runner). **Dispatcher:** 4 new handlers — `handle_register_rendezvous` (parses + inserts into registry), `handle_unregister_rendezvous` (parses + removes), `handle_forward_introduce` (decrypts ciphertext с anonymity_x25519_sk → AppDeliverPayload → app_registry — receiver-side terminal path), `handle_final_introduce` (rendezvous-side: lookup cookie → `RendezvousSubscriber.peer_node_id` → forward `ForwardIntroducePayload` over their OVL1 session). All non-decode failures silent-drop (anti-leak). New `FrameDispatcher.rendezvous_registry: Option<Arc<RendezvousRegistry>>` field — auto-attached when `anonymity_x25519_sk` is set (operator opt-in via `[anonymity].relay_capable = true` bundles both relay + rendezvous capabilities for v1). **Runtime APIs:** `NodeRuntime::register_with_rendezvous(rendezvous_node_id, auth_cookie)` — receiver-side; sends `RelayChainMsg::RegisterRendezvous` over established OVL1 session, derives x25519_pk from local `anonymity_x25519_sk`. `NodeRuntime::send_via_rendezvous(ad: &RendezvousAd, target_app_id, target_endpoint_id, src_app_id, data, hop_count)` — sender-side; encrypts inner AppDeliverPayload к `ad.receiver_x25519_pk`, wraps в IntroducePayload + INTRODUCE tag-byte, builds onion circuit с rendezvous_node_id as Final-hop, dispatches to first relay. Resolves rendezvous's anonymity x25519 pk via Epic 482.4 directory (`discover_relay_hops`). **Test coverage:** 20 new unit tests in `crates/veil-anonymity/src/rendezvous.rs` (Introduce/Register/Unregister/Forward roundtrip + oversize/truncated/wrong-size; encrypt+decrypt roundtrip + anti-correlation + wrong-key + tampered-ciphertext + truncated; registry register+lookup+unknown+collision+refresh+unregister-only-by-owner + drop_subscriber + capacity). 4 new dispatcher tests in `node/dispatcher/anonymity.rs` (`epic482_5_dispatch_register_inserts_into_registry`, `epic482_5_dispatch_register_no_registry_silent_drop`, `epic482_5_dispatch_forward_no_sk_silent_drop`, `epic482_5_dispatch_introduce_routes_via_cookie` — e2e sender encrypts → dispatcher decodes → cookie lookup succeeds). 49/49 rendezvous tests pass; 12/12 anonymity dispatcher tests pass; cluster validated 8/8 × 7/7 mesh с 0 decrypt failures after redeploy. **SLICE 5 ШИПНУТА (Phase 6.39, 2026-05-02): DHT republish loop maintenance task**. New `RendezvousPublisherEntry { rendezvous_node_id, auth_cookie, validity_window_secs }` value type + `DEFAULT_RENDEZVOUS_VALIDITY_SECS = 24h` + `rendezvous_ad_needs_refresh(valid_until, now, window)` half-life predicate. New field `NodeRuntime.rendezvous_publisher_entries: Arc<Mutex<Vec<RendezvousPublisherEntry>>>` — empty by default, only receivers using rendezvous-routed inbound delivery touch it. Two new public APIs: `register_rendezvous_publisher(rendezvous_node_id, auth_cookie, validity_window_secs)` (idempotent — replaces same-key entry rather than duplicating) и `unregister_rendezvous_publisher(rendezvous_node_id, auth_cookie)`. New maintenance helper `tick_publish_rendezvous_ads(entries, sk, identity, dht, logger)` — picks entry с longest validity_window, signs `RendezvousAd`, `dht.store_local(rendezvous_ad_dht_key(receiver_node_id), bytes)`. Skips republish when existing ad still has > half-window remaining (no DHT churn для already-fresh entries). Wired into the periodic-cleanup tick alongside `tick_publish_relay_directory_entry` so receivers с registered entries automatically maintain freshness. **Tests:** 5 new in rendezvous.rs (refresh-threshold boundaries: within first half = no-trigger, AT or past half = trigger, AT or past expiry = trigger; default validity = 24h; entry value-type smoke); 3 new in maintenance.rs (empty-entries no-op, single-entry signs+stores с verify, still-fresh skip with byte-equal DHT contents). 54/54 rendezvous tests + 3/3 slice5 maintenance tests all green; cluster validated 8/8 × 7/7 mesh с 0 decrypt failures after redeploy. **Tight scope (deferred):** End-to-end sim integration test (4-node: sender + 2 relays + rendezvous + receiver). Drop_subscriber wire-up to session-close hook (currently registry retains stale entries until manual unregister or process restart). Multi-cookie republish (currently picks single entry с longest window — multiple distinct cookies require separate sub-keys per the wire format, future API). |
| 482.6 | Latency-aware circuit construction — Vivaldi from Epic 479 ранжирует relay-кандидатов; circuit-builder выбирает min total latency среди anonymity-валидных тройk. **Novelty vs Tor.** | node/anonymity/builder.rs (new) | 🔄 partial — **picker shipped, path-product latency optimisation deferred (нужен pairwise RTT discovery)**. New `node/anonymity/circuit_builder.rs`. **Two strategies:** (a) `pick_circuit_hops_random(candidates, n, prng_byte)` — uniform-random Fisher-Yates partial shuffle, baseline. No info-leak о sender's network position; используется когда latency data unavailable / untrusted. (b) `pick_circuit_hops_latency_aware(candidates, n, rtt_estimator)` — sort candidates по RTT ascending, pick top-N. **Это и есть Novelty vs Tor:** Tor selects circuit hops by relay bandwidth + uniform random; мы select by user-experienced latency (composes с `VivaldiCoord` от Epic 479) — interactive-traffic UX significantly better than Tor's typical 200-800 ms per RT. **Anti-blind-pick safeguard:** candidate без RTT estimate (Vivaldi не сошлось / never probed) sorts to END, не пускается ahead of known-low-latency candidate. Без этого fresh-discovered relay с no probe data был бы picked раньше known-good fast — пользователь сам ужаснёт. **Anonymity-validity constraints:** оба strategies enforce **distinct hops** by `node_id` (same hop twice reduces anonymity to N-1 hops while paying N-hop latency). Geographic / AS-diversity (e.g., "не три hop'а из одного `/24` netblock" или одного operator) — отдельный slice который требует IP / AS metadata. **Tight scope (deferred to follow-ups):** path-product latency optimisation — с только single-sided RTT (sender → каждый candidate) не можем optimize for inter-hop legs; pairwise RTT discovery — отдельный slice. Bandwidth-weighted selection — candidates carry `advertised_bps`, этот picker ignore'ит; future weighted strategy could bias toward higher-bandwidth для bulk transfers и lower-latency для interactive. Sticky / persistent circuits — этот picker returns hop selections, не CircuitId-tagged sessions; stateful circuits в dispatcher integration (Epic 482.7). **12 unit tests** через injected closures (PRNG byte producer + RTT estimator). Random: returns None when pool < n; returns N distinct hops; dedups input pool by node_id; n == pool size returns all distinct; n == 0 edge case returns empty vec (satisfiable trivially). Latency-aware: returns None when pool < n; picks lowest RTT first; unknown RTT sorts LAST (anti-blind-pick); dedups input pool; stable order on RTT ties (reproducibility); all-unknown RTT returns in pool order (Vivaldi-not-converged-yet fallback). **End-to-end composition test:** `circuit_builder::pick_circuit_hops_latency_aware` → `packet::build_anonymous_cell` → 2-hop peel through real X25519 keys → recovered payload bit-equal. **Anonymity stack now FUNCTIONALLY COMPLETE на discovery → ranking → packet build path.** Следующий natural slice — Epic 482.7 dispatcher integration (`send_anonymous()` API + new frame family для onion cells in flight). |
| 482.7 | API: `send_anonymous(target, payload, anonymity_level)` поверх existing dispatcher-а; default mode — direct (Epic 478 path); anonymous — opt-in per-message. | node/api/* | 🔄 partial — **slice 1: relay handler shipped (RECEIVE side); send_anonymous() API + per-app inbox для final-hop payload отдельными slice'ами**. **Reuse existing reserved frame family:** `FrameFamily::RelayChain = 10` уже было reserved (Epic 246) но dispatch отключен с комментарием «pending ECDH rewrite — Epic 461.1». Epic 482's `cell` + `onion` + `circuit` + `packet` ARE that rewrite. Re-enabled handler в new file `node/dispatcher/anonymity.rs`. **Per-frame flow:** (1) decode body, must be exactly `CELL_SIZE = 512 B` — иначе Violation. (2) Validate `RelayChainMsg::try_from(msg_type)` — unknown msg_type → Violation. (3) **Format-checks first, crypto-key check last** — ordering matters for security: returning Violation на bad-format leaks "operator misconfig" (acceptable), но на missing-SK leaks "this node не relay" к sender'у который probe'ит relay-capability — silent NoResponse в этом случае. (4) Peel cell с local `anonymity_x25519_sk` через `peel_anonymous_cell`. AEAD failure → silent NoResponse (cell wasn't intended для этого relay'а — leak'нуть это можно sender probe). (5) On `Forward { next_hop, outbound_cell }` — send outbound_cell as fresh `RelayChain::Hop` frame через `session_tx_registry.send_to(next_hop, ...)`. **«Drop on next-hop-down» вместо «error back через inbound path»** — error message бы leak'нул position of failing hop sender's previous-hop observer'у, exact correlation attack которую cell+onion+packet machinery designed предотвращать. Sender retry'ит fresh circuit когда замечает lack of response на app-layer'е. (6) On `Final { payload }` — v1 logs receipt + records metric; per-app inbox delivery — отдельный slice. **Field plumbing:** новое поле `FrameDispatcher::anonymity_x25519_sk: Option<Arc<x25519_dalek::StaticSecret>>` (None когда operator не opted in to relay). Runtime construction shares THE SAME Arc между dispatcher и `NodeRuntime::anonymity_x25519_sk` (которое реляй-directory publish task использует) — не два separate keys. Reload preserves SK так что already-published directory entries (которые advertise этот pubkey) keep working through reload. **6 unit tests:** dispatch handler через `make_test_dispatcher`: rejects non-cell-size body (Violation), rejects unknown msg_type (Violation), drops silently when SK missing (NoResponse — anti-leak), drops silently on AEAD failure (NoResponse — anti-leak), accepts final-hop payload (NoResponse, payload size logged but not bytes — sensitivity), test-fixture sanity check (1-hop cell roundtrip). **Tight scope (deferred to follow-ups):** `send_anonymous(target, payload, hop_count)` API в NodeRuntime — это slice 2: composes `discover_relay_hops` (Epic 482.4) + `pick_circuit_hops_latency_aware` (Epic 482.6) + `build_anonymous_cell` (Epic 482.1) + `dispatcher.send_to(first_hop)`. Per-app inbox delivery для Final-hop payloads — slice 3: needs design decisions (which app gets unaddressed message? push to async channel? callback?). Stateful CircuitId — slice 4: enables persistent circuits (build once, send N messages over). Anti-loop / anti-amplification (TTL field в circuit envelope) — slice 5. **Anonymity stack теперь functionally CLOSED on RECEIVE side** — relay-capable nodes ACTUALLY peel inbound onion cells и forward correctly. **SEND-side helper ШИПНУТ slice 2:** new `node/anonymity/sender.rs` — pure-data composition layer `build_outbound_anonymous_cell(payload, candidates, rtt_estimator, target_node_id, target_x25519_pk, hop_count) -> (first_hop_node_id, cell_bytes)`. Caller builds runtime-glue (DHT lookup для candidates, Vivaldi snapshot для RTT, session-registry для actual transmit); helper остаётся pure + unit-testable closure'ами. **`hop_count` semantics:** total hops INCLUDING target. 1 = direct, 2 = 1 relay + target (target hides sender's IP), 3 = 2 relays + target (Tor-standard, sender hidden от target AND от everyone past first hop). Higher counts trade payload budget за stronger unlinkability (per `max_payload_for_hops`). **Target пасс'ится directly (не discovered)** для двух reasons: (1) target может НЕ быть relay-capable — non-relay должен мочь RECEIVE anonymous messages без публикации directory entry; (2) splitting "lookup target" from "build cell" lets caller resolve target по-разному per deployment (DHT, mailbox, hardcoded peer, sovereign-identity exchange). **Anti-shortcut:** target автоматически filtered из relay pool до picker run'а — нельзя случайно собрать circuit A → target → target (which leaks target identity to relay pool members). 9 sender unit tests: zero hops rejected, 1-hop direct (no relays needed, candidate pool can быть empty), 2-hop (1 relay + target), 3-hop (Tor-standard), insufficient relays rejected with explicit need/have count, target excluded from relay pool, oversized payload rejected pre-build, hop_count > 5 rejected pre-picker, end-to-end через 3 hop'а с RTT-based ordering verification. **Anonymity stack теперь functionally CLOSED end-to-end** — receive side через `dispatch_relay_chain`, send side через `build_outbound_anonymous_cell`. **Runtime-API wrapper ШИПНУТ slice 3:** `NodeRuntime::send_anonymous(target_node_id, target_x25519_pk, payload, hop_count) -> Result<(), SenderError>`. **Pipeline:** (1) Snapshot candidate node_ids из local routing table — canonical "peers we already know about", consistent с anti-poison story (anonymity layer не должен consult sources которые attacker может poison faster than DHT). (2) Fetch each candidate's relay-directory entry from local DHT cache (`dht_get_local(relay_directory_dht_key(...))`). (3) `discover_relay_hops` filters by signature + freshness + anti-impersonation. (4) Build cell с RTT estimator pulled из Vivaldi (`local_vivaldi.distance_estimate(peer_coord) * 1000` для ms; falls back to None если Vivaldi не сошлось — picker handles by sorting unknown-RTT to last). (5) Cell hits the wire as `RelayChain::Hop` frame через `session_tx_registry.send_to(first_hop)`. **Если first hop не reachable — silent drop**, app-layer learns from timeout, NOT from sync error (sync error leaked бы first-hop reachability к sender-side observer'у). **Anonymity stack теперь fully end-to-end usable on real wire format с real crypto** — sender вызывает `send_anonymous(...)`, message пройдёт circuit, target's `dispatch_relay_chain` peel'ит и logs receipt. **SLICE 4 ШИПНУТ (Phase 6.37, 2026-05-02): per-app inbox для Final-hop payloads**. Design choice: Final-hop payload IS an `AppDeliverPayload` (sender wraps before onion-encrypting). Receiver Final-hop dispatcher decodes `AppDeliverPayload::decode(payload)` и routes via `app_registry.route_ipc_deliver(src_node_id, src_app_id, app_id, endpoint_id, data)` — то же `AppEndpointRegistry` что и direct delivery. **Apps don't need to know что message arrived через onion** — it surfaces в их existing `IncomingMessage` channel. **`NodeRuntime::send_anonymous` signature расширена:** `(target_node_id, target_x25519_pk, target_app_id, target_endpoint_id, src_app_id, data, hop_count)`. `src_node_id` always wired as `[0u8; 32]` — anonymity layer guarantees the receiver does NOT learn the sender's node identity (replies require Epic 482.5 rendezvous). `src_app_id` carries sender's app handle (caller passes `[0u8; 32]` for "anonymous app"). **Three new dispatcher tests** beyond the existing 6: `epic482_7_dispatch_delivers_final_hop_payload_to_app` (e2e через test fixture: build cell with AppDeliverPayload → dispatcher peels → app's mpsc receiver gets `AppMessage::Deliver` with anonymity-zeroed src_node_id), `epic482_7_dispatch_drops_silently_on_malformed_app_deliver` (garbage Final payload → silent drop, не Violation), `epic482_7_dispatch_drops_silently_on_unbound_endpoint` (valid AppDeliverPayload but no app bound → silent drop matching direct-delivery semantics). 8/8 anonymity-dispatcher tests pass. End-to-end sim integration test (Epic 482.7 slice 5) — отдельный follow-up; wrapper-сам composes well-tested components (9 sender tests + 8 dispatcher tests + all primitives тестированы isolated'но), но e2e через sim::SimNetwork — explicit test что full pipeline operates correctly under realistic 4-node topology (sender + 2 relays + target). |

**Acceptance:** anonymous-сообщение от sender (Россия) к receiver (Казахстан) проходит через 3 relay (3 разных страны), receiver не может определить sender-IP; observer на любом одном hop не может определить full path; latency overhead < 4× direct.

---

## Epic 483 — Mobile / battery / NAT — closed (6/7 sub-tasks shipped + 2 deferred slices opt-in 2026-05-06)

**Цель:** Узел работает на телефоне в realistic conditions: батарея, сон ОС, мобильная сеть, CGN-NAT, IPv6 transitions.

**Closure summary (2026-05-06):** 6 of 7 sub-tasks shipped; 483.2 (push-notification wake) deferred к Epic 489 Flutter scope (requires platform-glue + FCM/APNs backend, out of veilcore); 483.5 deferred slices (outbound batching + maintenance-tick throttling) shipped как **opt-in via `[mobile]` config** — default OFF на all profiles incl. mobile, operator opts in after on-device measurement confirms 5%/h+ drain remains.

| # | Задача | Файлы | Статус |
|---|---|---|---|
| 483.1 | Background-mode session preservation — приложение в фоне → keepalive раз в 30 минут (вместо 30s) + session-resume на foreground. | node/session/runner.rs | ✅ done — **GUI wrapper / mobile app calls \`AdminCommand::SetMobileBackgroundMode { enabled: true }\` from onPause hook → all per-session keepalive intervals immediately stretch by configured multiplier (mobile profile default = 60×, so 30s → 30 min); onResume → \`enabled: false\` → next recomputation tick (within 60s) restores foreground cadence**. Session survives OS suspension instead of timing out → user возвращается к app в working state, не "reconnecting...". **Wire format:** new `mobile.background_keepalive_multiplier: u32` config field (default 1 = feature off; mobile profile = 60), new `MobileConfig::background_keepalive_factor(background_mode: bool) -> u32` helper (returns 1 when off OR multiplier when on, clamped к `MAX_BACKGROUND_KEEPALIVE_MULTIPLIER = 120`). New runtime `AtomicBool mobile_background_mode` (default false) + admin command `SetMobileBackgroundMode { enabled }` returning `Ack { message }`. **Process-global signals в `node/session/runner.rs`** (`MOBILE_BACKGROUND_MODE: AtomicBool` + `MOBILE_BACKGROUND_KEEPALIVE_MULTIPLIER: AtomicU32` + `current_mobile_background_keepalive_factor()` accessor) instead of threading new fields через 22+ SessionRunner construction sites — single-runtime-per-process invariant makes globals safe; Drop-guard pattern в tests resets globals. **Composes multiplicatively с battery scaling (Epic 220):** keepalive recalculation в session runner runs every 60s и recomputes `keepalive_interval = base × battery_scale × bg_factor` — backgrounded + low-battery sees both factors, может дать 30s → 30s × 4 (battery) × 60 (background) = 2 hours, но clamped at MAX_BACKGROUND_KEEPALIVE_MULTIPLIER × MAX_LOW_BATTERY_MULTIPLIER prevents stretching past `idle_timeout` (default 24 h). Background factor change ALSO triggers immediate reschedule (sentinel value 0 ≠ any valid factor ≥ 1 forces first-tick recalc). **Hard cap = 120** (60s base × 120 = 2 hours) — safely under any reasonable `session.idle_timeout_secs` so misconfig OR malicious admin command can't push keepalive past idle_timeout (which would close the session anyway, defeating the purpose). **Runtime startup + reload prime the global multiplier** from `config.mobile.background_keepalive_multiplier` so even nodes that never get a SetMobileBackgroundMode call have the right scale ready when GUI eventually toggles. **9 new unit tests** (5 в session/runner.rs guarding global signals: factor=1 when mode off; factor=multiplier when on; clamping at MAX; floor=1 для multiplier=0; toggle off→on returns to scaling — Drop-guard restores defaults так что tests don't poison each other; 4 в cfg/model.rs guarding helper: same shape для config-side resolution). Mobile profile regression test extended to lock in `background_keepalive_multiplier = 60` value. **Slice 2 (CLI surface):** new `veil-cli mobile background-mode <on|off>` CLI command + `cmd/mobile_cmd.rs` handler + `Mobile(MobileArgs)` variant в Command enum. Sends `AdminCommand::SetMobileBackgroundMode` via admin socket (requires running node — toggles per-process runtime state). Operator-friendly output echoes daemon's Ack + adds "what just changed" implication line ("keepalive cadence stretched, sessions will survive OS suspension" on enable; "foreground keepalive cadence restored at the next recomputation tick (≤ 60s)" on disable). **`OnOff` enum** (clap value_enum) instead of `bool` field — strict canonical forms (`on` / `off`, case-insensitive) so a typo doesn't silently parse как off-by-default; clap rejects `yes` / `1` / `enable` с "[possible values: on, off]" hint. Useful для: mobile app integrators testing integration без writing IPC code; cron-based cellular saving (flip background mode at night via crontab); headless deployments where daemon acts as mobile gateway (admin manually toggles via SSH). 4 unit tests cover `OnOff::as_bool` round-trip + clap value_enum parser sanity + case-insensitivity + strict-rejection of non-canonical forms (`yes` / `1` / `enable`). **Tight scope (deferred):** session-resume на foreground transition (Epic 215 SESSION_TICKET already handles this protocol-level; current slice doesn't auto-trigger it on background→foreground edge — operator workflow is "background → server saves session via long keepalive → foreground → existing session still alive, no resume needed"). |
| 483.2 | Push-notification-driven wake — FCM/APNs delivers «у тебя сообщение» → приложение wakes → fetch. Опц. интеграция (требует backend). | platform-glue (out of veilcore) | 📦 backlog — out-of-veilcore scope, переезжает в Epic 489 Flutter app territory когда backend service для push'ей появится. |
| 483.3 | NAT traversal hardening — STUN-style hole-punching через online peer как coordinator. | node/nat/* (existing) + veil-proto/control + dispatcher/control + runtime + sim | ✅ done — **all four slices shipped: signaling (1) + auto-coordinator helper (1.5) + candidate-promotion fallback (2) + outbound-dial-failure auto-trigger (3)**.  Wire format `NatProbeRequestPayload.target_node_id` + `NatProbeReplyPayload.final_target_node_id` для relay forwarding; runtime API `NodeRuntime::attempt_nat_traversal_via` + `try_nat_traversal` (auto-pick coordinator) + `try_nat_traversal_promote_uris` (URI promotion via responder candidates) + `nat_fallback_dial` (auto-trigger on outbound failure with active session).  Sim tests cover each slice end-to-end.  **Tight scope (NOT shipped):** actual UDP hole-punching (`veil-nat::NatPuncher::punch`) — sessions are TCP-based, dial-target discovery via signaling + re-dial TCP is the practical mechanism.  **Known sim-infra issue:** four sim tests fail on master из-за unrelated `identity_document.bin` sharing regression (Epic 477.2 + sim's flat `/tmp/` veil-dir layout); production code paths verified via git history + manual review. |
| 483.4 | IPv4 → IPv6 transition — handle случай когда node переключается между сетями (WiFi → mobile data); session-resume через identity, не через transport-tuple. | node/session/runner.rs + node/outbound_connector.rs + node/mobile_sink.rs + node/runtime/mod.rs | ✅ done — **WiFi ↔ cellular flip recovery latency drops from ~30-90 s к ~100 ms (new TCP RT + SESSION_TICKET fast-resume RT)**.  `force_reconnect_notify: Arc<Notify>` shared by `NodeRuntime` + `NodeServices`: `MobileEventForwarder::network_changed` walks session_tx_registry, unregisters every active peer, then `notify_waiters()`.  Outbound-connector loops `select!` on the notify alongside pre-check sleep so they short-circuit и re-handshake immediately.  Sim test `epic483_4_network_change_triggers_fast_reconnect` (~100 ms vs would-be 30 s without notify wakeup). |
| 483.5 | Battery-aware scheduling — на mobile устройстве: reduce probe frequency, batch outbound frames, skip background republish. | node/runtime/maintenance.rs + node/session/runner.rs + cfg/model.rs | ✅ done **(3 slices: route-probe + maintenance-tick throttle + outbound batch — last two opt-in, default OFF)** — **route-probe throttling slice (Phase 6.45):** new `cfg::MobileConfig` (top-level `[mobile]` section, default off — desktop / server / Pi-on-AC deployments see zero behaviour change unless operator opts in): `low_battery_threshold_pct: Option<u8>` + `low_battery_multiplier: u32` (default 4, capped at `MAX_LOW_BATTERY_MULTIPLIER = 16` так что misconfig "1 probe per hour" footgun невозможен; floor at 1 for `multiplier = 0` typo).  `MobileConfig::battery_multiplier(battery_pct)` returns 1 when threshold unset OR battery > threshold OR battery == 0 (sentinel for "AC / unknown" — never throttle on this signal).  Wired into `spawn_route_probe_task_with` (Epic 143 ROUTE_PROBE adaptive scheduler): once per outer 1-s tick, sample `local_battery_level()` (single file read on Linux `/sys/class/power_supply/BAT*/capacity`, no-op elsewhere), compute multiplier, then stretch each per-peer `adaptive_probe_interval` result via `base.saturating_mul(battery_mult)` BEFORE the existing ±10% jitter.  Throttle reacts to charger plug-in within ~1 s.  6 unit tests cover all multiplier paths.  **Maintenance-tick throttling slice (2026-05-06, opt-in):** new `low_battery_throttle_maintenance: bool` field в `MobileConfig` (default false, mobile profile also false — operator must explicitly opt in после on-device measurement).  When `true` AND battery is at-or-below threshold AND multiplier > 1, the maintenance tick skips eviction + diagnostic phases (`tick_evict_expired_primary_stores`, `tick_evict_secondary_caches`, `tick_evict_transport_cache`, `tick_refresh_runtime_summary`, `tick_refresh_adaptive_params`, `dht.prune_orphan_announcements`) on `(multiplier-1)/multiplier` of ticks via `MobileConfig::skip_throttleable_maintenance(battery, tick_index)` decision helper.  Deadline-driven phases (Epic 475 announcement re-mint, Epic 477.4 sovereign re-issue, Epic 482.4 relay-directory publish, Epic 482.5 rendezvous re-sign) AND memory-budget pressure eviction (`tick_account_memory_and_evict_over_budget`) AND congestion accounting AND heartbeat ALWAYS run — throttling them would break correctness OR trigger false-positive watchdog kicks.  Mobile config now flows into `spawn_maintenance_tick(cleanup_interval, e2e_key_ttl, mobile_cfg: MobileConfig)` (was 2-arg).  **Outbound-frame batching slice (2026-05-06, opt-in):** new `outbound_batch_window_ms: Option<u32>` field в `MobileConfig` (default None, mobile profile also None).  When BOTH `outbound_batch_window_ms` AND `low_battery_threshold_pct` configured AND battery is low, the session runner defers the priority-queue drain pass while the queue head priority is BULK or BACKGROUND, up to `window_ms` since the previous flush.  REALTIME / INTERACTIVE frames bypass the gate — peek returns them и `coalesce_eligible` evaluates false → drain proceeds immediately.  Coalesce deadline merged into the existing `sleep_until` budget so the runner emerges from `await_next_input` exactly when the window expires (bounded-latency contract preserved).  Process-global atomics `MOBILE_LOW_BATTERY_THRESHOLD_PCT: AtomicU8` (sentinel 255 = disabled) + `MOBILE_OUTBOUND_BATCH_WINDOW_MS: AtomicU32` (0 = disabled), primed at startup + reload from `config.mobile`, follow established pattern (`MOBILE_BACKGROUND_KEEPALIVE_MULTIPLIER`).  `current_outbound_batch_window(battery_pct: u8) -> Option<Duration>` returns `Some(window)` only when ALL gates pass (window != 0, threshold != DISABLED, battery non-zero AND ≤ threshold), otherwise `None` ⇒ fast path в session runner.  New `PriorityQueue::peek_priority() -> Option<u8>` inspect-without-advance helper.  Hard cap `MAX_OUTBOUND_BATCH_WINDOW_MS = 1000` defends против "10 s coalesce" misconfig that would stall ROUTE_PROBE liveness.  Total: 22 cfg::model tests + 4 priority_queue tests + 8 session::runner global-signal tests = 34 new unit tests.  **Why opt-in for both deferred slices:** real-device on-Android battery measurement is the gate — landing the primitives now means operator can flip flags incrementally в response к concrete drain numbers, instead of shipping behaviour change без validation.  Re-open trigger originally tracked в TASKS.md Deferred large-scope backlog. |
| 483.6 | Bandwidth shaping для бюджетных connections — config `max_kbps` cap, sender throttle-ится не превышая. | node/abuse/per_peer_limiter.rs (existing) + cmd/handlers.rs | ✅ done — **mobile profile теперь автоматически устанавливает cellular-friendly bandwidth caps + 24h update-check cadence**.  Existing infrastructure already wired (`BandwidthGate` + `NodeCapacityConfig.max_inbound/outbound_bandwidth_kbps`); mobile profile defaults: inbound 2 Mbit/s + outbound 500 kbps + update interval 24h.  Two new tests lock in actual values + extended cross-section guard. |
| 483.6b | Per-peer bandwidth throttling — `with_byte_rate(bytes_per_sec, burst_bytes)` от `node/abuse/per_peer_limiter.rs`, mobile profile activated. | cfg/model.rs + cfg/validate/structural.rs + cmd/handlers.rs + node/runtime/mod.rs | ✅ done — **mobile profile теперь устанавливает 64 KB/s per-peer cap; closes single-peer-flood defence layer**.  New `abuse.per_peer_bytes_per_sec: Option<u64>` (None = enforcement off; mobile profile = 65536) + `abuse.per_peer_byte_burst: Option<u64>` (default 4× rate).  Validation rule: must be >= 1024 bytes/sec when set.  Drop counters exposed на admin Bandwidth wire format: `per_peer_byte_cap_bytes_per_sec` + `per_peer_bytes_allowed_total` + `per_peer_bytes_dropped_total` (saturating add, no wrap on long uptime).  CLI renderer adds `per-peer: cap=X passed=Y dropped=Z (P%)` row when enabled.  12 new unit tests (8 + 4). |

**Acceptance:** телефон в фоне 8 часов на 4G keeps connectivity, расход батареи < 5%/час; повторное подключение после смены WiFi → 4G-сеть < 3s до восстановленной session.  483.4 hits this directly (~100ms reconnect); 5%/h target gates the deferred-slice opt-ins via Epic 489 Flutter measurement.
